    • 鬼不欲入人屋，可于外边，张幕设席，陈酒食于上 - Ambiguous phrase meaning 'ghosts do not want to enter human houses, so they can be set up outside, with a tent and a table, and wine and food arranged on it' or 'ghosts do not want to enter human houses, so they can be set up outside, with a tent and a table, and wine and food placed on it.'
    • 文本如其言，至时，仁茜见景两客来坐，从百余骑 - Ambiguous phrase meaning 'Wenben followed his words, and at the time, Ren Xian saw two guests arrive and sit down, accompanied by over a hundred riders' or 'Wenben followed his words, and at the time, Ren Xian saw two guests arrive and sit down, with over a hundred riders.'
    • 既坐文本向席再拜，谢以食之不精，亦传意，辞谢 - Ambiguous phrase meaning 'after sitting, Wenben bowed twice toward the table, apologized for the food not being refined, also conveyed the meaning, and expressed gratitude' or 'after sitting, Wenben bowed twice toward the table, apologized for the food not being refined, also conveyed the meaning, and expressed thanks.'
    • 初文本将设食，仁茜请有金帛以赎之 - Ambiguous phrase meaning 'initially, Wenben was going to prepare the food, and Ren Xian asked for gold and silk to redeem it' or 'initially, Wenben was going to prepare the food, and Ren Xian asked for gold and silk to exchange it.'
    • 文本问是何等物，鬼所用物，皆与人异，唯黄金及绢，为得通用，然亦不如假者，以黄色涂大锡作金，以纸为绢帛，最为贵上 - Ambiguous phrase meaning 'Wenben asked what kind of things they were, and Ren Xian explained that things used by ghosts differ from those used by humans, only gold and silk can be used universally, but they are not as good as fake ones, by coating large tin with yellow paint to make gold, and using paper to make silk, which are the most valuable' or 'Wenben asked what kind of things they were, and Ren Xian explained that things used by ghosts differ from those used by humans, only gold and silk can be used universally, but they are not as good as fake ones, by coating large tin with yellow paint to make gold, and using paper to make silk, which are the most valuable.'
    • 文本如言作之，及景食毕 - Ambiguous phrase meaning 'Wenben followed his instructions and made them, and when the guests finished eating' or 'Wenben followed his instructions and made them, and when the guests had finished eating.'

    Cultural and Historical Allusions

    • 鬼 - Refers to spirits or ghosts in Chinese folklore.
    • 人食 - Refers to human food, which is often considered sacred and off-limits to ghosts.
    • 金帛 - Refers to gold and silk, which are valuable items in Chinese culture.
    • 大锡 - Refers to large pieces of tin, a common material in ancient China.
    • 纸 - Refers to paper, which was invented in China and used for various purposes.
    • 鬼所用物 - Refers to items used by ghosts, which often have different properties and values compared to those used by humans.

    Proper Nouns

    • 仁茜 - The name of a ghost or spirit.
    • 文本 - The name of a person.
    • 金帛 - Refers to gold and silk.
    • 大锡 - Refers to large pieces of tin.
    • 纸 - Refers to paper.

    Yet, they could not be satisfied. They suffered from hunger. If they could obtain human food, they could be full for a year. Many ghosts often stole human food. Being valuable, I could not steal it. I asked you for a meal. Xian informed Wenben, and Wenben then prepared the meal, arranging exquisite delicacies. Xian said, "Ghosts do not want to enter human houses. They can be set up outside, with a tent and a table, and wine and food placed on it." Wenben followed his words. At the time, Ren Xian saw two guests arrive and sit down, accompanied by over a hundred riders. After sitting, Wenben bowed twice toward the table, apologized for the food not being refined, also conveyed the meaning, and expressed gratitude. Initially, Wenben was going to prepare the food, and Ren Xian asked for gold and silk to redeem it. Wenben asked what kind of things they were. Xian explained that things used by ghosts differ from those used by humans, only gold and silk can be used universally, but they are not as good as fake ones, by coating large tin with yellow paint to make gold, and using paper to make silk, which are the most valuable. Wenben followed his instructions and made them, and when the guests had finished eating. [31]

    令其从骑更代坐食。文本以所作金钱绢赠之。景深喜谢曰。因睦生烦郎君供给。郎君颇欲知年寿命乎。文本辞曰。不愿知也。景辞而去。数年后。仁茜遇病不甚困笃。而又不能起。月余曰。茜问常掌事。掌事云不知。便问长史。长史报云。国内不知。后曰因朝太山。为消息相报。至后月。长史自来报云。是君乡人赵武为太山主簿。主簿一员阙。荐君为此官。故为文案经纪。召君耳。案成者当死。茜问计将安出景云。君寿应年六十余。今始四十。但以赵主簿横徵召耳。当为君请之。乃曰赵主簿相问。睦兄昔与同学。恩情深重。 [32]

    Relevant Explanation

    Explanation

    [32]

    Idiomatic Expressions

    • 令其从骑更代坐食 - Literally 'let his followers take turns sitting and eating,' used to describe a method of providing food or rest to a group.
    • 文本以所作金钱绢赠之 - 'Wenben gave him the money and silk he had made,' where '金钱绢' refers to valuable goods or gifts.
    • 景深喜谢曰 - 'Jing deeply thanked and said,' indicating a formal expression of gratitude.
    • 因睦生烦郎君供给 - 'Because of the relationship with Muxing, it troubled you to provide for me,' where '睦生' likely refers to a person or relationship.
    • 郎君颇欲知年寿命乎 - 'Do you wish to know your lifespan?' where '年寿命' refers to one's expected lifespan.
    • 文本辞曰 - 'Wenben replied,' indicating a formal response.
    • 不愿知也 - 'I do not wish to know,' expressing a refusal.
    • 景辞而去 - 'Jing took his leave,' indicating a formal departure.
    • 数年后 - 'Several years later,' indicating a passage of time.
    • 仁茜遇病不甚困笃 - 'Ren Qian fell ill but was not severely troubled,' where '不甚困笃' means 'not very seriously ill.'
    • 而又不能起 - 'And yet could not get up,' indicating a state of illness.
    • 月余曰 - 'After more than a month,' indicating a passage of time.
    • 茜问常掌事 - 'Qian asked the usual attendant,' where '常掌事' refers to a regular attendant or servant.
    • 掌事云不知 - 'The attendant said he did not know,' indicating a lack of information.
    • 便问长史 - 'Then he asked the Grand Historian,' where '长史' refers to a high-ranking official.
    • 长史报云 - 'The Grand Historian reported,' indicating a formal response.
    • 国内不知 - 'No one in the country knew,' indicating a lack of information.
    • 后曰因朝太山 - 'Later, because of the court in Tai Shan,' where '朝太山' refers to a court or gathering in Tai Shan.
    • 为消息相报 - 'To report the news,' indicating a formal communication.
    • 至后月 - 'By the following month,' indicating a passage of time.
    • 长史自来报云 - 'The Grand Historian came to report,' indicating a formal communication.
    • 是君乡人赵武为太山主簿 - 'It was your fellow townsman Zhao Wu who became the Chief Clerk of Tai Shan,' where '乡人' refers to a fellow townsman.
    • 主簿一员阙 - 'There was a vacancy for the Chief Clerk,' indicating a position that needed to be filled.
    • 荐君为此官 - 'He recommended you for this position,' indicating a formal nomination.
    • 故为文案经纪 - 'Therefore, the documents were processed,' indicating formal paperwork.
    • 召君耳 - 'To summon you,' indicating a formal call.
    • 案成者当死 - 'Those who completed the case would die,' indicating a serious consequence.
    • 茜问计将安出 - 'Qian asked what plan would be taken,' indicating a request for a solution.
    • 景云 - 'Jing said,' indicating a formal response.
    • 君寿应年六十余 - 'Your lifespan should be around sixty,' indicating an expected lifespan.
    • 今始四十 - 'You are now forty,' indicating the current age.
    • 但以赵主簿横徵召耳 - 'But because Zhao, the Chief Clerk, arbitrarily summoned you,' indicating an arbitrary action.
    • 当为君请之 - 'I will plead for you,' indicating a formal request.
    • 乃曰赵主簿相问 - 'Then said that Zhao, the Chief Clerk, asked,' indicating a formal communication.
    • 睦兄昔与同学 - 'Muxing, your elder brother, studied with you in the past,' indicating a past relationship.
    • 恩情深重 - 'The bond of friendship was deep,' indicating a strong relationship.

    Cultural and Historical Allusions

    • 文本 - Refers to Wenben, a person in the story.
    • 景 - Refers to Jing, a person in the story.
    • 睦生 - Likely refers to a person or relationship.
    • 仁茜 - Refers to Ren Qian, a person in the story.
    • 长史 - Refers to a high-ranking official, possibly a Grand Historian.
    • 太山 - Refers to Tai Shan, a mountain with historical and cultural significance.
    • 赵武 - Refers to Zhao Wu, a person in the story.
    • 主簿 - Refers to a Chief Clerk, a high-ranking official.
    • 乡人 - Refers to a fellow townsman.
    • 文案 - Refers to formal paperwork or documents.

    Proper Nouns

    • 文本 - Refers to Wenben.
    • 景 - Refers to Jing.
    • 睦生 - Likely refers to a person or relationship.
    • 仁茜 - Refers to Ren Qian.
    • 长史 - Refers to a high-ranking official.
    • 太山 - Refers to Tai Shan.
    • 赵武 - Refers to Zhao Wu.
    • 主簿 - Refers to a Chief Clerk.
    • 乡人 - Refers to a fellow townsman.
    • 文案 - Refers to formal paperwork.

    Let his followers take turns sitting and eating. Wenben gave him the money and silk he had made. Jing deeply thanked and said, "Because of the relationship with Muxing, it troubled you to provide for me." Do you wish to know your lifespan? Wenben replied, "I do not wish to know." Jing took his leave. Several years later, Ren Qian fell ill but was not severely troubled, and yet could not get up. After more than a month, Qian asked the usual attendant, who said he did not know. Then he asked the Grand Historian, who reported, "No one in the country knew." Later, because of the court in Tai Shan, to report the news. By the following month, the Grand Historian came to report, "It was your fellow townsman Zhao Wu who became the Chief Clerk of Tai Shan. There was a vacancy for the Chief Clerk. He recommended you for this position. Therefore, the documents were processed to summon you. Those who completed the case would die." Qian asked what plan would be taken. Jing said, "Your lifespan should be around sixty. You are now forty. But because Zhao, the Chief Clerk, arbitrarily summoned you, I will plead for you." Then said that Zhao, the Chief Clerk, asked, "Muxing, your elder brother, studied with you in the past. The bond of friendship was deep." [32]

    今幸得为太山主簿。适遇一主簿阙。府今择人。吾已启公。公许相用。兄既不得长生。会当有死。死遇际会。未必得官。何惜一二十年。茍贪生也。今文书已出。不可复止。愿决作来意。无所疑也。茜忧惧。病愈笃。景谓茜曰。赵主簿。必欲致君。君可自太山。于府君陈诉。则可以免。茜问。何由见府君。景曰鬼者可得见耳。往太山庙。东度一小岭。岭平地。是其都所。君自当见之。茜以告文本。文本为具行装束。数曰。景又来告茜曰。文书欲成。君诉惧不可免。急一佛像。彼文书自消息。茜告文本。 [33]

    Relevant Explanation

    Explanation

    [33]

    Idiomatic Expressions

    • 幸得 - Fortunate to have; lucky to be.
    • 适遇 - Just happened to encounter.
    • 择人 - Selecting someone.
    • 启公 - Informed the governor.
    • 许相用 - Agreed to use me.
    • 既不得 - Cannot achieve.
    • 会当 - Will eventually.
    • 际会 - Opportunities or chances.
    • 未必 - May not necessarily.
    • 何惜 - Why be reluctant to give up.
    • 贪生 - Greedy for life.
    • 文书 - Official document.
    • 复止 - Be stopped.
    • 愿决 - I hope you can make a decision.
    • 作来意 - About coming.
    • 无所疑 - Without any doubt.
    • 忧惧 - Worried and fearful.
    • 愈笃 - Becomes more severe.
    • 谓 - Told.
    • 必欲 - Wants to.
    • 自 - From.
    • 陈诉 - Complaint.
    • 则可以 - Then you can.
    • 问 - Asked.
    • 何由 - How can.
    • 鬼者 - Ghosts.
    • 可得见耳 - Can be seen.
    • 东度 - Cross to the east.
    • 平地 - Level ground.
    • 都所 - Capital.
    • 自当 - Will naturally.
    • 以告 - Told.

    Cultural and Historical Allusions

    • 太山 - Tai Mountain, one of the Five Great Mountains in China, often associated with longevity and immortality.
    • 主簿 - A low-level administrative official, typically responsible for clerical duties.
    • 府君 - A term of respect for the governor or a high-ranking official.
    • 赵主簿 - A specific individual holding the position of Chief Clerk.
    • 太山庙 - The Temple of Tai Mountain, a significant religious site.

    Proper Nouns

    • 太山 - Tai Mountain.
    • 主簿 - Chief Clerk.
    • 府君 - Governor.
    • 赵主簿 - Zhao Chief Clerk.
    • 太山庙 - Temple of Tai Mountain.
    • 茜 - Qian.
    • 景 - Jing.
    • 文本 - Wenben.

    Now I am fortunate to have become the Chief Clerk of Tai Mountain. Just happened to encounter a vacancy for the Chief Clerk. The governor is now selecting someone. I have already informed the governor. The governor has agreed to use me. Brother, you cannot achieve immortality. You will eventually die. When you die, you may encounter opportunities. You may not necessarily get an official position. Why be reluctant to give up a few decades? If you are greedy for life. Now the official document has been issued. It cannot be stopped now. I hope you can make a decision about coming. Without any doubt. Qian is worried and fearful. The illness becomes more severe. Jing told Qian. Zhao Chief Clerk wants to bring you. You can come from Tai Mountain. To the governor for a complaint. Then you can avoid it. Qian asked. How can I see the governor? Jing said that only ghosts can be seen. Go to the Tai Mountain Temple. Cross a small hill to the east. The hill is level ground. This is their capital. You will naturally see it. Qian told Wenben. Wenben prepared the necessary travel arrangements. Several days later, Jing came again to tell Qian. The official document is about to be finalized. You should be afraid of making a complaint. Quickly get a Buddha statue. That way, the official document will naturally be resolved. Qian told Wenben. [33]

    以三千钱为画一座像于寺西壁。既而景来告曰免矣。茜情不信佛。意尚疑之。因问景云。佛法说有三世因果。此为虚实。答曰实。茜曰。即如是人死。当分入六道。那得尽为鬼。而赵武灵王。及君今尚为鬼耶。景曰。君县内几户。茜曰万余户。又狱囚几人。茜曰。常二十人已下。又曰。万户之内。有五品官几人。茜曰无。又曰。九品已上。官几人。茜曰。数十人。景曰。六道之内。亦一如此耳。其得六道。万无一人。如君县内无一五品官。得人道者有数人。如君九品。入地狱者亦数十。如君狱内囚。唯鬼及畜生。 [34]

    Relevant Explanation

    Explanation

    [34]

    Idiomatic Expressions

    • 以三千钱为画一座像于寺西壁 - Spends three thousand coins to have a painting of an image made on the west wall of the temple; '为画一座像' is an archaic expression meaning 'to commission a painting of an image'.
    • 既而景来告曰免矣 - '既而' means 'afterwards' or 'later', and '免矣' means 'it is exempted' or 'it is free'; indicates that later, Jing came to inform that something was exempted or free.
    • 茜情不信佛 - '茜情' is a proper name, likely a person's name, and '不信佛' means 'does not believe in Buddhism'.
    • 佛法说有三世因果 - '佛法' means 'Buddhist teachings', '三世' means 'three lifetimes' or 'past, present, and future', and '因果' means 'cause and effect'; refers to the Buddhist concept of karma and rebirth.
    • 此为虚实 - '此为' means 'this is', and '虚实' means 'true or false'; asks whether the concept is true or false.
    • 答曰实 - '答曰' means 'answered', and '实' means 'true'; indicates that the response was 'true'.
    • 即如是人死 - '即如是' means 'just as' or 'for example', and '人死' means 'people die'; introduces an example.
    • 当分入六道 - '当分' means 'will be divided', and '六道' means 'the six realms' (heaven, human, asura, animal, hungry ghost, and hell); suggests that people will be reborn into one of the six realms.
    • 那得尽为鬼 - '那得' means 'how can', and '尽为鬼' means 'all be ghosts'; questions how all people can become ghosts.
    • 而赵武灵王及君今尚为鬼耶 - '而' means 'and', '赵武灵王' is the name of a historical figure, '及' means 'and', '君' means 'you' or 'lord', and '尚为鬼耶' means 'still are ghosts?'; asks if Sui Wuling and the person being addressed are still ghosts.
    • 君县内几户 - '君' means 'you' or 'lord', '县内' means 'within the county', '几户' means 'how many households'; asks how many households are in the county.
    • 茜曰万余户 - '茜曰' means 'Xian said', and '万余户' means 'more than ten thousand households'; indicates that Xian responded with 'more than ten thousand households'.
    • 又狱囚几人 - '又' means 'also', '狱囚' means 'prisoners', and '几人' means 'how many people'; asks how many prisoners there are.
    • 茜曰常二十人已下 - '茜曰' means 'Xian said', and '常二十人已下' means 'usually no more than twenty people'; indicates that Xian responded with 'usually no more than twenty people'.
    • 万户之内 - '万户' means 'ten thousand households', and '之内' means 'within'; means 'within ten thousand households'.
    • 有五品官几人 - '有' means 'have', '五品官' means 'officials of the fifth rank', and '几人' means 'how many people'; asks how many officials of the fifth rank there are.
    • 茜曰无 - '茜曰' means 'Xian said', and '无' means 'none'; indicates that Xian responded with 'none'.
    • 九品已上 - '九品' means 'officials of the ninth rank', and '已上' means 'and above'; means 'officials of the ninth rank and above'.
    • 官几人 - '官' means 'officials', and '几人' means 'how many people'; asks how many officials there are.
    • 茜曰数十人 - '茜曰' means 'Xian said', and '数十人' means 'dozens of people'; indicates that Xian responded with 'dozens of people'.
    • 六道之内 - '六道' means 'the six realms', and '之内' means 'within'; means 'within the six realms'.
    • 亦一如此耳 - '亦' means 'also', '一' means 'like', '如此' means 'this way', and '耳' means 'only'; means 'it is also like this'.
    • 其得六道 - '其' means 'they', '得' means 'get' or 'obtain', and '六道' means 'the six realms'; means 'they get into the six realms'.
    • 万无一人 - '万无' means 'not a single', and '一人' means 'one person'; means 'not a single person'.
    • 如君县内无一五品官 - '如' means 'like', '君县内' means 'within your county', '无一' means 'not a single', and '五品官' means 'officials of the fifth rank'; means 'like within your county, not a single official of the fifth rank'.
    • 得人道者有数人 - '得' means 'get' or 'obtain', '人道' means 'the human realm', '者' means 'those who', '有' means 'have', and '数人' means 'several people'; means 'those who obtain the human realm have several people'.
    • 如君九品 - '如' means 'like', '君' means 'you' or 'lord', and '九品' means 'officials of the ninth rank'; means 'like you, officials of the ninth rank'.
    • 入地狱者亦数十 - '入' means 'enter', '地狱' means 'hell', '者' means 'those who', '亦' means 'also', and '数十' means 'dozens'; means 'those who enter hell also have dozens'.
    • 如君狱内囚 - '如' means 'like', '君' means 'you' or 'lord', '狱内' means 'within the prison', and '囚' means 'prisoners'; means 'like within your prison, prisoners'.
    • 唯鬼及畜生 - '唯' means 'only', '鬼' means 'ghosts', and '畜生' means 'animals'; means 'only ghosts and animals'.

    Cultural and Historical Allusions

    • 三千钱 - Refers to a specific amount of money, which was a significant sum in ancient times.
    • 寺西壁 - Refers to the west wall of a temple, indicating a specific location for the painting.
    • 景 - A proper name, likely a person's name.
    • 茜 - A proper name, likely a person's name.
    • 赵武灵王 - A historical figure, the King of Zhao during the Warring States period.
    • 六道 - Refers to the six realms of existence in Buddhist cosmology: heaven, human, asura, animal, hungry ghost, and hell.
    • 五品官 - Refers to officials of the fifth rank in the Chinese bureaucratic system.
    • 九品官 - Refers to officials of the ninth rank in the Chinese bureaucratic system.
    • 人道 - Refers to the human realm in Buddhist cosmology.

    Xian spent three thousand coins to have a painting of an image made on the west wall of the temple. Later, Jing came to inform that it was exempted or free. Xian, who did not believe in Buddhism, still had doubts. He then asked Jing, "According to Buddhist teachings, there is cause and effect across three lifetimes. Is this true or false?" Jing replied, "True." Xian said, "Just as people die, they will be divided into the six realms. How can all of them become ghosts? And Sui Wuling and you are still ghosts?" Jing said, "How many households are within your county?" Xian said, "More than ten thousand households." Jing asked again, "How many prisoners are there?" Xian said, "Usually no more than twenty people." Jing continued, "Within ten thousand households, how many officials of the fifth rank are there?" Xian said, "None." Jing asked, "How many officials of the ninth rank and above are there?" Xian said, "Dozens of people." Jing said, "Within the six realms, it is also like this. They get into the six realms, but not a single person. Like within your county, not a single official of the fifth rank, those who obtain the human realm have several people. Like you, officials of the ninth rank, those who enter hell also have dozens. Like within your prison, prisoners, only ghosts and animals." [34]

    最为多也。如君县内课役户。就此道中。又有等级。因指其从者曰。彼人大不如我。其不及彼者尤多。茜曰。鬼有死乎。曰然。茜曰。死入何道。答曰不知。如人知死。而不知死后之事。茜问曰。道家章醮。为有益不。景曰。道者。天帝总统六道。是谓天曹。阎罗王者。如人天子。太山府君尚书令。录五道神如诸尚书。若我辈国。如大州郡。每人间事。道上章请福。天曹受之。下阎罗王云。某月曰得某甲诉云云。宜尽理勿令枉滥。阎罗敬受而奉行之。如人之奉诏也。无理不可求免。有枉必当得申。可为无益也。 [35]

    Relevant Explanation

    Explanation

    [35]

    Idiomatic Expressions

    • 最为多也 - Comparative phrase meaning 'it is the most numerous' or 'it is the most abundant.'
    • 如君县内课役户 - Descriptive phrase referring to the households within a county that are subject to taxation and labor duties.
    • 就此道中 - Phrase meaning 'within this path' or 'in this context.'
    • 又有等级 - Phrase meaning 'there are also levels' or 'there are hierarchical distinctions.'
    • 彼人大不如我 - Comparative phrase meaning 'that person is not as good as I am' or 'that person is inferior to me.'
    • 其不及彼者尤多 - Phrase meaning 'those who are inferior to that person are even more numerous.'
    • 鬼有死乎 - Question meaning 'do ghosts die?'
    • 死入何道 - Question meaning 'which path do they enter after death?'
    • 如人知死，而不知死后之事 - Comparative phrase meaning 'just as people know about death but do not know what happens after death.'
    • 道家章醮 - Phrase referring to Daoist rituals involving the burning of offerings and incantations.
    • 为有益不 - Question meaning 'is it beneficial?'
    • 天帝总统六道 - Phrase meaning 'the Heavenly Emperor oversees the Six Paths.'
    • 天曹 - Term referring to the celestial bureaucracy.
    • 阎罗王者 - Term referring to the King of Hell.
    • 太山府君尚书令 - Term referring to the Lord of Mount Tai, who is often depicted as a high-ranking official in the underworld.
    • 录五道神 - Phrase meaning 'recording the Five Path deities.'
    • 诸尚书 - Term referring to various high-ranking officials.
    • 每人间事 - Phrase meaning 'each matter concerning the human world.'
    • 道上章请福 - Phrase meaning 'submitting petitions to the Dao for blessings.'
    • 天曹受之 - Phrase meaning 'the celestial bureaucracy receives them.'
    • 下阎罗王云 - Phrase meaning 'the King of Hell is informed.'
    • 某月曰得某甲诉云云 - Phrase meaning 'on a certain day, a certain person named A makes a complaint, etc.'
    • 宜尽理勿令枉滥 - Phrase meaning 'it should be handled properly without causing injustice.'
    • 阎罗敬受而奉行之 - Phrase meaning 'the King of Hell respectfully receives and carries out the instructions.'
    • 如人之奉诏也 - Phrase meaning 'just as a person obeys an imperial decree.'
    • 无理不可求免 - Phrase meaning 'there is no way to avoid it if there is no reason.'
    • 有枉必当得申 - Phrase meaning 'if there is injustice, it must be rectified.'
    • 可为无益也 - Phrase meaning 'it can be considered beneficial.'

    Cultural and Historical Allusions

    • 鬼 - Refers to ghosts or spirits in Chinese folklore.
    • 道家 - Refers to Daoism, a major Chinese philosophical and religious tradition.
    • 章醮 - Refers to specific Daoist rituals involving offerings and incantations.
    • 天帝 - Refers to the Heavenly Emperor, a central figure in Chinese cosmology.
    • 六道 - Refers to the Six Paths of Rebirth in Buddhist and Daoist cosmology.
    • 天曹 - Refers to the celestial bureaucracy, a concept in Chinese mythology and religion.
    • 阎罗王 - Refers to the King of Hell, a figure in Chinese folklore and religious belief.
    • 太山府君 - Refers to the Lord of Mount Tai, a deity associated with the underworld.
    • 尚书令 - Refers to a high-ranking official in the imperial administration.
    • 五道神 - Refers to the Five Path deities, which oversee different aspects of the afterlife.
    • 人间事 - Refers to matters concerning the human world.
    • 上章请福 - Refers to the practice of submitting petitions to the Dao for blessings and favors.

    Proper Nouns

    • 天帝 - The Heavenly Emperor.
    • 六道 - The Six Paths of Rebirth.
    • 天曹 - The celestial bureaucracy.
    • 阎罗王 - The King of Hell.
    • 太山府君 - The Lord of Mount Tai.
    • 五道神 - The Five Path deities.
    • 诸尚书 - Various high-ranking officials.

    The most numerous are the households within the county that are subject to taxation and labor duties. In this context, there are also hierarchical distinctions. He pointed to his attendants and said, "That person is not as good as I am, and those who are inferior to that person are even more numerous." Qian asked, "Do ghosts die?" The answer was yes. Qian asked, "Which path do they enter after death?" The response was unknown. Just as people know about death but do not know what happens after death, Qian asked, "Are Daoist rituals beneficial?" Jing replied, "The Dao is overseen by the Heavenly Emperor, who presides over the Six Paths, which is known as the celestial bureaucracy. The King of Hell is like a human emperor, and the Lord of Mount Tai, who is a high-ranking official, records the Five Path deities, similar to various high-ranking officials. If we were to consider our own country, it would be like a large state or prefecture. For each matter concerning the human world, petitions are submitted to the Dao for blessings. The celestial bureaucracy receives them and informs the King of Hell, saying, 'On a certain day, a certain person named A makes a complaint, etc.' It should be handled properly without causing injustice. The King of Hell respectfully receives and carries out the instructions, just as a person obeys an imperial decree. There is no way to avoid it if there is no reason, and if there is injustice, it must be rectified. It can be considered beneficial." [35]

    何又问。佛家修福何如。景曰。佛是大圣。无文书行下。其修福。天神敬奉。多得宽宥。若福厚者。虽有恶道文簿。不得追摄。此非吾所识。亦莫知其所以然。言毕去。茜一二曰能起便愈。文本父卒。还乡里。茜寄书曰。鬼神定是贪谄。往曰欲郎君饮食。乃尔殷勤。比知无复厚利相见殊落漠。然常掌事犹见随。本县为贼所陷。死亡略尽。仆为掌事所道。常如贼不见。竟以获全。贞观十六年九月九曰。文官赐识于玄武北门。文本时为中书侍郎。与家兄太府乡。及治书侍御史马周给事中韦琨。及临对坐。文本自谓诸人云尔。 [36]

    He then asked, "What is the nature of merit-making in Buddhism?" Jing replied, "Buddha is a great sage, and there are no written regulations. In terms of merit-making, he is revered by heavenly deities and often receives leniency. If one has accumulated great merit, even if there are records of bad deeds, they cannot be pursued. This is beyond my knowledge, and I also do not know why." After speaking, he left. Qian said after a short time that the patient could stand and recover. Wenben's father had died, and he returned to his hometown. Qian sent a letter saying, "Ghosts and spirits are definitely greedy and flattering. In the past, they wanted the gentleman to eat and drink, and were so diligent. Now they know there is no more profit, and the meeting is very cold. Although they still manage affairs, they still follow." The county was invaded by bandits, and almost everyone died. I was protected by the person in charge and was always like the bandits did not see me, and I finally managed to survive. On the ninth day of the ninth month in the sixteenth year of the Zhenguan era, officials were given recognition at the Northern Gate of Xuanwu. Wenben was serving as the Secretary of the Central Secretariat, with his elder brother, the Minister of the Imperial Household, and the Censor-in-Chief Ma Zhou, the Censor-in-Chief Wei Kun, and the Minister of the Imperial Household. Wenben said to all of them, "Thus." [36]

    殿中侍御医孙回璞。济阴人也。贞观十三年。从车驾幸九城宫三善父与魏太师邻家。尝夜二更。闻门外为人唤孙侍医声。璞出看。谓是太师之命。既出见两人。谓璞曰。官唤。璞曰。我不能步行。即取璞马乘之。随二人行。乃觉天地如昼曰光明。璞怪讶而不敢言。二人引璞出谷口。历朝堂东。又东北六七里。至苜蓿谷。遥见有两人。将韩凤方行。语所引璞二人曰。汝等错。我所得者是。汝宜放彼人。即放璞。璞循路而还。往还往不谓平生所处。既至家。系马。见婢当户眠。唤之不应。越度入户。见其身与妇并眠。欲就之而不得。但着南壁立。大声唤妇。终不应。屋内极明。见壁角中有蜘蛛网。网中有二蝇。一大一小。等见梁所着药物。无不分明。唯不得就床。知是死。甚忧闷。恨不得共妻别。倚立南壁。久之微睡。忽惊觉。觉身已卧床上。而屋中暗黑无所见。唤告妇。令妇燃火。而大汗。起视蜘蛛网。历然不殊。见马亦大汗。凤方是夜暴死。 [37]

    Relevant Explanation

    Explanation

    [37]

    Idiomatic Expressions

    • 殿中侍御医 - A title for an official physician in the imperial court, literally meaning 'attendant physician of the palace.'
    • 从车驾幸九城宫 - Following the imperial carriage to the Nine Cities Palace, where '车驾' refers to the emperor's entourage or carriage.
    • 三善父与魏太师邻家 - 'Three Good Father' and 'Wei Grand Tutor' were neighbors, where '三善父' and '魏太师' are proper names or titles.
    • 尝夜二更 - Once at the second watch of the night, where '二更' refers to a specific time period in the night.
    • 闻门外为人唤孙侍医声 - Hearing someone calling the name of the attendant physician outside the door, where '唤' means to call out.
    • 官唤 - The official is calling, a formal way of indicating that someone is being summoned.
    • 我不能步行 - I cannot walk, indicating physical inability.
    • 取璞马乘之 - Took Sun's horse and rode it, where '乘之' means to ride.
    • 天地如昼曰光明 - The world was as bright as day, a hyperbolic description of brightness.
    • 引璞出谷口 - Led Sun out of the valley, where '引' means to lead or guide.
    • 历朝堂东 - Passed by the east of the court, indicating a specific direction.
    • 又东北六七里 - Then northeast for six or seven li, where 'li' is an ancient Chinese unit of distance.
    • 至苜蓿谷 - Reached the Alfalfa Valley, where '苜蓿' is the name of a type of plant.
    • 遥见有两人 - Saw two people from afar, where '遥' means distant.
    • 将韩凤方行 - Guided Han Feng to walk, where '将' means to guide or lead.
    • 汝等错 - You are mistaken, a formal way of indicating error.
    • 我所得者是 - What I have is correct, indicating possession of something right.
    • 汝宜放彼人 - You should release that person, where '放' means to release.
    • 循路而还 - Followed the road back, where '循' means to follow.
    • 往还往不谓平生所处 - Going back and forth, it was not what he had experienced in life, indicating a sense of unfamiliarity.
    • 既至家 - After arriving home, where '既' indicates completion.
    • 系马 - Tied the horse, where '系' means to tie.
    • 见婢当户眠 - Saw a maid sleeping by the door, where '婢' means maid.
    • 唤之不应 - Called her but she did not respond, where '不应' means no response.
    • 越度入户 - Stepped over and entered the house, where '越度' means to step over.
    • 见其身与妇并眠 - Saw her body lying beside the wife, indicating a ghostly or dreamlike state.
    • 欲就之而不得 - Wanted to approach her but could not, indicating a sense of inability.
    • 但着南壁立 - But stood by the south wall, where '着' means to stand.
    • 大声唤妇 - Shouted loudly to the wife, where '大声' means loudly.
    • 终不应 - Finally did not respond, where '终' means finally.
    • 屋内极明 - The room was extremely bright, indicating a vivid description.
    • 见壁角中有蜘蛛网 - Saw a spider web in the corner of the wall, where '壁角' means corner of the wall.
    • 网中有二蝇 - The web had two flies, indicating a specific detail.
    • 一大一小 - One large and one small, describing the flies.
    • 等见梁所着药物 - Saw the medicine on the beam, where '梁' means beam.
    • 无不分明 - All were clearly visible, indicating clarity.
    • 唯不得就床 - But could not approach the bed, indicating a sense of inability.
    • 知是死 - Knew it was death, indicating a realization.
    • 甚忧闷 - Was very worried and sad, where '忧闷' means worried and sad.
    • 恨不得共妻别 - Wanted to say goodbye to his wife but could not, indicating a sense of regret.
    • 倚立南壁 - Leaned against the south wall, where '倚' means to lean.
    • 久之微睡 - After a while, fell into a light sleep, where '微睡' means light sleep.
    • 忽惊觉 - Suddenly woke up in a start, where '惊觉' means to wake up suddenly.
    • 觉身已卧床上 - Felt himself lying on the bed, indicating a return to reality.
    • 而屋中暗黑无所见 - But the room was dark and nothing could be seen, indicating a return to normalcy.
    • 唤告妇 - Called out to the wife, where '唤告' means to call out.
    • 令妇燃火 - Told the wife to light a fire, where '燃火' means to light a fire.
    • 而大汗 - And broke out in a sweat, indicating a physical reaction.
    • 起视蜘蛛网 - Got up and looked at the spider web, where '视' means to look.
    • 历然不殊 - Was still clearly visible, indicating continuity.
    • 见马亦大汗 - Saw the horse also sweating, indicating a parallel observation.
    • 凤方是夜暴死 - Han Feng died suddenly that night, where '暴死' means sudden death.

    Cultural and Historical Allusions

    • 殿中侍御医 - A high-ranking official physician in the imperial court, indicating a position of importance.
    • 从车驾幸九城宫 - The Nine Cities Palace was a significant imperial residence, and following the emperor's entourage was a prestigious duty.
    • 三善父与魏太师邻家 - 'Three Good Father' and 'Wei Grand Tutor' are likely historical figures or titles, indicating a connection to the imperial court.
    • 尝夜二更 - The second watch of the night (around 2 AM) was a time when most people were asleep, making the event unusual.
    • 闻门外为人唤孙侍医声 - Being called out by name at night suggests an urgent or official matter.
    • 官唤 - A formal way of indicating an official summons, adding to the gravity of the situation.
    • 我不能步行 - Indicating physical inability, which adds to the urgency of the situation.
    • 取璞马乘之 - Taking and riding a horse suggests a need for speed, indicating the seriousness of the matter.
    • 天地如昼曰光明 - A hyperbolic description of brightness, suggesting a supernatural or dreamlike state.
    • 引璞出谷口 - Being led out of a valley suggests a journey or a transition.
    • 历朝堂东 - Passing by the east of the court suggests a specific direction and route.
    • 又东北六七里 - Traveling northeast for six or seven li suggests a significant distance.
    • 至苜蓿谷 - The Alfalfa Valley is likely a specific location with cultural or historical significance.
    • 遥见有两人 - Seeing two people from afar suggests a sense of mystery or otherworldliness.
    • 将韩凤方行 - Guiding someone to walk suggests a sense of control or direction.
    • 汝等错 - A formal way of indicating error, suggesting a correction or guidance.
    • 我所得者是 - Indicating possession of something right suggests a sense of correctness or authority.
    • 汝宜放彼人 - A formal way of indicating release, suggesting a sense of justice or mercy.
    • 循路而还 - Following the road back suggests a return to normalcy.
    • 往还往不谓平生所处 - Indicating unfamiliarity suggests a sense of disorientation or otherworldliness.
    • 既至家 - Arriving home suggests a return to normalcy.
    • 系马 - Tying the horse suggests a sense of security or stability.
    • 见婢当户眠 - Seeing a maid sleeping by the door suggests a sense of domesticity or normalcy.
    • 唤之不应 - No response suggests a sense of absence or otherworldliness.
    • 越度入户 - Stepping over and entering the house suggests a sense of intrusion or transition.
    • 见其身与妇并眠 - Seeing her body lying beside the wife suggests a sense of ghostly or dreamlike state.
    • 欲就之而不得 - Wanting to approach but not being able to suggests a sense of inability or otherworldliness.
    • 但着南壁立 - Standing by the south wall suggests a sense of isolation or contemplation.
    • 大声唤妇 - Shouting loudly to the wife suggests a sense of urgency or distress.
    • 终不应 - Finally did not respond, where '终' means finally.
    • 屋内极明 - The room was extremely bright, indicating a vivid description.
    • 见壁角中有蜘蛛网 - Saw a spider web in the corner of the wall, where '壁角' means corner of the wall.
    • 网中有二蝇 - The web had two flies, indicating a specific detail.
    • 一大一小 - One large and one small, describing the flies.
    • 等见梁所着药物 - Saw the medicine on the beam, where '梁' means beam.
    • 无不分明 - All were clearly visible, indicating clarity.
    • 唯不得就床 - But could not approach the bed, indicating a sense of inability.
    • 知是死 - Knew it was death, indicating a realization.
    • 甚忧闷 - Was very worried and sad, where '忧闷' means worried and sad.
    • 恨不得共妻别 - Wanted to say goodbye to his wife but could not, indicating a sense of regret.
    • 倚立南壁 - Leaned against the south wall, where '倚' means to lean.
    • 久之微睡 - After a while, fell into a light sleep, where '微睡' means light sleep.
    • 忽惊觉 - Suddenly woke up in a start, where '惊觉' means to wake up suddenly.
    • 觉身已卧床上 - Felt himself lying on the bed, indicating a return to reality.
    • 而屋中暗黑无所见 - But the room was dark and nothing could be seen, indicating a return to normalcy.
    • 唤告妇 - Called out to the wife, where '唤告' means to call out.
    • 令妇燃火 - Told the wife to light a fire, where '燃火' means to light a fire.
    • 而大汗 - And broke out in a sweat, indicating a physical reaction.
    • 起视蜘蛛网 - Got up and looked at the spider web, where '视' means to look.
    • 历然不殊 - Was still clearly visible, indicating continuity.
    • 见马亦大汗 - Saw the horse also sweating, indicating a parallel observation.
    • 凤方是夜暴死 - Han Feng died suddenly that night, where '暴死' means sudden death.

    Attendant Physician Sun Huipu of the Palace. He was from Jiyin. In the thirteenth year of the Zhenguan era, he followed the imperial carriage to the Nine Cities Palace. Three Good Father and Wei Grand Tutor were neighbors. Once at the second watch of the night, he heard someone calling the name of the attendant physician outside the door. Sun went out to see, thinking it was the Grand Tutor's command. After going out, he saw two people who told Sun, "The official is calling." Sun replied, "I cannot walk." They then took Sun's horse and rode it. Following the two people, he felt as if the world was as bright as day. Sun was surprised but dared not speak. The two led Sun out of the valley, passing by the east of the court, then northeast for six or seven li, reaching the Alfalfa Valley. From afar, he saw two people guiding Han Feng to walk. They told the two who had led Sun, "You are mistaken. What I have is correct. You should release that person." They then released Sun. Sun followed the road back, finding it unlike anything he had experienced in life. After arriving home, he tied the horse, saw a maid sleeping by the door, called her but she did not respond. He stepped over and entered the house, seeing her body lying beside the wife. He wanted to approach her but could not, so he stood by the south wall, shouting loudly to the wife, who finally did not respond. The room was extremely bright, and he saw a spider web in the corner of the wall, with two flies, one large and one small. He saw the medicine on the beam, all clearly visible, but could not approach the bed. Knowing it was death, he was very worried and sad, wanting to say goodbye to his wife but could not. He leaned against the south wall, after a while fell into a light sleep, and suddenly woke up in a start, feeling himself lying on the bed, but the room was dark and nothing could be seen. He called out to the wife, told her to light a fire, and broke out in a sweat. Getting up and looking at the spider web, it was still clearly visible. He saw the horse also sweating. Han Feng died suddenly that night. [37]

    后至十七年。璞奉敕驰驿往。齐王佑疾还。至洛州东孝义驿。忽见一人来问。君是孙回璞不。曰是。君何问为。答曰我是鬼耳。魏太师有文书。追君为记室。因出文书示璞。璞视之。则郑国公魏徵署也。璞惊曰。郑公不死。何为遣君送书。鬼曰。已死矣。今为太阳都录大监。故令我召君。回璞引共食。鬼甚喜谢璞。璞请曰。我奉敕使未还。郑公不宜追。我还京奏事毕。然后听命。可乎。鬼许之。于是昼则同行。夜同宿。遂至阌乡。鬼辞曰。吾取过所度关待君。璞度关出西门。见鬼已在门外。复同行。至滋水驿。鬼又与璞别曰。待君奏事讫。相见也。君可勿食薰辛。璞许诺。既奏事毕。而访郑公已薨。挍其薨曰。则孝义驿之前曰也。璞自以必死与家人诀别。而请僧行道。造像写经。可六七曰。夜梦前鬼来召。引璞上高山。山岭有大宫殿。既入。见众君子迎谓曰。此人修福。不得留之。可放去。即推璞堕山。于是惊悟。遂至今无恙矣。回璞自为临说云尔。 [38]

    Relevant Explanation

    Explanation

    [38]

    Idiomatic Expressions

    • 后至十七年 - Formal expression indicating 'in the seventeenth year.'
    • 奉敕驰驿往 - Means 'to be ordered to travel by express post,' indicating an official mission.
    • 齐王佑疾还 - Means 'Prince Qi (or Duke Qi) returned due to illness,' referring to a noble returning home due to sickness.
    • 忽见一人来问 - Means 'suddenly saw a person come and ask,' introducing an unexpected encounter.
    • 君是孙回璞不 - Colloquial way of asking, 'Are you Sun Hui Pu?' using the particle 不 for confirmation.
    • 我是鬼耳 - Means 'I am just a ghost,' indicating the speaker's identity.
    • 魏太师有文书 - Means 'Wei Taishi has a document,' referring to a high-ranking official's written message.
    • 追君为记室 - Means 'to summon you as a secretary,' indicating the purpose of the document.
    • 郑国公魏徵署也 - Means 'signed by Duke Zheng Wei Zheng,' identifying the sender of the document.
    • 郑公不死 - Means 'Duke Zheng is not dead,' expressing surprise at the sender's status.
    • 太阳都录大监 - Means 'Grand Administrator of the Solar Capital,' indicating a high-ranking ghostly position.
    • 吾取过所度关待君 - Means 'I will go ahead to get the necessary documents for passing through the checkpoint and wait for you,' explaining the ghost's actions.
    • 夜梦前鬼来召 - Means 'I dreamed that the previous ghost came to summon me,' describing a dream encounter.
    • 引璞上高山 - Means 'led me up a high mountain,' setting the scene for the dream.
    • 山岭有大宫殿 - Means 'there was a large palace on the mountain,' describing the setting.
    • 此人修福，不得留之 - Means 'this person has done good deeds, so he cannot be kept here,' indicating the reason for the ghost's actions.
    • 推璞堕山 - Means 'pushed me off the mountain,' describing the ghost's action.
    • 惊悟 - Means 'awakened in shock,' describing the reaction upon waking.
    • 自以必死与家人诀别 - Means 'thinking he would surely die, he bid farewell to his family,' expressing the protagonist's fear.
    • 可六七日 - Means 'for about six or seven days,' indicating the duration of the event.
    • 临说云尔 - Means 'and said this at the time,' indicating the source of the story.

    Cultural and Historical Allusions

    • 齐王佑 - Refers to a noble, likely a prince or duke, named Qi Wuyou.
    • 魏太师 - Refers to a high-ranking official, likely a chancellor or prime minister, named Wei Taishi.
    • 郑国公魏徵 - Refers to Wei Zheng, a famous chancellor during the Tang Dynasty, posthumously honored as Duke Zheng.
    • 太阳都录大监 - Refers to a high-ranking official in the afterlife, possibly a metaphor for a powerful ghost or spirit.
    • 阌乡 - Refers to a place name, Wénxiāng, a county in Henan Province.
    • 滋水驿 - Refers to a place name, Zīshuǐ Yì, a驿站 (station) along the road.

    Syntactic Ambiguities

    • The phrase '后至十七年' sets the time frame but does not specify the era or dynasty.
    • The phrase '奉敕驰驿往' is elliptical but clearly indicates an official mission.
    • The phrase '齐王佑疾还' is elliptical but clearly indicates the reason for the return.
    • The phrase '忽见一人来问' is elliptical but clearly indicates the unexpected encounter.
    • The phrase '君是孙回璞不' is colloquial and uses a particle for confirmation.
    • The phrase '我是鬼耳' is straightforward but introduces the ghost's identity.
    • The phrase '魏太师有文书' is straightforward but introduces the document.
    • The phrase '追君为记室' is straightforward but indicates the purpose of the document.
    • The phrase '郑国公魏徵署也' is straightforward but identifies the sender.
    • The phrase '郑公不死' is straightforward but expresses surprise.
    • The phrase '太阳都录大监' is straightforward but indicates a high-ranking position.
    • The phrase '吾取过所度关待君' is elliptical but explains the ghost's actions.
    • The phrase '夜梦前鬼来召' is elliptical but describes the dream encounter.
    • The phrase '引璞上高山' is straightforward but sets the scene.
    • The phrase '山岭有大宫殿' is straightforward but describes the setting.
    • The phrase '此人修福，不得留之' is straightforward but explains the reason for the ghost's actions.
    • The phrase '推璞堕山' is straightforward but describes the ghost's action.
    • The phrase '惊悟' is straightforward but describes the reaction.
    • The phrase '自以必死与家人诀别' is elliptical but expresses the protagonist's fear.
    • The phrase '可六七日' is straightforward but indicates the duration.
    • The phrase '临说云尔' is straightforward but indicates the source of the story.

    Proper Nouns

    • 齐王佑 - A noble, likely a prince or duke, named Qi Wuyou.
    • 魏太师 - A high-ranking official, likely a chancellor or prime minister, named Wei Taishi.
    • 郑国公魏徵 - A famous chancellor during the Tang Dynasty, posthumously honored as Duke Zheng.
    • 太阳都录大监 - A high-ranking official in the afterlife, possibly a metaphor for a powerful ghost or spirit.
    • 阌乡 - A place name, Wénxiāng, a county in Henan Province.
    • 滋水驿 - A place name, Zīshuǐ Yì, a驿站 (station) along the road.

    In the seventeenth year, Pu was ordered to travel by express post. Prince Qi returned home due to illness and arrived at the East Xiangyi Station in Luoyang. Suddenly, a person came and asked, "Are you Sun Hui Pu?" Pu replied, "Yes, I am." The person then asked, "Why are you here?" Pu responded, "I am just a ghost. Wei Taishi has a document summoning you to be his secretary." The ghost showed Pu the document, which was signed by Duke Zheng Wei Zheng. Pu was shocked and said, "Duke Zheng is not dead. Why is he sending you to deliver the message?" The ghost replied, "He is dead now. He is the Grand Administrator of the Solar Capital. That is why he has sent me to summon you." Pu invited the ghost to eat with him, and the ghost was very pleased and thanked Pu. Pu then said, "I have not yet returned from my official mission. It would be inappropriate for Duke Zheng to summon me. I will return to the capital after I have completed my duties and then obey his command. Is that acceptable?" The ghost agreed. From then on, they traveled together during the day and shared the same accommodation at night. They eventually reached Wenxiang. The ghost said, "I will go ahead to get the necessary documents for passing through the checkpoint and wait for you." Pu passed through the checkpoint and exited the western gate, where he saw the ghost waiting outside. They continued their journey together and arrived at Zishui Station. The ghost said to Pu, "Wait until you have finished your duties and we will meet again. Do not eat spicy or strong-smelling foods." Pu agreed. After completing his duties, Pu learned that Duke Zheng had died. Upon checking the date of his death, it was found to be the same day as the arrival at Xiangyi Station. Pu thought he would surely die and bid farewell to his family. He also invited monks to recite scriptures, create statues, and write sutras for about six or seven days. One night, he dreamed that the previous ghost came to summon him. The ghost led Pu up a high mountain, where there was a large palace on the mountain. Upon entering, Pu saw many gentlemen who welcomed him and said, "This person has done good deeds and cannot be kept here. Let him go." They then pushed Pu off the mountain. Pu woke up in shock and has since remained unharmed. Pu later recounted this story himself. [38]

    户部尚书武昌公戴胃。素与舒州别驾沈裕善。胃以贞观七年薨。至八年八月。裕在州。梦其身行于京师义宁里南街。忽见胃。见胃着故弊衣。颜容甚悴。见裕悲喜。裕问曰。公生平修福。今者何为。答曰。吾生时。设奏杀一人。吾死后。他人杀一羊祭我。由此二事。辨答辛苦不可言。然亦势了矣。因谓裕曰。吾平生与君善。竟不能进君官位。深恨干怀。君今自得五品。文书已过天曹。相助欣庆。故以相报。言毕而寤。向人说之。冀梦有徵。其年冬。裕入京参选。为有铜罚不得官。又向人说所梦无验。九年春。裕将归江南。行至徐州。忽奉诏书。授裕五品。为务州治中（临兄为吏部侍郎闻之召裕问云尔）。 [39]

    In the year of the Zhenguan era, the Minister of Revenue, Duke Wuchang Dai Wei, had always been on good terms with Shen Yu, the Deputy Governor of Shuzhou. Dai Wei passed away in the seventh year of the Zhenguan era. By the eighth year, in the month of August, Shen Yu was in his post when he had a dream. In the dream, he saw himself walking on the southern street of Yining Li in the capital. Suddenly, he saw Dai Wei, who was wearing old and tattered clothes and looked very weary. Seeing Shen Yu, Dai Wei showed both sadness and joy. Shen Yu asked, "What are you doing now, given your past good deeds?" Dai Wei replied, "When I was alive, I arranged for someone to be executed. After my death, someone slaughtered a sheep to offer as a sacrifice to me. These two matters have made the process of explaining and defending myself extremely difficult to describe. However, the situation is now clear. I also regret that I was unable to advance your position, which deeply troubled me. Now that you have achieved a fifth-rank position, and the official documents have passed through the celestial bureaucracy, I share in your joy and celebration, so I am telling you this as a way of informing you." After speaking, he woke up. He told others about the dream, hoping it would come true. That winter, Shen Yu went to the capital for the selection process but was punished for a copper-related offense and thus not eligible for an official position. He told others that his dream had not come true. In the spring of the ninth year, Shen Yu was about to return to Jiangnan. On his way to Xuzhou, he suddenly received an imperial edict, appointing him as a fifth-rank official and deputy governor of Wuzhou. (His brother, who served as a Ministry of Personnel official, heard about it and summoned Shen Yu to ask him about it.) [39]

    陇西李大安。工部尚书大亮之兄也。武德年中。大亮为越州总管。大安自从京往省之。大亮遣奴婢数人从兄归。至谷州鹿桥。宿于逆旅。其奴有谋杀大安者。候其睡熟。以小剑刺大安项。洞之。刃着于床。奴因不拔而巡。大安惊觉。呼奴。其不叛奴婢至欲拔刃。大安曰。拔刃便死。可先取纸笔作书。奴仍告主人诉县。大安作书毕。县官亦至。困为拔刃。洗疮加药。大安遂绝。忽如梦者。见一物。长尺余。阔厚四五寸。形似猪肉。去地二尺许。从户入来至床前。其中有语曰。急还我肉。大安曰。我不食猪肉。何缘负汝。即闻户外有言曰错非也。此物即还。从户出去。大安仍见庭前有池水。清浅可爱。池西岸上。有金佛像。可高五寸。 [40]

    Relevant Explanation

    Explanation

    [40]

    Idiomatic Expressions

    • 武德年中 - Refers to the year during the reign of Emperor Gaozu of Tang (Li Yuan) in the early Tang Dynasty.
    • 越州总管 - A high-ranking military and administrative official position.
    • 从京往省之 - Means 'to go from the capital to visit him.'
    • 逆旅 - Refers to an inn or lodging place.
    • 洞之 - Means 'pierced through' or 'pierced to the bone.'
    • 刃着于床 - Means 'the blade stuck in the bed.'
    • 巡 - Means 'to walk around' or 'to move.'
    • 困为拔刃 - Means 'they were forced to pull out the blade.'
    • 洗疮加药 - Means 'to wash the wound and apply medicine.'
    • 忽如梦者 - Means 'suddenly as if in a dream.'
    • 长尺余 - Means 'about a foot long.'
    • 阔厚四五寸 - Means 'about four to five inches wide and thick.'
    • 形似猪肉 - Means 'shaped like pork.'
    • 去地二尺许 - Means 'about two feet above the ground.'
    • 急还我肉 - Means 'hurry and return my meat.'
    • 我不食猪肉 - Means 'I do not eat pork.'
    • 何缘负汝 - Means 'how could I owe you anything?'
    • 即闻户外有言曰错非也 - Means 'they heard someone outside say, 'That's wrong.''
    • 此物即还 - Means 'this thing will return.'
    • 从户出去 - Means 'went out through the door.'
    • 庭前有池水 - Means 'a pond of water in front of the courtyard.'
    • 清浅可爱 - Means 'clear and shallow, lovely.'
    • 池西岸上 - Means 'on the western bank of the pond.'
    • 金佛像 - Means 'a gold statue of Buddha.'
    • 可高五寸 - Means 'about five inches tall.'

    Cultural and Historical Allusions

    • 陇西李大安 - Refers to Li Da'an, a historical figure from the Tang Dynasty.
    • 工部尚书大亮之兄也 - Indicates that Li Da'an is the brother of Li Da-liang, who held the position of Minister of Works.
    • 武德年中 - Refers to the early years of the Tang Dynasty under Emperor Gaozu (Li Yuan).
    • 越州总管 - A high-ranking official position in the Tang Dynasty.
    • 从京往省之 - Describes the act of visiting a relative from the capital.
    • 逆旅 - A common term for an inn or lodging place in ancient China.
    • 洗疮加药 - Refers to the medical treatment of wounds, a common practice in ancient times.
    • 忽如梦者 - A poetic way of describing a sudden, dream-like experience.
    • 金佛像 - A gold statue of Buddha, indicating a religious or spiritual element in the story.

    Proper Nouns

    • 陇西李大安 - Li Da'an, a historical figure.
    • 工部尚书大亮 - Li Da-liang, the brother of Li Da'an, who held the position of Minister of Works.
    • 武德年中 - The early years of the Tang Dynasty under Emperor Gaozu (Li Yuan).
    • 越州总管 - A high-ranking official position in the Tang Dynasty.
    • 谷州鹿桥 - A specific location in ancient China.
    • 逆旅 - A common term for an inn or lodging place in ancient China.
    • 金佛像 - A gold statue of Buddha, indicating a religious or spiritual element in the story.

    During the reign of Emperor Gaozu in the early Tang Dynasty, Li Da'an, the elder brother of Li Da-liang, who was the Minister of Works, traveled from the capital to visit his brother. Li Da-liang sent several servants and maidservants to accompany their brother on his journey. They reached Luoyang and stayed at an inn in Guzhou's Lu Bridge. One of the servants had plotted to kill Li Da'an. When Li Da'an fell asleep, the servant waited until he was unconscious, then stabbed him in the neck with a small sword, piercing him deeply. The blade became stuck in the bed. The servant then walked around the room. Li Da'an suddenly woke up, calling out to the servant. The loyal servants, seeing the danger, tried to pull the blade out. Li Da'an said, "If you pull out the blade, I will die. Instead, get some paper and ink and write a letter." The servants then went to inform the innkeeper and reported the incident to the local authorities. After writing the letter, the county official arrived and helped to remove the blade, wash the wound, and apply medicine. Li Da'an then passed away. Suddenly, as if in a dream, he saw an object about a foot long, four to five inches wide and thick, shaped like pork, hovering about two feet above the ground and moving toward the bed. A voice from within the object said, "Hurry and return my meat." Li Da'an replied, "I do not eat pork. How could I owe you anything?" They heard someone outside say, "That's wrong." The object then returned and went out through the door. Li Da'an then saw a pond of water in front of the courtyard, clear and shallow, lovely to look at. On the western bank of the pond, there was a gold statue of Buddha, about five inches tall. [40]

    须臾渐大。而化为僧。被缘袈裟甚新净。谓大安曰。被伤耶。我今为汝将痛去。汝当平复。还家。念佛修善也。因以手摩大安项疮而去。大安志某形状。见僧背有红绘补袈裟。可方寸许。甚分明。既而大安觉遂苏。而疮亦不复痛。能起坐食。十数曰。京室子弟。迎至还家。家人故来视。大安为说被伤由状。及见僧像事。有一妇在旁闻说。因言。大安自之家初行也。安妻使婢诣像工为安造佛像。像成以渌书书衣。有一点朱。汗背上。当遣像工去之。不肯。今仍在。形状如郎君所说。大安因与专及家人共起观像。乃所见者也。其背朱点宛然补处。于是叹异。遂崇信佛法。大安妻夏候氏即郎。州刺史绚之妹。先为临说。后大安兄子适裕为大理卿。亦说云尔。 [41]

    Relevant Explanation

    Explanation

    [41]

    Idiomatic Expressions

    • 須臾 - Classical term meaning 'a short while' or 'soon.'
    • 漸大 - Means 'gradually grow' or 'gradually become larger.'
    • 化為僧 - Means 'transformed into a monk.'
    • 被緣袈裟 - Refers to a monk's robe with a border, indicating a new appearance.
    • 甚新淨 - Means 'very new and clean.'
    • 被傷耶 - A question meaning 'are you injured?'
    • 將痛去 - Means 'to take away the pain.'
    • 還家 - Means 'return home.'
    • 念佛修善 - Means 'chant Buddha's name and do good deeds.'
    • 因以手摩大安項 - Means 'then he touched the back of Dahan's neck.'
    • 項 - Refers to the back of the neck.
    • 項疮 - Means 'a wound on the neck.'
    • 去 - Means 'to leave' or 'to go away.'
    • 遂蘇 - Means 'then revived' or 'then woke up.'
    • 能起坐食 - Means 'was able to sit up and eat.'
    • 十数曰 - Means 'several days.'
    • 京室子弟 - Refers to the children of officials or nobles in the capital.
    • 迎至还家 - Means 'welcomed him back home.'
    • 家人故来视 - Means 'family members came to see him.'
    • 被伤由状 - Means 'the cause of the injury.'
    • 见僧像事 - Means 'seeing the image of the monk.'
    • 一点朱 - Means 'a dot of red.'
    • 汗背上 - Means 'on the back.'
    • 遣像工去之 - Means 'sent the sculptor to remove it.'
    • 不肯 - Means 'refused.'
    • 仍在 - Means 'still there.'
    • 形状如郎君所说 - Means 'the shape as described by the young master.'
    • 起观像 - Means 'to look at the statue.'
    • 乃所见者也 - Means 'it is indeed what he saw.'
    • 宛然补处 - Means 'clearly in the patch.'
    • 叹异 - Means 'to be amazed.'
    • 遂崇信佛法 - Means 'then he came to revere and believe in Buddhism.'
    • 大安妻夏候氏即郎 - Means 'Dahan's wife, Lady Xiahou, is the daughter of the governor.'
    • 州刺史绚之妹 - Means 'the sister of the governor Xuan.'
    • 先为临说 - Means 'previously told by the governor.'
    • 后大安兄子适裕为大理卿 - Means 'later, Dahan's nephew, Su, became the Chief Justice.'
    • 亦说云尔 - Means 'also said so.'

    Cultural and Historical Allusions

    • 僧 - Refers to a Buddhist monk.
    • 袈裟 - A traditional garment worn by Buddhist monks.
    • 念佛修善 - A common practice in Buddhism, involving chanting Buddha's name and performing good deeds.
    • 京室子弟 - Children of high-ranking officials or nobles in the capital city.
    • 州刺史 - A high-ranking official responsible for governing a state or province.
    • 大理卿 - The Chief Justice, a high-ranking judicial official.

    Proper Nouns

    • 大安 - The name of the main character.
    • 夏候氏 - The name of Dahan's wife.
    • 绚之 - The name of the governor.
    • 适裕 - The name of Dahan's nephew, who became the Chief Justice.

    In a short while, Dahan gradually grew larger and transformed into a monk. The monk wore a new and clean robe with a border. He asked Dahan, "Are you injured? I will take away your pain. You should recover, return home, and chant Buddha's name and do good deeds." Then he touched the back of Dahan's neck and left. Dahan remembered the monk's appearance, noting that the back of the monk's robe had a red patch, about the size of a square inch, very clearly visible. Soon after, Dahan revived and the wound on his neck no longer hurt. He was able to sit up and eat. Several days later, the children of officials in the capital welcomed him back home. Family members came to see him, and Dahan explained the cause of his injury and what he had seen of the monk. A woman who was present listened and said, "When Dahan first went out, his wife sent a maid to a sculptor to make a statue of Dahan. The statue was completed, and the robe was inscribed with blue writing. There was a dot of red on the back. When they sent the sculptor to remove it, he refused, and it is still there. The shape is as described by the young master." Dahan then went with his wife and family to view the statue, and it was indeed what he had seen. The red dot on the back of the robe was clearly in the patch. They were amazed, and Dahan then came to revere and believe in Buddhism. Dahan's wife, Lady Xiahou, is the daughter of the governor Xuan. Previously, she had told the governor. Later, Dahan's nephew, Su, became the Chief Justice and also said so. [41]

    武德中。以都水使者苏长。为邑州刺史。长将家口赴任。渡嘉陵江。中流风起船没。男女六十余人。一时溺死。唯有一妾。常读法花经。船在水入。妾头载经函。誓与俱没。妾独不没。随波泛滥。顷之着岸。逐经杨州。嫁为人妇。而逾笃信。岑说云。见在妾自言然。临因使其江上。船人说亦如此。
    　　河东董雄。少诚信佛道。蔬食数十年。贞观中。为大理丞。十四年春。坐为连季仙僮事。系御史台。干时上以为当大怒。使治书侍御史韦□等。鞠问甚急。防禁严密。禁者十数人。丈理丞李敬玄。司直王忻。并连此事。与雄同屋闭禁。皆被锁牢固。雄专念法华经普门品。数曰得三千遍。夜中独坐诵经。锁忽自解落地。雄惊告忻玄。忻玄共视锁。仍坚合在地。其钩亦俨然不壤。而钩锁相离数尺。玄等异之。雄恐责。告守者请锁开是。监察御史张敬一宿直命吏开锁。吏以烛之。见其钩锁不关而自然相离。甚怪异。因关锁之。用纸封縓其锁。书署封上。吏去。 [42]

    Relevant Explanation

    Explanation

    [42]

    Idiomatic Expressions

    • 武德中 - Refers to the early Tang Dynasty during the reign of Emperor Gaozu (618–626 AD).
    • 都水使者 - An official title, likely referring to a water management official.
    • 邑州刺史 - A local administrative position, equivalent to a prefect or governor.
    • 家口 - Refers to the family members accompanying the official.
    • 嘉陵江 - A river in Sichuan, China.
    • 法花经 - A mistranscription of the *Lotus Sutra* (法华经), a key Mahayana Buddhist text.
    • 普门品 - A chapter of the *Lotus Sutra* titled 'The Universal Gateway' (普门品), emphasizing the bodhisattva Avalokiteshvara.
    • 禁者十数人 - Refers to the guards or officials responsible for the prison.
    • 丈理丞 - An official title, likely a lower-ranking administrative officer.
    • 司直 - An official title, likely a judicial or supervisory role.
    • 监察御史 - A high-ranking official responsible for oversight and investigation.
    • 宿直 - Refers to the duty of staying overnight in the office.

    Cultural and Historical Allusions

    • 武德中 - The early years of the Tang Dynasty, a period of significant political and cultural change.
    • 苏长 - A historical figure, likely a Tang official, whose actions are described in the text.
    • 法花经 - The *Lotus Sutra* is a central text in Mahayana Buddhism, emphasizing compassion and the bodhisattva path.
    • 大理丞 - A high-ranking judicial official in the Tang Dynasty.
    • 御史台 - The central institution for oversight and investigation in the Tang Dynasty.
    • 贞观中 - The reign of Emperor Taizong (626–649 AD), a period of prosperity and reform.
    • 连季仙僮事 - A historical event or case involving a servant named '连季仙,' though the details are unclear.
    • 岑说 - A historical figure or source, likely a Tang official or historian.

    Proper Nouns

    • 武德 - The era name of Emperor Gaozu of Tang (618–626 AD).
    • 苏长 - A historical figure, likely a Tang official.
    • 邑州 - A specific location, likely a prefecture in the Tang Dynasty.
    • 嘉陵江 - A river in Sichuan, China.
    • 法花经 - A mistranscription of the *Lotus Sutra* (法华经).
    • 贞观 - The era name of Emperor Taizong of Tang (626–649 AD).
    • 连季仙 - A historical figure or servant involved in a case.
    • 岑说 - A historical figure or source, likely a Tang official or historian.
    • 张敬一 - A historical figure, likely a Tang official.

    During the reign of Emperor Gaozu in the early Tang Dynasty, Su Chang, the Water Management Commissioner, was appointed as the Prefect of Yi Prefecture. Su Chang took his family with him to assume his new post, crossing the Jialing River. In midstream, a sudden wind arose, causing the boat to capsize. More than sixty men and women drowned at once. Only one concubine survived; she had always recited the Lotus Sutra. When the boat sank, she placed the sutra on her head and vowed to drown with it. However, she alone did not drown, floating with the waves until she washed ashore. She eventually reached Yang Prefecture, where she married and became even more devout in her faith. According to Cen Shuo, she herself claimed that she had once been on the Jialing River, and the boatmen also recounted the same story.

    In Hedong, Dong Xiong was a young man who sincerely believed in Buddhism and Daoism. He had practiced vegetarianism for several decades. During the reign of Emperor Taizong, he served as a Deputy Justice of the Dali Court. In the spring of the fourteenth year of his reign, he was implicated in the case of Lian Jixian's servant and was imprisoned in the Office of Censorship. At the time, the emperor was expected to be furious, and the Chief Justice, Wei, was sent to interrogate him harshly. The prison was tightly guarded by more than ten officials. Deputy Justice Li Jingxuan and Inspector Wang Xin were also implicated in the case and were imprisoned in the same room as Dong Xiong, both of them locked securely. Dong Xiong devoted himself to reciting the Lotus Sutra and the Universal Gateway Chapter. In a few days, he had recited it three thousand times. One night, as he sat alone reciting the sutra, the lock suddenly fell off by itself. Surprised, Dong Xiong told Wang and Li about it. Wang and Li examined the lock and found that it was still firmly attached to the ground, with its hooks intact and undisturbed, yet the lock and hooks were several feet apart. They were amazed by this. Fearing punishment, Dong Xiong asked the guards to open the lock. The night watchman, Zhang Jingyi, ordered a clerk to open the lock. When the clerk inspected it with a candle, he saw that the hooks and lock were not connected but had naturally separated. He found this very strange and relocked it, sealing the lock with paper and writing his name on it. The clerk then left. [42]

    雄复坐诵经至五更。锁又解落而有声。如人开者。雄惧又告忻玄玄等谓。晓不宜请吏。既明共视之。钩锁各离在地。而锁犹合。其封署处。全因不动。钩甚定密。无可开理。玄自少长不信佛法。见妻读经。常谓曰。何乃为胡神。一媚而读此耶。及见雄此事。乃深叹寤曰。吾乃今知。佛之大圣。无有伦匹。诚不可思议也。时忻玄亦诵八菩萨名。满三万遍。尽曰锁自解落。视之锁状比雄不为异也。玄于是信服愧悔。既而三子俱雪。玄乃写法华经。书八菩萨像。归供养（临时病笃在家。玄来问疾。具说其事。临病愈摄职。问台内官吏。与玄说不殊。雄亦自说其事。而精厉弥笃雄今见在为盩厉令）。 [43]

    Relevant Explanation

    Explanation

    [43]

    Idiomatic Expressions

    • 復坐诵经至五更 - Refers to continuing to recite scriptures until the fifth watch of the night, indicating a prolonged period of devotion.
    • 锁又解落而有声 - Describes the lock falling and making a sound, suggesting a supernatural or miraculous event.
    • 如人开者 - A simile comparing the sound to that of someone opening something, adding a human-like quality to the event.
    • 晓不宜请吏 - Advises against summoning officials in the morning, possibly due to the nature of the event being considered private or spiritual.
    • 钩锁各离在地 - Describes the hooks and locks being separated on the ground, indicating a complete release.
    • 封署处全因不动 - States that the sealed and marked areas remain untouched, suggesting that the supernatural event did not affect these parts.
    • 钩甚定密，无可开理 - Describes the hooks as very secure and tightly fastened, making it impossible to open them by normal means.
    • 何乃为胡神 - A question expressing disbelief or skepticism about why one would worship foreign deities.
    • 一媚而读此耶 - A rhetorical question suggesting that reading such texts is a form of flattery or adoration.
    • 乃深叹寤 - Indicates a profound realization or awakening.
    • 佛之大圣，无有伦匹 - Describes the Buddha as the greatest saint, unparalleled in his greatness.
    • 诚不可思议也 - Expresses the idea that the Buddha's power is truly beyond comprehension.
    • 八菩萨名 - Refers to the names of the Eight Bodhisattvas, a common practice in Buddhist devotions.
    • 满三万遍 - Indicates that the recitation was completed three thousand times, emphasizing the dedication.
    • 尽曰锁自解落 - Describes the locks falling and releasing themselves completely.
    • 视之锁状比雄不为异也 - States that the locks appeared the same as those of the previous case, suggesting consistency in the supernatural events.
    • 玄于是信服愧悔 - Indicates that the person (Xuan) became convinced, felt ashamed, and repented.
    • 既而三子俱雪 - Describes the three sons being exonerated, possibly from some form of accusation or suspicion.
    • 写法华经，书八菩萨像 - Describes the act of writing the Lotus Sutra and drawing images of the Eight Bodhisattvas, indicating a deep commitment to Buddhist practice.
    • 归供养 - Refers to offering the written sutra and images for worship and veneration.
    • 临时病笃在家 - Describes a situation where someone is critically ill at home.
    • 具说其事 - Indicates that the person (Xuan) detailed the entire story.
    • 临病愈摄职 - Describes the person resuming their duties after recovering from illness.
    • 问台内官吏 - Refers to questioning officials within the government.
    • 与玄说不殊 - States that the accounts given by the officials are the same as those given by Xuan.
    • 精厉弥笃 - Describes a deepening of one's dedication and practice.
    • 雄今见在为盩厉令 - Indicates that the person (Xiong) is currently serving as the magistrate of Zhiuli.

    Cultural and Historical Allusions

    • 五更 - Refers to the fifth watch of the night, a traditional division of the night into five hours.
    • 锁 - Refers to locks, which were used for securing doors and other items in ancient China.
    • 佛 - Refers to the Buddha, the central figure in Buddhism.
    • 八菩萨 - Refers to the Eight Bodhisattvas, important figures in Mahayana Buddhism.
    • 法华经 - Refers to the Lotus Sutra, a significant Buddhist text.
    • 盩厉 - Refers to a place name, possibly a county or district in ancient China.

    Proper Nouns

    • 雄 - Refers to the person named Xiong.
    • 忻玄玄 - Refers to the person named Xin Xuanxuan.
    • 佛 - Refers to the Buddha.
    • 八菩萨 - Refers to the Eight Bodhisattvas.
    • 法华经 - Refers to the Lotus Sutra.
    • 盩厉 - Refers to a place name, possibly a county or district in ancient China.

    Xiong continued to recite scriptures until the fifth watch of the night. The lock fell and made a sound, as if someone had opened it. Xiong, fearing this, told Xin Xuanxuan and others that it was not advisable to summon officials in the morning. When morning came, they all examined it together. The hooks and locks were separated on the ground, but the lock remained closed. The sealed and marked areas remained untouched. The hooks were very secure and tightly fastened, making it impossible to open them by normal means. Xin Xuanxuan, who had never believed in Buddhism since his youth, often said to his wife when he saw her reading scriptures, "Why do you worship foreign deities? Are you flattering them by reading these texts?" Upon witnessing this event involving Xiong, he deeply realized and exclaimed, "I now understand that the Buddha is the greatest saint, unparalleled in his greatness. His power is truly beyond comprehension." At that time, Xin Xuanxuan also recited the names of the Eight Bodhisattvas three thousand times. When he finished, the locks fell and released themselves completely. Upon examination, the locks appeared the same as those of Xiong's case. Xin Xuanxuan thus became convinced, felt ashamed, and repented. Shortly after, the three sons were exonerated. Xin Xuanxuan then wrote the Lotus Sutra and drew images of the Eight Bodhisattvas, offering them for worship. (When he was critically ill at home, Xin Xuanxuan came to ask about his condition. He detailed the entire story. When he recovered and resumed his duties, he questioned the officials within the government. Their accounts matched those given by Xin Xuanxuan. Xiong also recounted the same story, and his dedication and practice became even more intense. Xiong is currently serving as the magistrate of Zhiuli.) [43]

    武德中。以都水使者苏长。为邑州刺史。苏长。将家口。赴任。渡嘉陵江。中流风起。船没。男女六十余人。一时溺死。唯有一妾。常读法华经。江水入。妾头载经函。誓与俱没。既船没。妾独不沈。随波泛滥。顷之着岸。逐载经函。而开视其经。了无湿汗。今尚存杨州。嫁为人妇。而愈笃信（岑令说云。见此妾自言然。临因使其江上。船人说亦云尔）。 [44]

    Relevant Explanation

    Explanation

    [44]

    Idiomatic Expressions

    • 中流风起 - A metaphorical expression meaning 'a sudden storm arose in midstream.'
    • 誓与俱没 - Means 'swore to go down with it.'
    • 了无湿汗 - Means 'completely dry and unsoiled.'

    Cultural and Historical Allusions

    • 武德 - The reign title of Emperor Gaozu of Tang (Li Yuan), who ruled from 618 to 626.
    • 法华经 - The Lotus Sutra, a significant Buddhist scripture.
    • 杨州 - Refers to Yangzhou, an important city in China, often associated with cultural and economic significance.

    Proper Nouns

    • 武德 - The reign title of Emperor Gaozu of Tang.
    • 都水使者 - Official title.
    • 邑州刺史 - Official title.
    • 苏长 - Name of the official.
    • 嘉陵江 - Name of the river.
    • 法华经 - Name of the Buddhist scripture.
    • 杨州 - Name of the city.
    • 岑令说 - Name of the recorder.

    During the Wu De period, Su Chang, the Inspector of Waterways, was appointed as the Prefect of Yi Prefecture. Su Chang, along with his family, set out to take office. As they crossed the Jialing River, a sudden storm arose in midstream, causing the boat to sink. More than sixty men and women drowned at the same time. Only one concubine, who often read the Lotus Sutra, managed to survive. The river water entered the boat, and the concubine placed the scripture case on her head, swearing to go down with it. After the boat sank, the concubine alone did not sink, drifting with the waves. Soon, she reached the shore, then carried the scripture case, and opened to look at the scripture, which was completely dry and unsoiled. Now, she still exists in Yangzhou, having married and become a wife, and has become even more devout.

    According to the account by Cen Lingshuo, who recorded this event, the concubine herself claimed it happened as described, and the boatmen on the river also confirmed the story. [44]

    中书令岑文本。江陵人。少信佛。常念诵法华经普门品。尝乘船于吴江。中流船坏。人尽死。文本在水中。闻有人言。但念佛。必不死也。如是三言之。既而随波涌出。已着北岸。遂免。后于江陵设斋。僧徒集其家。有一客僧独后去。谓文本曰。天下方乱。君幸不类其交。终逢太平致富贵也。言毕趋出。既而文本。自食碗中。得舍利二枚。后果如其言（文本自向临说云尔）。
    　　河南元大宝。贞观中为大理丞。一生不信因果之事。与同僚张散册。友善。常谓曰。二人若先死者。当来报因果之有无也。元以十一年。从驾幸洛阳病卒。散册在京未知。一夕梦。元来告曰。仆已死矣。生平不信善恶之有报。今乃定有不虚。故来报。君其勉修福业。张问其状。答曰。冥报因不可说。他亦不可道。但报君知定有耳。张寤。向同僚说之。二曰而丧问至。张勘其梦乃死之后曰也（张自向临说云尔也）。 [45]

    Relevant Explanation

    Explanation

    [45]

    Idiomatic Expressions

    • 中书令岑文本 - Cen Wenben, the Secretary of the Central Secretariat. This is a formal title indicating a high-ranking official.
    • 少信佛 - Young and believing in Buddhism. This phrase indicates a personal religious belief.
    • 常念诵法华经普门品 - Often recites the *Saddharma Puṇḍarīka Sūtra* (Lotus Sutra) and the *Universal Gate Chapter*. This is a specific Buddhist practice.
    • 乘船于吴江 - Traveling by boat on Wujiang River. This is a literal description of an action.
    • 中流船坏 - The boat broke in midstream. This describes a sudden and dangerous event.
    • 人尽死 - Everyone died. This is a stark statement of a tragic event.
    • 文本在水中 - Wenben was in the water. This describes the protagonist's situation.
    • 闻有人言 - Hearing someone say. This indicates an auditory experience.
    • 但念佛 - Just recite the Buddha's name. This is a common Buddhist practice.
    • 必不死也 - Will definitely not die. This is a promise or assurance.
    • 如是三言之 - Repeated three times. This emphasizes the repetition of the message.
    • 随波涌出 - Rising with the waves. This describes a physical action.
    • 已着北岸 - Already on the northern shore. This indicates a change in location.
    • 遂免 - Thus escaped. This summarizes the outcome.
    • 设斋 - Holding a vegetarian meal. This is a religious ritual.
    • 僧徒集其家 - Monks gathered at his home. This describes a gathering.
    • 有一客僧独后去 - There was a guest monk who left last. This introduces a character.
    • 天下方乱 - The world is in chaos. This is a statement about the political situation.
    • 君幸不类其交 - You are fortunate not to be like others. This is a compliment.
    • 终逢太平致富贵也 - Will eventually encounter peace and prosperity. This is a prediction.
    • 言毕趋出 - After finishing speaking, he hurriedly left. This describes an action.
    • 自食碗中 - In his own bowl. This is a specific location.
    • 得舍利二枚 - Found two relics. This is a significant discovery.
    • 后果如其言 - Later, as he said. This indicates a fulfillment of a prophecy.
    • 河南元大宝 - Yuan Dabao of Henan. This is a formal introduction of a person.
    • 贞观中为大理丞 - During the Zhenguan era, he served as the Chief Justice. This is a formal title and period.
    • 一生不信因果之事 - Never believed in the doctrine of cause and effect. This is a personal belief.
    • 与同僚张散册友善 - Was friends with his colleague Zhang Sance. This describes a relationship.
    • 常谓曰 - Often said. This indicates a recurring statement.
    • 二人若先死者 - If one of us dies first. This is a hypothetical scenario.
    • 当来报因果之有无也 - Will come back to report whether there is a cause and effect. This is a promise.
    • 元以十一年 - Yuan in the eleventh year. This specifies a time.
    • 从驾幸洛阳病卒 - Accompanied the emperor to Luoyang and died of illness. This describes a sequence of events.
    • 散册在京未知 - Zhang Sance in the capital did not know. This indicates a lack of information.
    • 一夕梦 - One night in a dream. This introduces a dream.
    • 元来告曰 - Yuan came to say. This describes a dream encounter.
    • 仆已死矣 - I have already died. This is a statement in the dream.
    • 生平不信善恶之有报 - Never believed in the reward for good and evil. This is a personal belief.
    • 今乃定有不虚 - Now it is definitely true. This is a realization.
    • 故来报 - So I came to report. This explains the purpose of the dream.
    • 君其勉修福业 - You should diligently cultivate good deeds. This is advice.
    • 张问其状 - Zhang asked about the situation. This describes an inquiry.
    • 答曰 - Replied. This indicates a response.
    • 冥报因不可说 - The cause and effect in the afterlife cannot be explained. This is a statement.
    • 他亦不可道 - Others cannot describe it. This reinforces the previous statement.
    • 但报君知定有耳 - Just let you know it definitely exists. This is a conclusion.
    • 张寤 - Zhang woke up. This describes the end of the dream.
    • 向同僚说之 - Told his colleagues. This describes an action.
    • 二曰而丧问至 - Two days later, the news of death arrived. This describes a sequence of events.
    • 张勘其梦乃死之后曰也 - Zhang verified that the dream occurred after Yuan's death. This is a verification.

    Cultural and Historical Allusions

    • 中书令 - A high-ranking official in the Tang Dynasty, responsible for drafting imperial edicts.
    • 岑文本 - A famous Tang Dynasty statesman and scholar.
    • 法华经普门品 - A chapter from the *Lotus Sutra*, a major Buddhist scripture.
    • 吴江 - A river in Jiangsu Province, China.
    • 贞观 - The reign title of Emperor Taizong of Tang, known for its prosperity and cultural achievements.
    • 大理丞 - The Chief Justice, a high-ranking official in the Tang Dynasty's judicial system.
    • 舍利 - Buddhist relics, often associated with the remains of the Buddha or other enlightened beings.

    Proper Nouns

    • 岑文本 - Cen Wenben, a famous Tang Dynasty statesman and scholar.
    • 江陵 - Jingling, a city in Hubei Province, China.
    • 法华经普门品 - The *Universal Gate Chapter* from the *Lotus Sutra*.
    • 吴江 - Wujiang River, a river in Jiangsu Province, China.
    • 贞观 - The reign title of Emperor Taizong of Tang.
    • 大理丞 - The Chief Justice, a high-ranking official in the Tang Dynasty's judicial system.

    Cen Wenben, the Secretary of the Central Secretariat, was from Jingling. When he was young, he believed in Buddhism and often recited the Saddharma Puṇḍarīka Sūtra (Lotus Sutra) and the Universal Gate Chapter. Once, he traveled by boat on the Wujiang River, but the boat broke in midstream, and everyone aboard died. Cen Wenben was in the water when he heard someone say, "Just recite the Buddha's name, and you will definitely not die." This was repeated three times. Soon after, he rose with the waves and found himself on the northern shore, thus escaping death. Later, in Jingling, he held a vegetarian meal, and monks gathered at his home. A guest monk left last and told Cen Wenben, "The world is in chaos, but you are fortunate not to be like others. You will eventually encounter peace and prosperity." After finishing speaking, the monk hurriedly left. Soon after, Cen Wenben found two relics in his own bowl. Later, as he said, everything turned out as predicted.

    Yuan Dabao of Henan served as the Chief Justice during the Zhenguan era. He never believed in the doctrine of cause and effect. He was friends with his colleague Zhang Sance, and they often said, "If one of us dies first, we will come back to report whether there is a cause and effect." Yuan died in the eleventh year, accompanying the emperor to Luoyang and dying of illness. Zhang Sance in the capital did not know this. One night, Zhang had a dream where Yuan came to say, "I have already died. I never believed in the reward for good and evil, but now it is definitely true. So I came to report. You should diligently cultivate good deeds." Zhang asked about the situation, and Yuan replied, "The cause and effect in the afterlife cannot be explained, and others cannot describe it. Just let you know it definitely exists." Zhang woke up and told his colleagues. Two days later, the news of Yuan's death arrived. Zhang verified that the dream occurred after Yuan's death. [45]

    东宫右监门兵曹参军郑师辨。年未弱冠时。异病死。三曰而苏。自言。有数人见收。将行入官府大门。见有囚百余人。皆重行北面立。凡为六行。其前行者。形状肥白。好衣服。如贵人。后行。渐瘦恶。或着枷锁。或但去巾带。皆行连袂。严兵守之。师辨至。配入第三行东头第三立。亦去巾带。连袂。辨忧惧。专心念佛。忽见生平相识僧来。入兵围行内。兵莫之止。因至辨所。谓曰。平生不修福。今忽至此如何。辨求哀请救。僧曰。吾今救汝得出。可持戒也。辨许诺。须臾。吏引入诸囚。至官前以次许问至辨。因见。向者僧。为官说其福业。官曰放之。僧因引辨出至门外。为授五戒。用瓶水灌其额。谓曰。曰西当活。 [46]

    Relevant Explanation

    Explanation

    [46]

    Idiomatic Expressions

    • 東宮右監門兵曹參軍 - A title referring to a military officer in the Eastern Palace (Imperial Palace), specifically the right gate military department. This is a specific administrative position within the imperial court.
    • 年未弱冠時 - Refers to a young man who has not yet reached the age of 20, traditionally the age of adulthood in ancient China.
    • 異病死 - Literally 'died of a strange illness,' but this could also be interpreted as 'died of an unusual or supernatural illness.'
    • 三曰而蘇 - '三曰' is likely a typo or corruption of '三日' (three days), meaning he revived after three days.
    • 有數人見收 - '有數人' means 'several people,' and '見收' means 'were taken' or 'were captured.' The phrase suggests that several people were apprehended.
    • 將行入官府大門 - '將行' means 'about to go,' and '官府大門' refers to the main gate of an official government building. This phrase describes the act of entering the main gate of an official building.
    • 見有囚百余人 - '見有' means 'there are,' and '囚' means 'prisoners.' This phrase indicates the presence of over a hundred prisoners.
    • 皆重行北面立 - '皆' means 'all,' '重行' means 'in rows,' and '北面立' means 'facing north.' This describes the prisoners standing in rows facing north.
    • 其前行者，形状肥白，好衣服，如贵人 - '其前行者' means 'those in the front,' '形状肥白' means 'fat and white in appearance,' '好衣服' means 'good clothes,' and '如贵人' means 'like nobles.' This describes the prisoners in the front row as well-dressed and resembling nobility.
