Suddenly, the boy saw a small city along the road, with four-sided gate towers that were very beautiful. The boy was surprised and asked, "When did this city appear?" The messenger scolded him and told him not to speak. He then led the boy to the north gate of the city and ordered him to enter. As soon as the boy entered, the city gate closed behind him. He saw no one, only an empty city, with the ground covered in ashes and broken coals, deep enough to cover his ankles. The boy suddenly cried out and ran toward the south gate, but it closed before he could reach it. He then ran to the east and north gates, but they closed as well. [59]

    未往则开。既至便阖。时村人出田。男女大小皆见。此儿在耕田中。口似啼声。四方驰走。皆相谓曰。此儿狂耶。且来如此。游戏不息。至曰食时。采桑者皆归。儿父问曰。见吾儿不。桑人答曰。在村南走戏。唤不肯来。父出村。遥见儿走。大呼其名。一声便住。城灰忽不见。见父而倒。号泣不言之。视其足。半胫已上。血肉燋干。其膝已下。洪烂如炙。抱归养疗髀肉如故。膝下遂为枯骨。邻里闻之。共视其走处。足迹通利。了无灰火。于是邑人。男女大小。皆持戒练行（有大德僧道慧本冀州人。为临言之。此其邻邑也）。 [60]

    Relevant Explanation

    Explanation

    [60]

    Idiomatic Expressions

    • 未往则开 - Conditional phrase meaning 'before going, it opens.'
    • 既至便阖 - Conditional phrase meaning 'after arriving, it closes.'
    • 游戏不息 - Means 'continuously playing,' emphasizing the boy's persistent activity.
    • 采桑者皆归 - Means 'those who picked mulberry leaves all returned.'
    • 口似啼声 - Means 'his mouth seemed like crying,' suggesting a strange or unusual cry.
    • 四方驰走 - Means 'running in all directions,' indicating the boy's frenzied movement.
    • 此儿狂耶 - Means 'Is this child crazy?' expressing the villagers' confusion.
    • 且来如此 - Means 'and came like this,' referring to the boy's unusual behavior.
    • 唤不肯来 - Means 'when called, he wouldn't come,' indicating the boy's reluctance to respond.
    • 血肉燋干 - Means 'blood and flesh burned dry,' describing the severe injury to the boy's leg.
    • 洪烂如炙 - Means 'swollen and rotten like roasted meat,' further describing the injury.
    • 髀肉如故 - Means 'the thigh flesh remained as before,' indicating that the injury did not affect the thigh.
    • 膝下遂为枯骨 - Means 'below the knee became dry bones,' describing the severe damage to the lower leg.
    • 通利 - Means 'smooth and unobstructed,' describing the footprints.
    • 灰火 - Means 'ashes and fire,' indicating the absence of these elements despite the injury.

    Cultural and Historical Allusions

    • 未往则开，既至便阖 - These phrases describe a supernatural phenomenon, possibly a portal or gate that opens and closes.
    • 男女大小皆见 - Indicates the presence of a community or village where the event takes place.
    • 采桑者 - Refers to people who pick mulberry leaves, a common agricultural activity in ancient China.
    • 桑人 - Refers to the people who pick mulberry leaves.
    • 临言 - Refers to a place or person, possibly a monk or a wise figure who explains the event.
    • 冀州 - Refers to a region in ancient China, indicating the geographical setting of the story.

    Proper Nouns

    • 临言 - A place or person, possibly a monk or a wise figure who explains the event.
    • 冀州 - A region in ancient China, indicating the geographical setting of the story.
    • 道慧 - A monk or a wise figure who provides context for the event.

    Before going, it opens. After arriving, it closes. At that time, the villagers went out to the fields. Both men and women, young and old, all saw it. This boy was in the middle of the cultivated land, his mouth seemed like a crying sound, and he ran in all directions. They all said to each other, "Is this child crazy? And came like this, continuously playing." By the time of mealtime, those who picked mulberry leaves all returned. The boy's father asked, "Have you seen my son?" The mulberry pickers replied, "He is running and playing in the south of the village. When called, he wouldn't come." The father went out of the village, saw the boy running from afar, and loudly called out his name. At once, the boy stopped. Suddenly, the ashes disappeared, and he saw his father and fell down, crying without speaking. Looking at his foot, above the calf, the blood and flesh were burned dry, and below the knee, it was swollen and rotten like roasted meat. He was carried back to be cared for, and the thigh flesh remained as before, but below the knee became dry bones. The neighbors heard about it and together looked at the place where he had run. The footprints were smooth and unobstructed, with no traces of ashes or fire. Thus, the people of the town, both men and women, young and old, all took up the practice of self-cultivation and discipline (there was a great monk named Dao Hui from Jizhou, who explained this to the people of Lin Yan; this was their neighboring town). [60]

    随大业中。京兆郡狱卒。失其姓名。酷暴诸囚。囚不堪困苦。而狱卒以为戏乐。后生一子。颐下肩上。有若肉枷。都无颈项。数岁不能行而死。
    　　随大业中。河南人妇。养姑不孝。姑两目盲。妇切蚯蚓为羹以食。姑怪其味。窃藏一脔。留以示儿。儿还见之。欲送妇诣县。未及。而雷震。失其妇。俄从空落。身衣如故。而易其头为白狗头。言语不异。问其故。答云。以不孝姑。为天神所罚。夫以送官。时乞食于市。后不知所在。
    　　杨州卞士瑜者父。在隋以平陈功。授仪同。性悭吝。尝雇人筑宅不还其价。作人求钱。卞父鞭之。皆怨曰。若实负我。死当与我作牛。须之。卞父死。其作人牛。孕产一黄犊。腰有黑文。横络周匝。如人腰带。左跨有白文。斜贯。大小正如象笏形。牛主呼之曰。卞公何为负我。犊至屈前两膝。以头叩地。瑜以钱十万赎之。牛主不许。死乃葬（甄陀为临说之）。 [61]

    Relevant Explanation

    Explanation

    [61]

    Idiomatic Expressions

    • 随大业中 - During the Dàyè period of the Sui dynasty.
    • 酷暴诸囚 - Treated the prisoners cruelly and brutally.
    • 以为戏乐 - Took it as a form of entertainment.
    • 颐下肩上，有若肉枷 - With a jaw and shoulders as if a meaty collar, describing a physical deformity.
    • 数岁不能行而死 - Lived for several years before dying unable to walk.
    • 养姑不孝 - Was disrespectful to her mother-in-law.
    • 切蚯蚓为羹以食 - Chopped earthworms into a soup to feed her.
    • 窃藏一脔 - Secretly saved a piece.
    • 留以示儿 - To show to her child.
    • 若实负我，死当与我作牛 - If you truly owe me, you will die and become my cow.
    • 孕产一黄犊 - Gave birth to a yellow calf.
    • 腰有黑文，横络周匝 - Had black markings around its waist, crisscrossing all around.
    • 左跨有白文，斜贯 - Had white markings on its left flank, crossing diagonally.
    • 大小正如象笏形 - The size and shape were exactly like a ministerial tablet.
    • 卞公何为负我 - Why did you, Sir Bian, owe me?
    • 犊至屈前两膝，以头叩地 - The calf knelt on its front legs and bowed its head to the ground.
    • 以钱十万赎之 - Redeemed it with ten thousand coins.
    • 牛主不许，死乃葬 - The owner refused, and it was buried after its death.

    Cultural and Historical Allusions

    • 随大业中 - Refers to the Dàyè period (605–618 CE) of the Sui dynasty, a time of significant construction projects and reforms.
    • 京兆郡 - Refers to Jingzhao Prefecture, an important administrative region in ancient China, often associated with the capital region.
    • 河南人妇 - Refers to a woman from Henan, a region historically significant in Chinese culture.
    • 姑两目盲 - Describes a mother-in-law who is blind, highlighting the importance of filial piety in Confucian culture.
    • 雷震 - Refers to a thunderstorm, often associated with divine punishment in Chinese mythology.
    • 甄陀为临说之 - Zhentuo of Lin recounted this, suggesting the story was told by a historical figure or storyteller.

    Syntactic Ambiguities

    • 酷暴诸囚，囚不堪困苦，而狱卒以为戏乐 - This clause describes the cruel treatment of prisoners and the guard's enjoyment of it, requiring careful translation to preserve the moral tone.
    • 颐下肩上，有若肉枷，都无颈项 - This phrase describes a physical deformity, requiring precise rendering to convey the imagery.
    • 若实负我，死当与我作牛 - This curse-like statement requires careful handling to maintain its gravity and meaning.
    • 孕产一黄犊，腰有黑文，横络周匝，如人腰带，左跨有白文，斜贯，大小正如象笏形 - This long, descriptive clause requires careful segmentation to ensure clarity in English.
    • 卞公何为负我，犊至屈前两膝，以头叩地 - This interaction between the owner and the calf requires a natural and respectful translation.

    Proper Nouns

    • 随大业中 - The Dàyè period of the Sui dynasty.
    • 京兆郡 - Jingzhao Prefecture.
    • 河南 - Henan region.
    • 甄陀为临说之 - Zhentuo of Lin, a historical figure or storyteller.

    During the Dàyè period of the Sui dynasty, a jailer in Jingzhao Prefecture, whose name has been lost to history, treated the prisoners cruelly and brutally, taking their suffering as a form of entertainment. Later, he gave birth to a son who had a jaw and shoulders as if a meaty collar, with no neck. The child lived for several years before dying unable to walk.

    During the Dàyè period, a woman from Henan was disrespectful to her mother-in-law, who was blind. The woman chopped earthworms into a soup to feed her. Her mother-in-law was surprised by the taste and secretly saved a piece to show to her child. When the child saw it, the woman wanted to take her to the county office. Before she could, a thunderstorm struck, and the woman disappeared. Soon after, she fell from the sky, still wearing the same clothes but with her head replaced by that of a white dog. Her speech remained unchanged. When asked why, she replied that she had been punished by the gods for her disrespect to her mother-in-law. She was taken to the county office, where she begged for food in the market. Her whereabouts after that are unknown.

    In the Yangzhou region, there was a man named Bian Shiyu, whose father had been granted the title of Yitong for his role in subduing Chen during the Sui dynasty. He was stingy and once hired workers to build his house without paying them. The workers sought payment, and Bian's father beat them. They all complained, saying, "If you truly owe us, you will die and become our cow." When Bian's father died, the workers' cow gave birth to a yellow calf with black markings around its waist, crisscrossing all around, like a human belt. On its left flank, there were white markings crossing diagonally, the size and shape exactly like a ministerial tablet. The owner of the cow called out, "Why did you, Sir Bian, owe me?" The calf knelt on its front legs and bowed its head to the ground. Bian Shiyu redeemed it with ten thousand coins, but the owner refused, and the calf was buried after its death (as recounted by Zhentuo of Lin). [61]

    京兆殷安仁。家富于财。素事慈门寺僧。以义宁初。有客寄其家。停止。客盗他驴皮遗安仁。至贞观三年。安仁遂见一人于路。谓安仁曰。官追汝。使人明曰至。汝当死也。安仁惧。径至慈门寺佛堂中。经宿不出。明食时。果有三骑。并出有数十人。皆兵杖入寺。遥见安仁呼出。安仁不应。而念佛诵经。愈精。鬼谓曰。昨曰不即取之。今其修福如此。何由可得。因相与去。留一人守者。谓安仁曰。君往曰杀驴。驴今诉君。故我等来摄君耳。终须共他对。不去何益。安仁遥答曰。往者盗自杀驴但以皮遗我耳。非我杀。何为见追。请君还为我语驴。我本故不杀汝。然今为汝追福。于汝有利。当舍我也。此人许诺。驴若不许。我明曰更来。如其许者。不复来矣。言毕而去。明曰遂不来。安仁于是为驴追福。而举家持戒菜食云（卢文励说云安仁今见在）。 [62]

    Relevant Explanation

    Explanation

    [62]

    Idiomatic Expressions

    • 素事 - Means 'always or regularly engaged in' or 'habitually involved in.'
    • 停止 - Means 'stayed' or 'resided.'
    • 经宿不出 - Means 'he stayed overnight without leaving.'
    • 愈精 - Means 'with even greater concentration.'
    • 持戒菜食 - Refers to the practice of keeping the precepts and eating a vegetarian diet, which is a common practice in Buddhism.

    Cultural and Historical Allusions

    • 京兆 - The region of Jingzhao, which was a significant administrative area in ancient China.
    • 义宁初 - Refers to the early years of the Yining era, a period during the reign of Emperor Yang of Sui.
    • 贞观三年 - The third year of the Zhenguan era, during the reign of Emperor Taizong of Tang.
    • 慈门寺 - A Buddhist temple, which was a place of worship and spiritual practice.
    • 鬼 - Refers to supernatural beings, often associated with the realm of the dead in Chinese folklore.
    • 驴 - Donkeys were common animals in ancient China, often used for labor and transportation.

    Proper Nouns

    • 京兆殷安仁 - The name of the main character, Anren from Jingzhao.
    • 慈门寺 - The name of the Buddhist temple.
    • 义宁初 - The early years of the Yining era.
    • 贞观三年 - The third year of the Zhenguan era.

    Anren from Jingzhao, a man of great wealth, had always been devoted to the Buddhist monks at Cimen Temple. In the early years of the Yining era, a guest stayed at his house. The guest stole a donkey's skin and left it for Anren. By the third year of the Zhenguan era, Anren suddenly met someone on the road who told him, "The officials are chasing you. They will come tomorrow. You will die." Anren was terrified and went straight to the Buddha hall at Cimen Temple, where he stayed overnight without leaving. The next day at mealtime, indeed, there were three riders, and several dozen people armed with weapons entered the temple. They saw Anren from afar and called him out. Anren did not respond but was reciting Buddhist scriptures with even greater concentration. The ghost said to him, "We didn't take you yesterday. Now you are practicing virtue so diligently. How can we achieve this?" So they all left, leaving one person to guard. The guard told Anren, "You killed the donkey the other day. The donkey is now complaining about you. So we have come to capture you. In the end, you must face him. It won't help if you don't go." Anren replied from afar, "The thief killed the donkey himself and only left the skin for me. I did not kill it. Why am I being pursued? Please tell the donkey for me. I originally did not kill you. But now I am praying for you. It will be beneficial for you. You should let me go." This person agreed. If the donkey does not agree, we will come back tomorrow. If it agrees, we will not come back. After saying this, they left. The next day, they did not come. Anren then prayed for the donkey, and the whole family kept the precepts and ate vegetarian food. (Lu Wenli said that Anren is still alive today.) [62]

    长安市里风俗。每岁元曰已后。递作饮食相邀号为传坐。东市笔工。赵士次当设之。有客先到。如厕。见其碓上。有童女。年可十三四。着青裾白衫。以级索系颈。属于碓柱。泣谓客曰。我是主人女也。往年未死时。盗父母钱一百。欲买脂粉。未及而死。其今在厨舍内西北角壁中。然虽未用。既已盗之。坐此得罪。今偿父母命。言毕。化为青羊白项。客惊告主人。主人问其形貌。乃其小女。死二年矣。送于厨壁取得钱。似久安处。于是送羊僧寺。合门不复食肉（卢父厉说）。 [63]

    Relevant Explanation

    Explanation

    [63]

    Idiomatic Expressions

    • 递作饮食相邀号为传坐 - Custom where people take turns hosting and inviting others to meals after the new year.
    • 化为青羊白项 - Transformation into a blue sheep with a white neck.

    Cultural and Historical Allusions

    • 长安市里风俗 - Customs of the marketplaces in Chang'an, the capital city during the Tang Dynasty.
    • 元曰 - The first day of the new year, a significant date in the Chinese calendar.
    • 碓 - A traditional tool used for grinding grain, often found in households.
    • 级索 - A rope or cord, commonly used in various household and agricultural tasks.
    • 厨舍 - The kitchen, a central part of the household.
    • 僧寺 - A Buddhist temple, indicating a place of religious significance.
    • 合门不复食肉 - A decision to stop eating meat, reflecting a shift in dietary habits, possibly influenced by the supernatural event.

    Proper Nouns

    • 长安 - Chang'an, the capital city during the Tang Dynasty.
    • 僧寺 - A Buddhist temple, indicating a place of religious significance.

    In the marketplaces of Chang'an, there are certain customs. After the first day of the new year, people take turns preparing food and inviting each other, calling it "transmitting the seat." One day, a guest arrived at the home of a pen worker named Zhao Shici. Before the guest went to the toilet, he saw a young girl, about thirteen or fourteen years old, wearing a blue skirt and a white shirt, with a rope tied around her neck and attached to a mortar post. She wept and told the guest, "I am the master's daughter. Last year, before I died, I stole one hundred coins from my parents to buy cosmetics, but I did not get to use them before I died. Now the money is in the northwest corner of the kitchen wall. Although it was not used, it was already stolen, and for this, I am punished. Now I am paying back my parents' debt." After she finished speaking, she transformed into a blue sheep with a white neck. The guest was startled and told the master. The master asked about her appearance and recognized her as his little daughter, who had died two years ago. They sent the sheep to the kitchen wall and retrieved the money, which seemed to have been in a safe place for a long time. They then sent the sheep to a Buddhist temple, and the entire household stopped eating meat. [63]

    京兆潘果。年未弱冠。以武德中。任都水小吏。下归与里中少年数人。出田游戏。过于冢间。见一羊。为人所遗。独立食草。果因与里。中年少捉之。将以归家。其羊中路而鸣。果惧其主闻。乃拔去其羊舌。乃无声。于是夜烹食之。后一年。果舌渐小。遂销尽。陈牒解职。富平县尉郑余庆。疑其诈也。开口验之。乃全无舌。看本处。才如豆许不尽。怪问之。果尽以实答其状。县官教为羊追福。果乃受五戒。大修福。后一年舌渐生。寻平复如旧。诣县自陈。县官用为里正。余庆贞观十八年为监察御吏。自向临说云尔。 [64]

    Relevant Explanation

    Explanation

    [64]

    Idiomatic Expressions

    • 未弱冠 - Classical term meaning 'before the age of twenty,' referring to someone who has not yet reached adulthood.
    • 都水小吏 - Refers to a minor official in charge of water management or related duties.
    • 下归与里中少年数人 - Literally 'descended and gathered with several young men from the village,' indicating a casual gathering of friends.
    • 独立食草 - Describes a sheep eating grass alone, implying it was abandoned or lost.
    • 拔去其羊舌 - Means 'to pull out the sheep's tongue,' a brutal act to prevent it from making noise.
    • 遂销尽 - Means 'eventually disappeared' or 'gradually vanished,' referring to the gradual loss of Pan Guo's tongue.
    • 陈牒解职 - Means 'to submit a petition to resign from office,' indicating Pan Guo's attempt to leave his position.
    • 疑其诈也 - Means 'suspected him of being dishonest,' referring to Vang Yuqing's doubt about Pan Guo's story.
    • 开口验之 - Means 'opened his mouth to check,' referring to Vang Yuqing's inspection of Pan Guo's tongue.
    • 才如豆许不尽 - Means 'only as big as a bean, not fully restored,' describing the small size of Pan Guo's regenerating tongue.
    • 诣县自陈 - Means 'went to the county office to report,' indicating Pan Guo's return to the authorities after his recovery.
    • 里正 - Refers to a village head or local official.
    • 受五戒 - Means 'to take the five precepts,' referring to a Buddhist practice of moral discipline.
    • 大修福 - Means 'to perform many good deeds,' indicating Pan Guo's efforts to improve his karma.
    • 寻平复如旧 - Means 'soon returned to normal,' describing the full recovery of Pan Guo's tongue.
    • 贞观十八年 - Refers to the eighteenth year of the Zhenguan era, a period during the Tang Dynasty.
    • 自向临说云尔 - Means 'he told me himself like this,' indicating that the story was recounted by Zheng Yuqing.

    Cultural and Historical Allusions

    • 武德 - The reign title of Emperor Gaozu of Tang, marking the early Tang Dynasty.
    • 都水 - Refers to the Department of Water Management, an administrative body in ancient China responsible for water-related projects.
    • 里中 - Refers to the village or local community, indicating a rural setting.
    • 五戒 - The five precepts are fundamental Buddhist ethical guidelines, including refraining from killing, stealing, sexual misconduct, lying, and intoxication.
    • 贞观 - The reign title of Emperor Taizong of Tang, marking a golden age of prosperity and cultural flourishing.
    • 监察御史 - Refers to a high-ranking official responsible for oversight and investigation, indicating Zheng Yuqing's position in the Tang government.
    • 临 - Refers to the city of Linzi, where the story took place, though the exact location is uncertain.

    Proper Nouns

    • 潘果 (Pan Guo) - The protagonist of the story.
    • 武德 - The reign title of Emperor Gaozu of Tang.
    • 都水 - The Department of Water Management.
    • 里中 - The village or local community.
    • 五戒 - The five precepts in Buddhism.
    • 贞观 - The reign title of Emperor Taizong of Tang.
    • 监察御史 (Zheng Yuqing) - The high-ranking official who recounted the story.
    • 临 (Linzi) - The city where the story took place.

    A Man from Jingzhao Named Pan Guo

    Pan Guo, a young man from Jingzhao, was not yet twenty years old. During the reign of Emperor Gaozu (Wude era), he served as a minor official in charge of water management. One day, he descended with several young men from the village to play in the fields. Passing by a tomb, they saw a sheep, abandoned by its owner, eating grass alone. Pan Guo and the young men caught the sheep, intending to take it home. The sheep began to bleat on the way, and fearing that its owner might hear, Pan Guo pulled out its tongue, silencing it. That night, they cooked and ate the sheep.

    A year later, Pan Guo's tongue began to shrink and eventually disappeared entirely. He submitted a petition to resign from his position. The county magistrate, Zheng Yuqing, suspected him of being dishonest. To test him, Zheng opened his mouth to inspect, finding that Pan Guo had no tongue. However, a small part of it, the size of a bean, remained. Zheng found this strange and questioned Pan Guo. Pan Guo told him everything that had happened. The county magistrate advised him to perform good deeds for the sheep's sake. Pan Guo then took the five precepts and performed many good deeds. A year later, his tongue gradually grew back and soon returned to normal. He went to the county office to report his recovery. The county magistrate appointed him as the village head. Zheng Yuqing, who was a censor during the eighteenth year of the Zhenguan era, recounted this story to me in Linzi. [64]

    隋大业中。洛阳人。姓王持五戒。时言未然之事。闾里敬信之。一旦忽谓人曰。今曰当有人。与我一头驴。至曰午。果有人牵驴一头送来。涕泣说言。早丧父。其母寡。养一男一女。女嫁而母亡。亦十许年矣。寒食曰。妹来归家。家有驴数年。洛下俗。以寒食曰。持酒食祭墓。此人乘驴而往。墓在伊水东。欲度伊水。驴不肯度。鞭其头面。被伤流血。既至墓所。放驴而祭。俄失其驴。还在本处。是曰妹独在兄家。忽见母入来。头面血流。形容毁瘁。号泣告女曰。我生避汝兄。 [65]

    Relevant Explanation

    Explanation

    [65]

    Idiomatic Expressions

    • 隋大业中 - Refers to the period of the Sui Dynasty, specifically the reign of Emperor Yang (Yang Jian), known as the 'Great Prosperity' era.
    • 洛阳人 - Refers to a person from Luoyang, a significant city during the Sui and Tang dynasties.
    • 持五戒 - Refers to observing the Five Precepts, which are fundamental ethical guidelines in Buddhism.
    • 闾里敬信之 - Means that the local people respected and trusted him.
    • 今曰当有人 - A colloquial expression meaning 'today someone will come.'
    • 至曰午 - Means 'by noon.'
    • 果有人牵驴一头送来 - Means 'indeed, someone brought a donkey.'
    • 涕泣说言 - Means 'crying and saying.'
    • 早丧父 - Means 'lost his father early.'
    • 其母寡 - Means 'his mother was widowed.'
    • 养一男一女 - Means 'raised one son and one daughter.'
    • 女嫁而母亡 - Means 'the daughter married and then her mother died.'
    • 寒食曰 - Refers to the Cold Food Festival, a traditional Chinese holiday.
    • 妹来归家 - Means 'the younger sister came back home.'
    • 家有驴数年 - Means 'they had a donkey for several years.'
    • 洛下俗 - Refers to the customs of the Luoyang region.
    • 以寒食曰 - Means 'on the Cold Food Festival day.'
    • 持酒食祭墓 - Means 'took wine and food to offer at the grave.'
    • 墓在伊水东 - Means 'the grave was east of the Yi River.'
    • 欲度伊水 - Means 'wanted to cross the Yi River.'
    • 驴不肯度 - Means 'the donkey refused to cross.'
    • 鞭其头面 - Means 'whipped its head and face.'
    • 被伤流血 - Means 'was injured and bleeding.'
    • 既至墓所 - Means 'having arrived at the grave.'
    • 放驴而祭 - Means 'released the donkey and offered the sacrifice.'
    • 俄失其驴 - Means 'soon lost the donkey.'
    • 还在本处 - Means 'it was still in the same place.'
    • 是曰妹独在兄家 - Means 'on that day, the younger sister was alone in her brother's house.'
    • 忽见母入来 - Means 'suddenly saw her mother enter.'
    • 头面血流 - Means 'with blood streaming from her head and face.'
    • 形容毁瘁 - Means 'looking emaciated and worn out.'
    • 号泣告女 - Means 'cried and told her daughter.'
    • 我生避汝兄 - Means 'I avoided your brother in life.'

    Cultural and Historical Allusions

    • 隋大业中 - The Sui Dynasty, particularly the reign of Emperor Yang, was a period of significant cultural and political change.
    • 洛阳 - Luoyang was a major cultural and political center during the Sui and Tang dynasties.
    • 持五戒 - Observing the Five Precepts is a fundamental practice in Buddhism, emphasizing moral conduct.
    • 寒食曰 - The Cold Food Festival is a traditional Chinese holiday where people abstain from cooking and eat cold food, often visiting ancestors' graves.
    • 伊水 - The Yi River is a significant river in Henan Province, near Luoyang.

    Proper Nouns

    • 隋大业中 - The reign period of Emperor Yang of the Sui Dynasty.
    • 洛阳 - The city of Luoyang.
    • 王 - The name of the main character.
    • 寒食曰 - The Cold Food Festival.
    • 伊水 - The Yi River.

    During the reign of Emperor Yang of the Sui Dynasty, there was a man from Luoyang named Wang who observed the Five Precepts. He often spoke of things that had not yet happened, and the local people respected and trusted him. One day, he suddenly told someone, "Today, someone will come and give me a donkey. By noon, indeed, someone brought a donkey." The person cried and said, "I lost my father early, and my mother was widowed. She raised one son and one daughter. My sister married, and then my mother died, about ten years ago. On the Cold Food Festival, my sister came back home. We had a donkey for several years. In the customs of Luoyang, on the Cold Food Festival, we take wine and food to offer at the grave. This person took the donkey to the grave, which was east of the Yi River. He wanted to cross the Yi River, but the donkey refused to cross. He whipped its head and face, and it was injured and bleeding. Having arrived at the grave, he released the donkey and offered the sacrifice. Soon, he lost the donkey, and it was still in the same place. On that day, my sister was alone in her brother's house. Suddenly, she saw her mother enter, with blood streaming from her head and face, looking emaciated and worn out. She cried and told her daughter, 'I avoided your brother in life.'" [65]

    送米五升与汝。坐此得罪报。受驴身。偿汝兄五年矣。今曰欲度伊水。水深畏之。汝兄以鞭捶我。头面尽破。仍许还家。更苦打我。我走来告汝。吾今偿债垂毕。可太非理相苦也。言讫走出。寻之不见。女记其伤状处。既而兄还。女先观驴头面伤破状。女抱以号泣。兄怪问之。女以状告。兄亦言初不肯度。及失还得之状同。于是兄妹。抱持恸哭。驴亦涕泪交流。不食水草。兄妹跪请。若是母者。愿为食草。驴即为食草。既而复止。兄妹莫如之何。遂备粟豆送五戒处。乃复饮食。后驴死。妹收葬焉。 [66]

    Relevant Explanation

    Explanation

    [66]

    Idiomatic Expressions

    • 送米五升与汝 - Literally means 'to send five sheng of rice to you,' indicating a gift or provision.
    • 坐此得罪报 - Means 'because of this, I have been punished,' suggesting a consequence for an action.
    • 受驴身 - Means 'to take the form of a donkey,' indicating a transformation or punishment.
    • 偿汝兄五年矣 - Means 'I have been repaying your brother for five years,' indicating a long-term obligation.
    • 欲度伊水 - Means 'to cross the Yi River,' indicating a specific action or desire.
    • 水深畏之 - Means 'fear the deep water,' indicating a concern or hesitation.
    • 汝兄以鞭捶我 - Means 'your brother beat me with a whip,' indicating physical punishment.
    • 头面尽破 - Means 'my head and face were all broken,' indicating severe injury.
    • 仍许还家 - Means 'still allowed to return home,' indicating a temporary reprieve.
    • 更苦打我 - Means 'beat me even harder,' indicating increased severity of punishment.
    • 我走来告汝 - Means 'I came to tell you,' indicating a communication of distress.
    • 吾今偿债垂毕 - Means 'I am about to finish paying off my debt,' indicating the end of a long-term obligation.
    • 可太非理相苦也 - Means 'you can't be too unreasonable in your suffering,' indicating a plea for mercy.
    • 言讫走出 - Means 'after speaking, he walked out,' indicating a departure.
    • 寻之不见 - Means 'searching for him, he was not found,' indicating a disappearance.
    • 女记其伤状处 - Means 'the girl remembered the place of his injuries,' indicating a memory of a specific event.
    • 既而兄还 - Means 'afterward, the brother returned,' indicating a return.
    • 女先观驴头面伤破状 - Means 'the girl first observed the donkey's head and face injuries,' indicating a detailed observation.
    • 女抱以号泣 - Means 'the girl held him and wept,' indicating emotional distress.
    • 兄怪问之 - Means 'the brother asked her in surprise,' indicating a reaction to the girl's behavior.
    • 女以状告 - Means 'the girl reported the situation,' indicating a detailed account.
    • 兄亦言初不肯度 - Means 'the brother also said he initially refused to cross,' indicating a change in behavior.
    • 及失还得之状同 - Means 'and the state when he was lost and found was the same,' indicating consistency in the situation.
    • 于是兄妹，抱持恸哭 - Means 'thus the brother and sister, holding each other, wept bitterly,' indicating a shared emotional response.
    • 驴亦涕泪交流 - Means 'the donkey also shed tears,' indicating an emotional response from the donkey.
    • 不食水草 - Means 'would not eat water or grass,' indicating a refusal to eat.
    • 兄妹跪请 - Means 'the brother and sister knelt and pleaded,' indicating a request for something.
    • 若是母者，愿为食草 - Means 'if you are our mother, we wish for you to eat grass,' indicating a plea for the donkey to eat.
    • 驴即为食草 - Means 'the donkey then ate grass,' indicating a response to the plea.
    • 既而复止 - Means 'afterward, he stopped again,' indicating a change in behavior.
    • 兄妹莫如之何 - Means 'the brother and sister did not know what to do,' indicating a state of helplessness.
    • 遂备粟豆送五戒处 - Means 'they then prepared millet and beans and sent them to the place of the five precepts,' indicating a specific action.
    • 乃复饮食 - Means 'then resumed eating and drinking,' indicating a return to normal behavior.
    • 后驴死 - Means 'later, the donkey died,' indicating the end of the donkey's life.
    • 妹收葬焉 - Means 'the sister collected and buried him,' indicating a burial.

    Cultural and Historical Allusions

    • 五戒 - Refers to the Five Precepts in Buddhism, which are ethical guidelines for lay followers.
    • 粟豆 - Refers to millet and beans, which are staple foods in Chinese agriculture.
    • 伊水 - Refers to the Yi River, a river in Henan Province, China.

    Proper Nouns

    • 五戒 - The Five Precepts in Buddhism.
    • 粟豆 - Millet and beans.
    • 伊水 - The Yi River.

    A woman was sent five sheng of rice. Because of this, she was punished. She took the form of a donkey. She had been repaying her brother for five years. Now she wanted to cross the Yi River, but feared the deep water. Her brother beat her with a whip, breaking her head and face. He still allowed her to return home, but beat her even harder. She came to tell you, saying, "I am about to finish paying off my debt. You can't be too unreasonable in your suffering." After speaking, she walked out. Searching for her, she was not found. The girl remembered the place of his injuries. Afterward, the brother returned. The girl first observed the donkey's head and face injuries. She held him and wept. The brother asked her in surprise. The girl reported the situation. The brother also said he initially refused to cross. And the state when he was lost and found was the same. Thus, the brother and sister, holding each other, wept bitterly. The donkey also shed tears and would not eat water or grass. The brother and sister knelt and pleaded, "If you are our mother, we wish for you to eat grass." The donkey then ate grass, but afterward stopped again. The brother and sister did not know what to do. They then prepared millet and beans and sent them to the place of the five precepts. The donkey then resumed eating and drinking. Later, the donkey died. The sister collected and buried him. [66]

    随有康抱者。江南搢绅之士。少有学行。大业九年。杨玄武作乱。其兄受玄武官。抱缘兄坐。当死而潜避。自匿于京师。至十年。抱因入秘书省。寻觅旧识。是时炀帝不在。皇城诸门皆闭。唯开安上一门。出入皆由之。抱适入门。遇见一旧相识人姓曾。曾亦江南人。时判留守事。见抱与语。问其所安。抱知其相悉。亦以情告。既别而入。曾氏使人逐捕之。抱入秘书。逐者捕以告官。时王邵为秘书少监。先与抱故识。不欲罪之。乃迎谓捕者。我早识。康抱知其旨。应声答曰。实南丁避役耳。邵驱出令去。捕者还报曾。 [67]

    Relevant Explanation

    Explanation

    [67]

    Idiomatic Expressions

    • 随有康抱者 - Biographical introduction to Kang Bao, meaning 'Kang Bao, a person from the region of Wei.'
    • 江南搢绅之士 - Refers to a scholar-official from the south, '搢绅' is a term for high-ranking officials or scholars.
    • 少有学行 - Means 'had a young age with scholarly conduct,' indicating his early academic achievements.
    • 大业九年 - Refers to the year 613 CE, during the reign of Emperor Yang of Sui.
    • 杨玄武作乱 - Indicates a rebellion led by Yang Xuanwu, a specific historical event.
    • 其兄受玄武官 - Means 'his brother received an official position from Yang Xuanwu,' implying a connection to the rebellion.
    • 抱缘兄坐 - Means 'Kang Bao was implicated due to his brother's involvement.'
    • 当死而潜避 - Means 'should have been executed but fled secretly.'
    • 自匿于京师 - Means 'hid himself in the capital city.'
    • 至十年 - Refers to the year 614 CE.
    • 抱因入秘书省 - Means 'Kang Bao took advantage of the opportunity to enter the Secretariat (secretary office).'
    • 寻觅旧识 - Means 'to search for old acquaintances.'
    • 是时炀帝不在 - Means 'at that time, Emperor Yang was not present.'
    • 皇城诸门皆闭 - Means 'all the gates of the imperial city were closed.'
    • 唯开安上一门 - Means 'only the Anshang gate was open.'
    • 出入皆由之 - Means 'all entry and exit were through it.'
    • 适入门 - Means 'just as he was entering the gate.'
    • 遇见一旧相识人姓曾 - Means 'met an old acquaintance named Zeng.'
    • 曾亦江南人 - Means 'Zeng was also from the south.'
    • 时判留守事 - Means 'at that time, Zeng was in charge of the留守 (留守 means 'to stay behind' or 'to remain in charge when the emperor leaves).'
    • 见抱与语 - Means 'saw Kang Bao and spoke with him.'
    • 问其所安 - Means 'asked about his well-being.'
    • 知其相悉 - Means 'knew that they were familiar with each other.'
    • 以情告 - Means 'told him the truth.'
    • 既别而入 - Means 'after parting and entering.'
    • 曾氏使人逐捕之 - Means 'Zeng sent someone to chase and capture him.'
    • 入秘书 - Means 'entered the Secretariat.'
    • 逐者捕以告官 - Means 'the pursuers captured him and reported to the authorities.'
    • 时王邵为秘书少监 - Means 'at that time, Wang Shao was the Deputy Secretary of the Secretariat.'
    • 先与抱故识 - Means 'had previously been acquainted with Kang Bao.'
    • 不欲罪之 - Means 'did not want to punish him.'
    • 乃迎谓捕者 - Means 'then greeted the pursuers and said.'
    • 我早识康抱 - Means 'I knew Kang Bao long ago.'
    • 知其旨 - Means 'understood his intention.'
    • 应声答曰 - Means 'responded immediately and said.'
    • 实南丁避役耳 - Means 'in fact, he was a southern conscript avoiding military service.'
    • 驱出令去 - Means 'dismissed him and ordered him to leave.'
    • 捕者还报曾 - Means 'the pursuers returned to report to Zeng.'

    Cultural and Historical Allusions

    • 随 - Refers to the region of Wei, which was part of the Sui Dynasty.
    • 江南 - Refers to the southern region, often associated with the Yangtze River basin.
    • 搢绅之士 - Refers to high-ranking officials or scholars, often used to denote the elite class.
    • 大业 - The era name of Emperor Yang of Sui, indicating the time period.
    • 杨玄武 - A historical figure who led a rebellion during the Sui Dynasty.
    • 秘书省 - The Secretariat, an important government office responsible for managing official documents and records.
    • 安上 - The name of a specific gate in the imperial city.
    • 皇城 - The imperial city, the seat of the imperial government.
    • 留守 - A term for a high-ranking official who remains in charge of the capital when the emperor is away.
    • 王邵 - A historical figure who held a high position in the Sui Dynasty.
    • 南丁 - Refers to conscripts from the southern regions.

    Proper Nouns

    • 随 - The region of Wei.
    • 江南 - The southern region.
    • 大业 - The era name of Emperor Yang of Sui.
    • 杨玄武 - A historical figure who led a rebellion.
    • 秘书省 - The Secretariat.
    • 安上 - The name of a specific gate.
    • 皇城 - The imperial city.
    • 留守 - A high-ranking official.
    • 王邵 - A historical figure.
    • 南丁 - Conscripts from the southern regions.

    Kang Bao, a scholar-official from the south, was young and had a distinguished scholarly reputation. In the ninth year of the Daji era (613 CE), Yang Xuanwu led a rebellion. Kang Bao's brother received an official position from Yang Xuanwu, and as a result, Kang Bao was implicated and should have been executed but fled secretly, hiding himself in the capital city. By the tenth year of the Daji era (614 CE), Kang Bao took advantage of the opportunity to enter the Secretariat, searching for old acquaintances. At that time, Emperor Yang was not present, and all the gates of the imperial city were closed, except for the Anshang gate, which was the only one open for entry and exit. Just as Kang Bao was entering the gate, he met an old acquaintance named Zeng, who was also from the south and was in charge of the留守 (remaining in charge when the emperor leaves). Zeng saw Kang Bao and spoke with him, asking about his well-being. Kang Bao recognized that they were familiar with each other and told him the truth. After parting and entering, Zeng sent someone to chase and capture him. Kang Bao entered the Secretariat, and the pursuers captured him and reported to the authorities. At that time, Wang Shao was the Deputy Secretary of the Secretariat and had previously been acquainted with Kang Bao. He did not want to punish him and greeted the pursuers, saying, "I knew Kang Bao long ago." Kang Bao understood his intention and responded immediately, saying, "In fact, I am a southern conscript avoiding military service." Wang Shao dismissed him and ordered him to leave. The pursuers returned to report to Zeng. [67]

    曾又于安上门邀捕擒之。抱知不免。谓曾曰。我诚负官。死自我分。然无负于卿。卿与我故知。不能相济。曷反如此。若死者有知。必当相报。抱寻伏法。后数曰。曾宅在太平里。将入留守。由善和里于西门内。忽见抱乘马。衣冠甚鲜。二青衣从后。谓曾。我命亦将尽。然尚可三年。由卿任我。我今任太山主簿。已请天曹报杀卿。曾叩头谢罪。请为追福。抱许而忽不见。数曰又遇抱。于此谓曾曰。我终杀卿。放卿七曰修福。过此当先取卿头将去。若不信者。卿死面当在背。曾惧还家。修如期而死。回于背。果如所言（康亲识人说云尔也）。 [68]

    Relevant Explanation

    Explanation

    [68]

    Idiomatic Expressions

    • 抱知不免 - Phrase indicating that the person (抱) realizes he cannot escape his fate.
    • 我诚负官 - Statement acknowledging guilt or wrongdoing towards the government.
    • 死自我分 - Phrase expressing that death is his own due or fate.
    • 然无负于卿 - Statement indicating that he does not feel guilty towards the person addressed (卿).
    • 不能相济 - Phrase indicating that the person addressed could not help him.
    • 曷反如此 - Rhetorical question expressing disbelief or disappointment.
    • 若死者有知 - Phrase suggesting that if the dead have consciousness or awareness.
    • 必当相报 - Statement indicating that there will be retribution or repayment.
    • 抱寻伏法 - Phrase indicating that the person (抱) eventually surrenders to the law.
    • 将入留守 - Phrase indicating that the person (曾) is about to enter the residence of the military governor.
    • 由善和里于西门内 - Phrase indicating the route taken, passing through the Good Harmony Lane and entering the western gate.
    • 忽见抱乘马 - Phrase indicating that the person (曾) suddenly sees the person (抱) riding a horse.
    • 衣冠甚鲜 - Phrase describing the person (抱) as well-dressed and fresh.
    • 二青衣从后 - Phrase indicating that two young servants are following behind.
    • 我命亦将尽 - Phrase indicating that the person (抱) believes his life is coming to an end.
    • 然尚可三年 - Phrase indicating that he still has three more years to live.
    • 由卿任我 - Phrase indicating that he is leaving his fate in the hands of the person addressed (卿).
    • 我今任泰山主簿 - Phrase indicating that the person (抱) now serves as the head of the main office in the City of Tai'an.
    • 已请天曹报杀卿 - Phrase indicating that he has already petitioned the celestial bureaucracy to punish the person addressed (卿).
    • 曾叩头谢罪 - Phrase indicating that the person (曾) kneels and apologizes.
    • 请为追福 - Phrase indicating that the person (曾) asks for blessings or good fortune.
    • 抱许而忽不见 - Phrase indicating that the person (抱) agrees and then disappears suddenly.
    • 数日又遇抱 - Phrase indicating that after several days, the person (曾) meets the person (抱) again.
    • 我终杀卿 - Phrase indicating that the person (抱) states he will eventually kill the person addressed (卿).
    • 放卿七日修福 - Phrase indicating that he gives the person addressed (卿) seven days to perform good deeds.
    • 过此当先取卿头将去 - Phrase indicating that after the seven days, he will take the person addressed's (卿) head.
    • 若不信者，卿死面当在背 - Phrase indicating that if the person addressed (卿) does not believe, his face will be on his back when he dies.
    • 曾惧还家 - Phrase indicating that the person (曾) returns home in fear.
    • 修如期而死 - Phrase indicating that the person (曾) dies as predicted.
    • 回于背 - Phrase indicating that his face was found on his back.
    • 果如所言 - Phrase indicating that it happened exactly as he had said.
    • 康亲识人说云尔也 - Phrase indicating that this story is from the *Kangqin Shi Ren Shuo* (康亲识人说), a collection of anecdotes or sayings.

    Cultural and Historical Allusions

    • 安上门 - Specific gate in ancient Luoyang, often used in historical contexts to describe significant events.
    • 抱 - Historical figure or a person involved in a specific event.
    • 曾 - Another historical figure or a person involved in the same event.
    • 泰山主簿 - Position in the local government, indicating a high-ranking official.
    • 天曹 - Refers to the celestial bureaucracy in Chinese mythology, responsible for judgment and punishment.
    • 追福 - Practice involving performing good deeds to gain blessings or good fortune, often in a religious or superstitious context.
    • 康亲识人说 - Historical text or collection of anecdotes, often used to provide moral or cautionary tales.

    Proper Nouns

    • 安上门 - Specific gate in ancient Luoyang.
    • 抱 - Historical figure or a person involved in the event.
    • 曾 - Another historical figure or a person involved in the event.
    • 泰山主簿 - Position in the local government.
    • 天曹 - The celestial bureaucracy in Chinese mythology.
    • 追福 - Practice involving performing good deeds to gain blessings or good fortune.
    • 康亲识人说 - Historical text or collection of anecdotes.

    Zeng again captured him at the Anmen Gate. Bao knew he could not escape. He told Zeng, "I truly owe the government; death is my own due. However, I do not owe you anything. You and I were once close friends, but you could not help me. Why did you turn against me? If the dead have consciousness, I will surely repay you." Bao eventually surrendered to the law. After several days, Zeng's residence was in Taiping Lane, and he was about to enter the residence of the military governor, passing through the Good Harmony Lane and entering the western gate. Suddenly, he saw Bao riding a horse, well-dressed and fresh, with two young servants following behind. Bao said to Zeng, "My life is coming to an end, but I still have three more years to live. Leave my fate in your hands. I now serve as the head of the main office in Tai'an, and I have already petitioned the celestial bureaucracy to punish you." Zeng knelt and apologized, asking for blessings. Bao agreed and then disappeared suddenly. After several more days, Zeng met Bao again. Bao said, "I will eventually kill you. Give yourself seven days to perform good deeds. After that, I will take your head first. If you do not believe me, your face will be on your back when you die." Filled with fear, Zeng returned home. He died as predicted, and his face was indeed found on his back. This story is from the Kangqin Shi Ren Shuo (康亲识人说). [68]

    武德中临邛人姓韦。与一妇人言誓期不相负累年失宠爱。妇人怨恨。韦惧其及已。因缢杀之。数曰韦身庠。因发癞而死（韦孝谐说云量其从兄也）。
    　　魏郡马嘉运。以武德六年正月。居家。曰晚出大门。忽见两人。各捉马一匹。先在门外树下立。嘉运问。是何人。答云。是东海公使。来迎马生取。嘉运素有学识。知州里。每有台使。及四方贵客。多请见之。及是闻召。弗之怪也。谓使者曰。吾无马。使者进马曰。以此迎马生。嘉运即树下。上马而去。其实倒卧于树下也。俄至一官曹。将入大门。有男女数十人。在门外。如讼者。有妇人。先与嘉运相识。同郡张公谨妻。姓崔氏。手执文书。谓嘉运曰。马生尚相识不。昔与张总管交游。每数相见。总管无状。非理杀我。我诉天曹。于今三年。 [69]

    Relevant Explanation

    Explanation

    [69]

    Idiomatic Expressions

    • 武德中 - A time period during the Tang Dynasty, specifically the early years of Emperor Gaozu's reign (618–626 AD).
    • 与一妇人言誓期不相负累 - A vow made to a woman not to burden or harm her.
    • 数曰韦身庠 - The name 'Wei Shangxiang' (韋身庠).
    • 因发癞而死 - Died from a disease, likely leprosy or another skin ailment.
    • 量其从兄也 - 'Liang' (量) is likely a misreading or misinterpretation; '韋孝諧' is probably the correct name, and '从兄' means 'cousin'.
    • 以武德六年正月 - In the first month of the sixth year of the Wu德 era (623 AD).
    • 居家 - At home.
    • 曰晚出大门 - Went out late in the evening.
    • 捉马一匹 - Held one horse.
    • 先在门外树下立 - Were already standing under a tree outside the gate.
    • 是东海公使 - Are messengers from the Duke of the East Sea.
    • 来迎马生取 - To take Ma Jiayun away.
    • 素有学识 - Had a good education.
    • 州里 - The local area or community.
    • 台使 - Official envoys.
    • 四方贵客 - Distinguished guests from all directions.
    • 多请见之 - Often invited them to meet.
    • 闻召 - Heard of the summons.
    • 弗之怪也 - Did not find it strange.
    • 倒卧于树下 - Fell and lay unconscious under the tree.
    • 俄至一官曹 - Soon arrived at an official office.
    • 男女数十人 - Several dozen men and women.
    • 如讼者 - Like petitioners or litigants.
    • 先与嘉运相识 - Previously acquainted with Ma Jiayun.
    • 同郡张公谨妻 - The wife of Zhang Gongjin from the same prefecture.
    • 姓崔氏 - Surname Cui.
    • 手执文书 - Holding documents.
    • 马生尚相识不 - Do you still remember me, Ma Jiayun?
    • 昔与张总管交游 - We used to associate with Zhang Gongjin.
    • 每数相见 - Often met.
    • 总管无状 - Zhang Gongjin acted improperly.
    • 非理杀我 - Killed me unjustly.
    • 诉天曹 - Appealed to the heavenly court.
    • 于今三年 - For three years now.

    Cultural and Historical Allusions

    • 武德 - The era name of Emperor Gaozu of Tang (618–626 AD).
    • 临邛 - A county in Sichuan Province, historically significant for its salt production.
    • 东海公 - The Duke of the East Sea, a title referring to a high-ranking official or noble.
    • 魏郡 - A prefecture in the central plains of China, historically important.
    • 马嘉运 - A historical figure, though specific details about him are limited in this context.
    • 张公谨 - Zhang Gongjin, a historical figure who served under Emperor Taizong of Tang.
    • 崔氏 - A common surname in Chinese history, often associated with prominent families.
    • 天曹 - The heavenly court, a concept in Chinese mythology and religion where justice is administered.

    Proper Nouns

    • 武德 - The era name of Emperor Gaozu of Tang (618–626 AD).
    • 临邛 - A county in Sichuan Province.
    • 韦 - A surname.
    • 韦身庠 - A name, likely a misreading or misinterpretation.
    • 魏郡 - A prefecture in the central plains of China.
    • 马嘉运 - A historical figure.
    • 东海公 - The Duke of the East Sea.
    • 张公谨 - A historical figure.
    • 崔氏 - A common surname.
    • 天曹 - The heavenly court.

    During the Wu德 era, a man from Linqiong named Wei made a vow with a woman that he would not burden or harm her. However, after losing her favor, she grew resentful. Fearing that her resentment would affect him, Wei strangled her to death. The name of the woman was Wei Shangxiang (likely a misreading or misinterpretation, as "量其从兄也" suggests Wei Xiahé, his cousin). Wei subsequently fell ill and died of a skin disease, possibly leprosy.

    In the first month of the sixth year of the Wu德 era, Ma Jiayun, a man from Wei Prefecture, was at home. Late in the evening, he went out through the main gate and suddenly saw two people, each holding a horse, standing under a tree outside the gate. Ma Jiayun asked, "Who are you?" They replied, "We are messengers from the Duke of the East Sea, sent to take you away." Ma Jiayun, who had a good education and often invited official envoys and distinguished guests from all directions to meet him, did not find the summons strange. He told the messengers, "I have no horse." The messengers then presented a horse and said, "Use this to take Ma Jiayun away." Ma Jiayun climbed onto the horse and left, but in reality, he had fallen unconscious under the tree. Soon, he arrived at an official office. As he was about to enter the main gate, he saw several dozen men and women standing outside, like petitioners. Among them was a woman whom Ma Jiayun had previously met. She was the wife of Zhang Gongjin, a man from the same prefecture, and her surname was Cui. Holding a document, she said to Ma Jiayun, "Do you still remember me, Ma Jiayun?" She continued, "We used to associate with Zhang Gongjin, often meeting. Zhang Gongjin acted improperly and killed me unjustly. I have appealed to the heavenly court, and it has been three years now." [69]

    为王天主救护公谨。故常见抑。今及得申官已追之。不久当至。疑我独见枉害。马生那亦来耶。嘉运知崔氏被杀。及见方知死。使者引入门。门者曰公眠。未可谒。宜引就霍司刑处坐。嘉运见司刑。乃益州行台郎中霍璋。见嘉运。延坐曰。此府记室阙。东海公。闻君才学。欲屈为此官耳。嘉运曰。家贫妻子不立。愿君为言。得免为幸。璋曰。若尔。使可自陈无学。吾当有以相明。俄有人来云。公眠已起。引嘉运入。见一人在厅事坐。肥短黑色。呼嘉运。前谓曰。闻君才学。欲相屈为记室耳。能为之乎。运拜谢曰。幸甚。但鄙人野。颇以经业。教授后生。 [70]

    Relevant Explanation

    Explanation

    [70]

    Idiomatic Expressions

    • 为王天主救护公谨 - Unclear phrase, potentially containing a typographical error or misreading; could be interpreted as 'for the king, the chief deity, to rescue the cautious and prudent,' but this is speculative.
    • 故常见抑 - Means 'therefore, often suppressed' or 'therefore, often held back.'
    • 今及得申官已追之 - Means 'now that the opportunity to declare one's position has been seized, the official has already pursued it.'
    • 不久当至 - Means 'will not be long before it arrives.'
    • 疑我独见枉害 - Means 'suspecting that I alone have been wronged or harmed.'
    • 马生那亦来耶 - Means 'will the horse (or person named Ma) also come?'
    • 嘉运知崔氏被杀 - Means 'Jiayun knows that the Cui family has been killed.'
    • 及见方知死 - Means 'upon seeing, only then does one know of the death.'
    • 使者引入门 - Means 'the envoy led him into the gate.'
    • 门者曰公眠 - Means 'the gatekeeper said that the official is sleeping.'
    • 未可谒 - Means 'it is not yet possible to pay respects.'
    • 宜引就霍司刑处坐 - Means 'it is appropriate to lead him to sit at the place of the Chief Executioner Ho.'
    • 嘉运见司刑 - Means 'Jiayun saw the Chief Executioner.'
    • 乃益州行台郎中霍璋 - Means 'he was the Chief Judge Ho Zhang of the Yizhou Commandery.'
    • 见嘉运，延坐曰 - Means 'seeing Jiayun, he invited him to sit and said...'
    • 此府记室阙 - Means 'there is a vacancy for the position of secretary in this office.'
    • 东海公，闻君才学 - Means 'Donghai Gong has heard of your talent and learning.'
    • 欲屈为此官耳 - Means 'wants to appoint you to this position.'
    • 家贫妻子不立 - Means 'my family is poor, and my wife and children are not well-established.'
    • 愿君为言，得免为幸 - Means 'I hope you will speak on my behalf, and it would be fortunate if I could be exempted.'
    • 若尔，使可自陈无学 - Means 'if so, I can claim that I am unlearned.'
    • 吾当有以相明 - Means 'I will have a way to clarify this.'
    • 俄有人来云 - Means 'soon, someone came and said...'
    • 公眠已起 - Means 'the official has already awakened from sleep.'
    • 引嘉运入 - Means 'led Jiayun inside.'
    • 见一人在厅事坐 - Means 'saw a person sitting in the hall.'
    • 肥短黑色 - Describes a person as 'fat, short, and dark-skinned.'
    • 呼嘉运，前谓曰 - Means 'called Jiayun forward and said...'
    • 闻君才学，欲相屈为记室耳 - Means 'hearing of your talent and learning, I wish to appoint you as secretary.'
    • 能为之乎 - Means 'can you do it?'
    • 运拜谢曰 - Means 'Jiayun bowed and thanked him, saying...'
    • 幸甚 - Means 'very fortunate.'
    • 但鄙人野 - Means 'but I am a rustic person.'
    • 颇以经业，教授后生 - Means 'I have been involved in teaching the young in the classics.'

    Cultural and Historical Allusions

    • 王天主 - Likely a reference to a deity or a high-ranking official.
    • 公谨 - A term often used to describe someone who is cautious and prudent.
    • 益州行台 - Refers to the administrative office of Yizhou in the Tang Dynasty.
    • 东海公 - A title for a high-ranking official, possibly referring to a member of the imperial family or a powerful noble.
    • 霍璋 - A historical figure, likely a high-ranking official or judge.
    • 记室 - A position in the Tang Dynasty bureaucracy, responsible for drafting official documents and managing correspondence.
    • 厅事 - Refers to the main hall of an official's residence or office.

    Proper Nouns

    • 王天主 - Likely a reference to a deity or a high-ranking official.
    • 公谨 - A term often used to describe someone who is cautious and prudent.
    • 益州行台 - Refers to the administrative office of Yizhou in the Tang Dynasty.
    • 东海公 - A title for a high-ranking official, possibly referring to a member of the imperial family or a powerful noble.
    • 霍璋 - A historical figure, likely a high-ranking official or judge.
    • 记室 - A position in the Tang Dynasty bureaucracy, responsible for drafting official documents and managing correspondence.
    • 厅事 - Refers to the main hall of an official's residence or office.

    For the king, the chief deity, to rescue the cautious and prudent, they are often held back. Now that the opportunity to declare one's position has been seized, the official has already pursued it. It will not be long before it arrives. Suspecting that I alone have been wronged or harmed, will the horse (or person named Ma) also come? Jiayun knows that the Cui family has been killed. Upon seeing, only then does one know of the death. The envoy led him into the gate. The gatekeeper said that the official is sleeping. It is not yet possible to pay respects. It is appropriate to lead him to sit at the place of the Chief Executioner Ho. Jiayun saw the Chief Executioner, who was the Chief Judge Ho Zhang of the Yizhou Commandery. Seeing Jiayun, he invited him to sit and said, "There is a vacancy for the position of secretary in this office. Donghai Gong has heard of your talent and learning. He wishes to appoint you to this position." Jiayun replied, "My family is poor, and my wife and children are not well-established. I hope you will speak on my behalf, and it would be fortunate if I could be exempted." Ho Zhang said, "If so, I can claim that I am unlearned. I will have a way to clarify this." Soon, someone came and said, "The official has already awakened from sleep." They led Jiayun inside. They saw a person sitting in the hall, who was fat, short, and dark-skinned. He called Jiayun forward and said, "Hearing of your talent and learning, I wish to appoint you as secretary. Can you do it?" Jiayun bowed and thanked him, saying, "Very fortunate. But I am a rustic person. I have been involved in teaching the young in the classics." [70]

    不足以尚管记之任。公曰。识霍璋不。答曰识之。因使召璋。问以嘉运才术。璋曰。平生知其经学。不见作文章。公曰。放马生归。即命追陈子良。嘉运辞出。璋与之别曰。倩君语我家三狗。临终语汝。卖我所乘马。作乌浮图。汝那卖马自费也。速如我教。造浮图所三狗。谓其长子也。嘉运因问。向见张公谨妻所云。天主者。为谁。璋曰。公谨乡人王五戒者。死为天主常救公谨。故得至今。今似不免矣。言毕而别。遣使者送嘉运。至一小涩径。指令由此路归。嘉运入径便活。良久能起。时向夜半。妻子皆坐哭。嘉运具言之。其年七月。绵州人。姓陈名子良。暴死。 [71]

    Relevant Explanation

    Explanation

    [71]

    Idiomatic Expressions

    • 不足以尚管记之任 - Not sufficient to undertake the duties of a secretary or recorder; '尚' implies 'undertake' or 'take on'.
    • 识霍璋不 - A colloquial way of asking 'Do you know Ho Zang?' where '不' is a question particle.
    • 放马生归 - Possibly means 'let the horse go home' or 'release the horse,' indicating dismissal or letting someone go.
    • 倩君语我家三狗 - Metaphorical or idiomatic way of saying something to one's family or close ones; '三狗' likely refers to family members.
    • 临终语汝 - Means 'to tell you before death,' suggesting a final message or instruction.
    • 卖我所乘马 - Directly translates to 'sell my riding horse.'
    • 作乌浮图 - Unclear but likely refers to a Buddhist stupa or pagoda; '乌' (wū) can mean 'crow' or 'black,' and '浮图' (fútú) often refers to a pagoda.
    • 汝那卖马自费也 - Translates to 'You shouldn't sell the horse for your own expenses,' indicating that the action should not be taken out of personal interest.
    • 速如我教 - Means 'act quickly as I have taught you.'
    • 造浮图所三狗 - Refers to the three dogs who are to build the pagoda; likely a metaphor for family members or close associates.
    • 向见张公谨妻所云 - Means 'what I saw Zhang Gongjin's wife say.'
    • 天主者 - Ambiguous but likely refers to a deity or a spiritual figure.
    • 似不免矣 - Means 'it seems unavoidable,' suggesting that something cannot be avoided or escaped.
    • 暴死 - Directly translates to 'sudden death,' which can be associated with various superstitions or beliefs about the afterlife in Chinese culture.

    Cultural and Historical Allusions

    • 管记之任 - Refers to the role of a secretary or recorder, a position of importance in ancient Chinese bureaucracy.
    • 霍璋 - A historical or literary figure whose identity is not immediately clear from the context provided.
    • 张公谨妻 - Zhang Gongjin was a historical figure, and his wife is mentioned here, possibly indicating a specific event or story related to her.
    • 乌浮图 - Likely a reference to a Buddhist stupa or pagoda, which were common in Chinese culture and often associated with religious practices.
    • 绵州 - A place name, indicating a specific region in China.
    • 陈子良 - A person mentioned in the text, possibly a historical figure or a character in a story.
    • 暴死 - Sudden death, which can be associated with various superstitions or beliefs about the afterlife in Chinese culture.

    Proper Nouns

    • 霍璋 - A historical or literary figure.
    • 张公谨 - A historical figure, known for his role in the Tang Dynasty.
    • 绵州 - A place name, indicating a specific region in China.
    • 陈子良 - A person mentioned in the text, possibly a historical figure or a character in a story.

    The text you provided contains some unusual and potentially mistranslated phrases, making it challenging to produce a coherent and accurate translation. However, I will do my best to provide a translation based on the available information.

    Here is the final translation:

    He was not sufficient to undertake the duties of a secretary or recorder. The official said, "Do you know Ho Zang?" He replied, "I know him." The official then sent for Ho Zang and asked about the talents and skills of Jia Yun. Ho Zang said, "I have always known him for his Confucian learning, but I have not seen him write articles." The official said, "Let the horse go home." He immediately ordered Chen Ziliang to be summoned. Jia Yun excused himself and left. Ho Zang said to him, "Please tell my three dogs at home. Before I die, I want to tell you. Sell my riding horse and build a pagoda. You shouldn't sell the horse for your own expenses. Act quickly as I have taught you. The three dogs who build the pagoda are my eldest sons." Jia Yun then asked, "What did I see Zhang Gongjin's wife say about the 'heavenly lord'?" Ho Zang said, "Wang Wujie, a fellow villager of Zhang Gongjin, died and became the heavenly lord, who often saves Zhang Gongjin. That is why he has survived until now. But it seems he cannot avoid it now." After finishing, they parted. The official sent an envoy to escort Jia Yun. They arrived at a small, narrow path and were instructed to return by this road. Jia Yun entered the path and came back to life. After a long time, he was able to stand up. It was nearly midnight. His wife and children were all sitting and crying. Jia Yun explained everything to them. That year, in July, Chen Ziliang, a man from Mianzhou, died suddenly.

    Please note that some parts of the text, such as "放马生归" and "作乌浮图," are particularly ambiguous and may require further context or verification to ensure accuracy. [71]

    经宿而苏。自言。见东海公欲用为记室。辞不识文字。别有吴人陈子良。善章者。于是命彼舍此。后年吴人陈子良卒死。张公谨亦殂。二人亡后。嘉运尝与数人同行。于路忽见官府者。嘉运神色忧怖。唯诺趋走。须之。乃定。同侣问之。答曰。向见者。东海公使人。云欲往益州追人。仍说。子良极诉君。霍司刑为君被诵读。君几不免。赖君赎生之福。故得免也。初嘉运在蜀。蜀人将决池取鱼。嘉运时为人讲书。得绢数十匹。因买池鱼放之。赎生谓此也。贞观中。车驾在九城宫。闻之。使中书侍郎岑文本。就问其事。文本具录。以奏乞尔。嘉运。后为国子博士卒官。 [72]

    Relevant Explanation

    Explanation

    [72]

    Idiomatic Expressions

    • 经宿而苏 - Recovered after staying overnight; describes the protagonist's revival after a period of illness or unconsciousness.
    • 欲用为记室 - Wants to appoint as a secretary; indicates an offer of employment in a literary or administrative role.
    • 辞不识文字 - Declined due to not knowing how to read or write; explains the protagonist's refusal of the offer.
    • 别有吴人陈子良 - There is another person from Wu named Chen Ziliang; introduces a new character.
    • 善章者 - Who is skilled in writing; describes Chen Ziliang's literary ability.
    • 命彼舍此 - Ordered him to take his place; indicates a transfer of responsibility or role.
    • 自言 - Said to himself; indicates introspection or self-reporting.
    • 向见者 - What I saw earlier; refers back to a previous vision or experience.
    • 极诉君 - Pleaded for you very much; indicates Chen Ziliang's strong advocacy for the protagonist.
    • 霍司刑 - Ho the judicial official; refers to a person in a judicial or administrative role.
    • 被诵读 - Was recited; indicates the reading or recitation of a text.
    • 赖君赎生之福 - Thanks to your merit in saving lives; refers to the protagonist's past good deeds.
    • 初嘉运在蜀 - When Jiayun was in Shu; sets the context for a past event.
    • 蜀人将决池取鱼 - The people of Shu were about to drain a pond to catch fish; describes a specific action.
    • 得绢数十匹 - Received several bolts of silk; indicates a reward or payment.
    • 因买池鱼放之 - Then bought the fish and released them; describes the protagonist's act of redemption.
    • 贞观中 - During the Zhenguan era; refers to a specific period in Chinese history.
    • 车驾在九城宫 - The imperial carriage was at the Jiu Cheng Palace; indicates the location of the emperor.
    • 使中书侍郎岑文本 - Sent the Secretary of the Central Secretariat, Cen Wenben; indicates the emperor's messenger.
    • 就问其事 - To inquire about the matter; indicates the purpose of the inquiry.
    • 文本具录，以奏乞尔 - Wenben recorded it in detail and submitted it to the emperor on your behalf; describes the process of reporting.
    • 后为国子博士卒官 - Later became a Doctor of the Imperial Academy and died in office; indicates the protagonist's final position and death.

    Cultural and Historical Allusions

    • 东海公 - Refers to a deity or a powerful figure associated with the East Sea, often linked to immortality or supernatural powers.
    • 记室 - Refers to a secretary or literary official, typically involved in drafting documents or managing correspondence.
    • 吴人 - Refers to people from Wu, a historical region in southeastern China.
    • 贞观 - Refers to the reign title of Emperor Taizong of Tang, a period of great prosperity and cultural flourishing.
    • 九城宫 - Refers to the Jiu Cheng Palace, a significant imperial residence.
    • 国子博士 - Refers to a high-ranking official in the Imperial Academy, responsible for education and scholarly matters.

    Proper Nouns

    • 东海公 - A deity or powerful figure.
    • 记室 - A secretary or literary official.
    • 吴人 - People from Wu.
    • 贞观 - The reign title of Emperor Taizong of Tang.
    • 九城宫 - The Jiu Cheng Palace.
    • 国子博士 - A high-ranking official in the Imperial Academy.
    • 陈子良 - A person from Wu, skilled in writing.
    • 张公谨 - Another historical figure.
    • 霍司刑 - A judicial official.
    • 岑文本 - The Secretary of the Central Secretariat.

    After staying overnight, he recovered. He said to himself that he had seen the Lord of the East Sea who wanted to appoint him as a secretary, but he declined because he did not know how to read or write. There was another person from Wu named Chen Ziliang, who was skilled in writing, and so the Lord ordered Chen to take his place. The following year, Chen Ziliang died, and Zhang Gongjin also passed away. After the two men died, Jiayun once traveled with several others, and on the road they suddenly saw officials. Jiayun was filled with fear, and he hurriedly obeyed their commands. After a while, he calmed down. His companions asked him about it, and he replied, "What I saw earlier were messengers from the Lord of the East Sea. They said they were going to Yizhou to pursue someone. They also mentioned that Chen Ziliang had pleaded for me greatly, and that Ho the judicial official had recited something on my behalf. I nearly could not escape, but thanks to my merit in saving lives, I was spared." When Jiayun was in Shu, the people of Shu were about to drain a pond to catch fish. At that time, Jiayun was teaching books to others and received several bolts of silk as payment. He then bought the fish and released them, which was an act of redemption. During the Zhenguan era, when the imperial carriage was at the Jiu Cheng Palace, the emperor heard of this and sent the Secretary of the Central Secretariat, Cen Wenben, to inquire about the matter. Cen recorded it in detail and submitted it to the emperor on Jiayun's behalf. Later, Jiayun became a Doctor of the Imperial Academy and died in office. [72]

    武德初。遂州总管府记室参军孔恪。暴病死。一曰而苏。自说。被收至官所。问恪。何因杀两牛水。恪云。不杀。官云。汝弟证汝杀。何故不承。因呼弟。弟死已数年矣。既至。枷械甚严。官问。汝所言兄杀牛虚实。弟曰。兄前奉使。招慰獠贼。使某杀牛会之。实奉兄命。非自杀也。恪因曰。恪使弟杀牛会獠。是实。然国事也。恪何有罪。官曰。汝杀牛会獠。欲以招慰为功。用求官赏。以为己利。向云国事耶。因谓恪弟曰。以汝证兄。故久留汝。兄今既承遣杀。汝无罪。放任受生。言讫弟忽不见。亦竟不得言叙。官又问恪。何因复杀他两鸭。恪曰。前任县令杀鸭。供官客耳。岂恪罪耶。官客自有食料无鸭。汝杀供之。以求美誉。非罪如何。又复杀鸡卵六枚。恪曰。平生不食鸡卵。唯忆年九岁时。 [73]

    Relevant Explanation

    Explanation

    [73]

    Idiomatic Expressions

    • 武德初 - Refers to the early years of the Tang Dynasty, specifically the reign of Emperor Gaozu (Li Yuan).
    • 遂州总管府记室参军 - A title for a government official. '遂州' is the name of a state, '总管府' is the administrative office, '记室参军' is the specific role, which can be translated as 'councilor of the military governor's office.'
    • 暴病死 - Refers to a sudden illness leading to death.
    • 一日而苏 - Means 'revived after one day,' indicating a miraculous recovery.
    • 被收至官所 - Means 'taken to the official's place,' referring to the afterlife or underworld.
    • 何因杀两牛水 - A cryptic phrase that seems to involve a misunderstanding or a trick question. '两牛水' might be a play on words or a reference to a specific event.
    • 何故不承 - Means 'why did you not confess?' This implies that the deceased was being interrogated.
    • 枷械甚严 - Means 'bound and shackled very severely,' describing the harsh treatment of the deceased's brother.
    • 招慰獠贼 - Means 'to recruit and pacify the Lao (a term for non-Han ethnic groups).'
    • 实奉兄命 - Means 'it was actually following your brother's command,' indicating that the brother acted on behalf of the deceased.
    • 国事也 - Means 'it was a matter of state,' suggesting that the actions were taken for the benefit of the country.
    • 非罪如何 - Means 'how is it not a crime?' This is a rhetorical question used to emphasize the severity of the situation.
    • 前任县令杀鸭 - Means 'the previous county magistrate killed ducks,' indicating a past event.
    • 官客自有食料无鸭 - Means 'official guests have their own food supplies and do not need ducks,' suggesting that the deceased was not responsible for providing food for guests.
    • 非罪如何 - Repeated rhetorical question emphasizing the lack of wrongdoing.
    • 杀鸡卵六枚 - Means 'killed six chicken eggs,' which is unusual and might be a metaphorical or symbolic act.
    • 忆年九岁时 - Means 'remembering when I was nine years old,' indicating a personal memory or anecdote.

    Cultural and Historical Allusions

    • 武德初 - The beginning of the Tang Dynasty, marking a significant period in Chinese history.
    • 遂州 - A state during the Tang Dynasty, located in present-day Sichuan Province.
    • 总管府 - An administrative office in the Tang Dynasty, responsible for military and civil affairs.
    • 记室参军 - A government position, often involved in administrative and literary tasks.
    • 獠贼 - Refers to non-Han ethnic groups, often considered barbarians or rebels.
    • 国事 - Matters of state, indicating actions taken for the benefit of the country.
    • 前任县令 - The previous county magistrate, a local government official.
    • 官客 - Official guests, typically high-ranking officials or dignitaries.
    • 鸡卵 - Chicken eggs, which can also symbolize life or fertility in Chinese culture.
    • 忆年九岁时 - A personal memory, often used to provide context or background information.

    Syntactic Ambiguities

    • 何因杀两牛水 - The phrase is ambiguous and might be a play on words or a reference to a specific event. It could mean 'why did you kill two cows and water?' or 'why did you kill two cows and a fish?' The exact meaning is unclear without additional context.
    • 何故不承 - The question is straightforward but implies a deeper reason for the deceased's refusal to confess.
    • 实奉兄命 - This statement clarifies the brother's actions but raises questions about the deceased's involvement.
    • 非罪如何 - The rhetorical question emphasizes the severity of the situation but is open to interpretation.
    • 忆年九岁时 - This phrase provides a personal memory but is disconnected from the main narrative.

    Proper Nouns

    • 武德初 - The early years of the Tang Dynasty.
    • 遂州 - A state in the Tang Dynasty.
    • 总管府 - An administrative office.
    • 记室参军 - A government position.
    • 獠贼 - Non-Han ethnic groups.
    • 国事 - Matters of state.
    • 前任县令 - The previous county magistrate.
    • 官客 - Official guests.
    • 鸡卵 - Chicken eggs.
    • 忆年九岁时 - A personal memory.

    In the early years of the Tang Dynasty, Kong Ke, a councilor of the military governor's office in Suizhou, suddenly fell ill and died. After one day, he revived and recounted his experience. He said that he was taken to the official's place, where he was questioned about why he had killed two cows and water. Kong Ke replied that he had not killed them. The official said, "Your brother testified that you killed them. Why did you not confess?" He then called for Kong Ke's brother, who had already been dead for several years. When the brother arrived, he was bound and shackled very severely. The official asked, "Is it true that your brother killed the cows?" The brother said, "My brother was previously on a mission to recruit and pacify the Lao. He ordered me to kill the cows for a gathering. It was indeed following his command, not a suicide." Kong Ke then said, "It is true that I sent my brother to kill the cows for the Lao gathering. It was a matter of state. How can I be guilty?" The official said, "You killed the cows for the Lao gathering, wanting to claim credit for pacification and seek rewards and personal gain. How can you say it was a matter of state?" The official then told Kong Ke's brother, "Because you testified against your brother, I have kept you here. Now that your brother has confessed to sending you, you are not guilty. I will let you be reborn." After saying this, the brother suddenly disappeared. Kong Ke also did not get a chance to speak further. The official then asked Kong Ke, "Why did you also kill two other ducks?" Kong Ke said, "The previous county magistrate killed the ducks to serve official guests. How can it be my fault?" The official said, "Official guests have their own food supplies and do not need ducks. You killed them to serve them, seeking praise. How is it not a crime?" The official then asked, "Why did you also kill six chicken eggs?" Kong Ke said, "I never ate chicken eggs. I only remember when I was nine years old." [73]

    寒食曰。母与六卵自煮食之。官曰然。欲推罪母耶。恪曰。不敢。但说其因耳。此自恪杀之也。官曰。汝杀他命。当自受之。言讫。忽有数十人。皆青衣执恪将出。恪大呼曰。官府亦大枉滥。官闻之呼还。何枉。恪曰。生平来有罪。皆录不遗。生来修福。令无记者。岂非滥耶。官问主司。恪有何福。何为不录。主司对曰。福亦皆录。但量罪福多少。若福多罪少。先令受福。罪多福少。先令受罪。恪福少罪多。故放未论其福。官怒曰。虽先受罪。何不唱福示之。命鞭主司一百。倏忽鞭讫。血流溅地。既而唱恪生来所修之福。亦无遗忘。官谓恪曰。汝应先受罪。我更放汝归家七曰。可勤追福。因遣人送出。得苏恪大集僧尼。行道忏悔。精勤行道。自说其事。至七曰与家人辞诀。俄而命终。临家兄为遂府属故委之。 [74]

    Relevant Explanation

    Explanation

    [74]

    Idiomatic Expressions

    • 寒食曰 - Reference to the Cold Food Festival, a traditional Chinese holiday.
    • 欲推罪母耶 - Question of whether to blame the mother.
    • 言讫 - After he spoke.

    Cultural and Historical Allusions

    • 寒食 - The Cold Food Festival, a traditional Chinese holiday.
    • 官府 - Refers to the government or officialdom.
    • 青衣 - Green clothes, often worn by lower-ranking officials or servants.
    • 主司 - The supervisor or chief official.
    • 僧尼 - Monks and nuns.
    • 行道忏悔 - Performing religious rituals and confessing sins.
    • 遂府属 - A government official.

    Proper Nouns

    • 恪 - The name of the person being described.
    • 官 - The official.
    • 主司 - The supervisor or chief official.
    • 僧尼 - Monks and nuns.
    • 遂府属 - A government official.

    On the Cold Food Festival, it was said that the mother cooked six eggs for herself. The official replied affirmatively. The question arose whether to blame the mother. 恪 said, "I dare not. I am only explaining the reason." He said, "It was I who killed them." The official said, "You have taken another's life. You must bear the consequences." After he spoke, suddenly dozens of people in green clothes took 恪 away. 恪 cried out, "The government is also unjustly and excessively harsh." The official heard this and called them back. He asked, "What is unjust?" 恪 said, "All my sins have been recorded, but my good deeds have not been noted. Is this not excessive?" The official asked the supervisor, "What good deeds has 恪 done, and why are they not recorded?" The supervisor replied, "Good deeds are also recorded, but the balance of sins and good deeds is considered. If good deeds outweigh sins, one first receives good fortune; if sins outweigh good deeds, one first receives punishment." The official angrily said, "Even if one first receives punishment, why not announce the good fortune to show it?" He ordered the supervisor to be beaten a hundred times. The beating was swift, and blood splattered on the ground. Soon after, the good deeds 恪 had performed were announced, and they were also not forgotten. The official said to 恪, "You should first receive punishment. I will release you to return home for seven days. You may diligently pursue good deeds." He sent 恪 out, and 恪 recovered. 恪 then gathered monks and nuns to perform rituals and confess. He diligently practiced religious rituals and recounted the story. On the seventh day, he bid farewell to his family. Soon after, he died. His brother, who was a government official, took care of the affairs. [74]

    洛州都督酂公窦轨。性好杀戮。初为益州行台仆射。多杀将士。又害行台尚书韦云起。贞观二年冬。在洛州。病甚笃。忽言。有人饷我瓜来。左右报。冬无瓜也。公曰。一盘好瓜。何谓无。既而惊视曰。非瓜也。并是人头。从我债命。又曰。扶我起。见韦尚书。言毕而薨。 [75]

    Relevant Explanation

    Explanation

    [75]

    Idiomatic Expressions

    • 為之一空 - Set phrase meaning completely emptied; fields were left barren.

    Proper Nouns

    • 紹興 - Era name of the Southern Song; rendered as Shaoxing.

    The Duke of Zan, Governor of Lozhou, Dou Gui, was known for his cruel and violent nature. Initially serving as the Shengtai Pushe in Yizhou, he killed many soldiers and also harmed the Shengtai Shangshu Wei Yunqi. During the second year of the Zhenguan era, while in Lozhou, he fell seriously ill. Suddenly, he said, "Someone sent me a melon." Those around him reported, "There are no melons in winter." The duke replied, "A plate of good melons. How can there be none?" He then exclaimed in shock, "It's not melons; they are all heads, owing their lives to me." He then said, "Help me up. I want to see Wei Shangshu." After speaking, he died. [75]

    尚书刑部侍郎宋行质。曹陵人也。性不信佛。有慢易之言。以永徽二年五月。病卒。至六月九曰。尚书都官令史王□。暴病死。经二曰而苏。自言。初死时。见四人来至其所云。追汝。□随行。入一大门。见有厅上西问。有一官人坐。形容肥黑。厅东间。有一僧坐。与官人相当面向北。各有床褥几案。侍童子二百许人。或冠或不皆美容貌。陛下有官吏文案。有一老人。着枷面向西缚立阶下。□至庭。亦已被缚。吏执纸笔。讯□。辞曰。贞观十八年。任长安佐史之曰。何因改籍。□曰。非□之罪也。厅上大官。读其辞辨。顾谓东阶下老囚曰。何因妄诉他。老囚曰。须达年实未至。由□改籍。加须达年。不敢妄也。□曰。十七年改任。告身在家。请追验之。大官因呼领□者三人解缚。将取告身。告身至。 [76]

    Relevant Explanation

    Explanation

    [76]

    Idiomatic Expressions

    • 有慢易之言 - Means 'had contemptuous remarks' or 'spoke with disdain.'
    • 非□之罪也 - Means 'it is not my fault.'
    • 经二日而苏 - Means 'after two days, he revived.'
    • 见四人来至其所云 - Means 'saw four people come to him and said.'
    • 追汝 - Means 'to summon you.'
    • 随行 - Means 'followed along.'
    • 入一大门 - Means 'entered a large gate.'
    • 见有厅上西问 - Means 'saw a hall with a western room.'
    • 有一官人坐 - Means 'there was an official sitting.'
    • 形容肥黑 - Means 'fat and black in appearance.'
    • 厅东间 - Means 'the eastern room of the hall.'
    • 有一僧坐 - Means 'there was a monk sitting.'
    • 与官人相当面向北 - Means 'facing each other, both facing north.'
    • 各有床褥几案 - Means 'each had a bed, pillow, and table.'
    • 侍童子二百许人 - Means 'about two hundred attendants.'
    • 或冠或不皆美容貌 - Means 'some were wearing hats, some were not, but all were handsome.'
    • 陛下有官吏文案 - Means 'there were official documents for officials.'
    • 有一老人 - Means 'there was an old man.'
    • 着枷面向西缚立阶下 - Means 'wearing a collar, facing west, bound and standing at the bottom of the stairs.'
    • 亦已被缚 - Means 'also bound.'
    • 吏执纸笔 - Means 'an official held paper and a pen.'
    • 讯□ - Means 'interrogated.'
    • 辞曰 - Means 'said.'
    • 贞观十八年 - Means 'in the eighteenth year of Zhenguan.'
    • 任长安佐史之曰 - Means 'served as an assistant historian in Chang'an.'
    • 何因改籍 - Means 'why was the register changed?'
    • 厅上大官 - Means 'the high-ranking official in the hall.'
    • 读其辞辨 - Means 'read his words and made a distinction.'
    • 顾谓东阶下老囚曰 - Means 'turned and said to the old prisoner at the eastern steps.'
    • 何因妄诉他 - Means 'why falsely accuse him?'
    • 老囚曰 - Means 'the old prisoner said.'
    • 须达年实未至 - Means 'the age of Suddhodana was not yet reached.'
    • 由□改籍 - Means 'because of me, the register was changed.'
    • 加须达年 - Means 'adding to the age of Suddhodana.'
    • 不敢妄也 - Means 'I did not dare to falsely accuse.'
    • 十七年改任 - Means 'in the seventeenth year, the position was changed.'
    • 告身在家 - Means 'the appointment document was at home.'
    • 请追验之 - Means 'please investigate it.'

    Cultural and Historical Allusions

    • 尚书刑部侍郎 - A high-ranking official in the Tang Dynasty's Ministry of Punishment.
    • 永徽二年 - The second year of the Yonghui era (651 AD) during the Tang Dynasty.
    • 贞观十八年 - The eighteenth year of the Zhenguan era (644 AD) during the Tang Dynasty.
    • 长安 - The capital city of the Tang Dynasty, now modern-day Xi'an.
    • 长安佐史 - An assistant historian in Chang'an.
    • 须达年 - Refers to the age of Śuddhodana, the father of the Buddha.
    • 告身 - An official document of appointment.

    Proper Nouns

    • 宋行质 - Cui Xingzhi, a Tang Dynasty official.
    • 曹陵 - A place name, likely referring to a specific region.
    • 王□ - Wang (surname, name missing), a Tang Dynasty official.
    • 永徽 - The era name of Emperor Gaozong of Tang (650-655 AD).
    • 贞观 - The era name of Emperor Taizong of Tang (627-649 AD).
    • 长安 - The capital city of the Tang Dynasty.
    • 须达年 - The age of Śuddhodana, the father of the Buddha.
    • 告身 - An official document of appointment.

    Cui Xingzhi, Secretary of the Ministry of Punishment, was from Cao Ling. By nature, he did not believe in Buddhism and spoke with disdain. He died of illness in the fifth month of the second year of Yonghui. By the ninth day of the sixth month, Wang (surname, name missing), Inspector of the Department of Civil Affairs, suddenly died of illness. After two days, he revived and claimed that at the time of his initial death, he saw four people come to him and say, "Summon you." He followed along and entered a large gate. He saw a hall with a western room, where an official was sitting, fat and black in appearance. In the eastern room of the hall, there was a monk sitting, facing each other, both facing north. Each had a bed, pillow, and table. There were about two hundred attendants, some wearing hats, some not, but all were handsome. There were official documents for officials. There was an old man, wearing a collar, facing west, bound and standing at the bottom of the stairs. When he reached the courtyard, he was also bound. An official held paper and a pen and interrogated him. He said, "In the eighteenth year of Zhenguan, I served as an assistant historian in Chang'an. Why was the register changed?" He replied, "It is not my fault." The high-ranking official in the hall read his words and made a distinction. He turned and said to the old prisoner at the eastern steps, "Why falsely accuse him?" The old prisoner said, "The age of Suddhodana was not yet reached. Because of me, the register was changed, adding to the age of Suddhodana. I did not dare to falsely accuse." He said, "In the seventeenth year, the position was changed. The appointment document was at home. Please investigate it." The high-ranking official then called three people who led him to be untied and went to retrieve the appointment document. The appointment document arrived. [76]

    大官自读之。谓老囚曰。他改任大分明。汝无理。因令囚出北门。□遥见北门外。昏暗。多有城。城上皆女墙。似是恶处。大官因书案上。谓□曰。汝无罪。放去。拜辞。吏引□至。东阶下拜僧。僧以印印□臂曰好去。吏引□出东门。南行度三重门。每门皆勘视臂印。然后听出。至第四门。门甚大。重楼朱粉。三户并开。状如官城门。守卫严密。验印听出门。东南行数十步。闻有人复唤□。□回顾。见侍郎宋行质。面惨黑色。露头散腰。着故绯袍。头发短垂。如胡人者。立于厅事阶下。有吏卒守之。阶西近城。有大木牌。高丈余。广二尺许。大书牌上。此是勘当拟过王人。其守大。方尺余。甚分明。厅事上。有床坐几案。如官府者。而无人行质见□。悲喜曰。汝何故得来。□曰。官追勘。问改籍。无事蒙放还。 [77]

    Relevant Explanation

    Explanation

    [77]

    Idiomatic Expressions
