    • 大官自读之 - Formal expression indicating that the official read the document himself, emphasizing his authority.
    • 他改任大分明 - Ambiguous phrase that could mean 'he changed his appointment in a clear manner' or 'he was appointed with clarity.'
    • 汝无理 - Direct statement meaning 'you have no reason' or 'you are unreasonable.'
    • 因令囚出北门 - Phrase meaning 'he then ordered the prisoner to go out through the northern gate.'
    • □遥见北门外，昏暗，多有城，城上皆女墙，似是恶处 - Descriptive passage indicating that the prisoner saw the northern gate area as dark, with many walls and battlements, suggesting a dangerous place.
    • 大官因书案上，谓□曰，汝无罪，放去 - Formal statement from the official, meaning 'the official wrote on his desk and said to the prisoner, 'You are innocent, go free.'
    • 拜辞 - Formal expression meaning 'to bow and take leave.'
    • 吏引□至，东阶下拜僧，僧以印印□臂曰好去 - Sequence of actions: the official led the prisoner to the eastern steps, where the prisoner bowed to a monk, and the monk stamped the prisoner's arm with a seal, saying 'Go well.'
    • 吏引□出东门，南行度三重门，每门皆勘视臂印，然后听出 - Detailed description of the prisoner being led out through the eastern gate, passing through three gates, each time having his arm stamped checked before being allowed to proceed.
    • 至第四门，门甚大，重楼朱粉，三户并开，状如官城门，守卫严密，验印听出门 - Vivid description of the fourth gate, which is large, with red-painted towers, three doors open, resembling an official city gate, with strict guards checking the seal before allowing passage.
    • 东南行数十步，闻有人复唤□，□回顾，见侍郎宋行质，面惨黑色，露头散腰，着故绯袍，头发短垂，如胡人者，立于厅事阶下，有吏卒守之 - Detailed description of the prisoner hearing someone call him, turning around, and seeing a court official named Song Xingzhi, who appears disheveled and dressed in an old red robe, standing at the steps of a hall with guards present.
    • 阶西近城，有大木牌，高丈余，广二尺许，大书牌上，此是勘当拟过王人 - Description of a large wooden sign near the city wall, about ten feet high and two feet wide, inscribed with the words 'This is for the examination and approval of the king's people.'
    • 其守大，方尺余，甚分明 - Description of the sign's dimensions and clarity.
    • 厅事上，有床坐几案，如官府者，而无人行质见□，悲喜曰，汝何故得来 - Description of the hall with a bed, chair, and table, resembling an official office, but no one is present. Song Xingzhi, upon seeing the prisoner, expresses a mix of sadness and joy and asks why the prisoner has come.
    • 官追勘，问改籍，无事蒙放还 - Statement meaning 'the official summoned me for investigation, asked about changing my register, and found nothing, so I was released.'

    Cultural and Historical Allusions

    • 大官 - Refers to a high-ranking official, possibly a judge or a government official.
    • 北门 - The northern gate, often associated with danger or the underworld in Chinese folklore.
    • 女墙 - Battlements, typically found on city walls, symbolizing defense and danger.
    • 拜僧 - Bowing to a monk, a common gesture of respect in Chinese culture.
    • 印 - A seal, used for official documents and as a symbol of authority.
    • 厅事 - A hall, often used for official business or meetings.
    • 侍郎 - A high-ranking official, typically a deputy minister.
    • 宋行质 - The name of a specific official, possibly a historical figure.
    • 绯袍 - A red robe, often worn by officials in ancient China, symbolizing rank and authority.
    • 胡人 - A term for people from Central Asia, often associated with foreign cultures in ancient China.

    Proper Nouns

    • 宋行质 - The name of a specific official, possibly a historical figure.
    • 绯袍 - A red robe, often worn by officials in ancient China, symbolizing rank and authority.

    The official read the document himself. He said to the old prisoner, "Your appointment was clearly defined. You have no reason." He then ordered the prisoner to go out through the northern gate. The prisoner saw the northern gate area as dark, with many walls and battlements, suggesting a dangerous place. The official wrote on his desk and said to the prisoner, "You are innocent, go free." The prisoner bowed and took leave. The official led the prisoner to the eastern steps, where the prisoner bowed to a monk, and the monk stamped the prisoner's arm with a seal, saying "Go well." The official led the prisoner out through the eastern gate, passing through three gates, each time having his arm stamped checked before being allowed to proceed. At the fourth gate, it was very large, with red-painted towers, three doors open, resembling an official city gate, with strict guards checking the seal before allowing passage. The prisoner walked southeast for several dozen steps and heard someone call him. The prisoner turned around and saw a court official named Song Xingzhi, who appeared disheveled and dressed in an old red robe, standing at the steps of a hall with guards present. Near the city wall, there was a large wooden sign, about ten feet high and two feet wide, inscribed with the words "This is for the examination and approval of the king's people." The sign was about a foot square and very clear. The hall had a bed, chair, and table, resembling an official office, but no one was present. Song Xingzhi, upon seeing the prisoner, expressed a mix of sadness and joy and asked why the prisoner had come. The prisoner replied, "The official summoned me for investigation, asked about changing my register, and found nothing, so I was released." [77]

    行质舒其两手。谓□曰。吾被责问功德簿。吾手中无功德簿。坐此困极。加之饥渴。苦不可言。君可努力。至我家语。令作功德。如是殷勤。遍嘱之□乃辞去。行数十步。又呼□还。未及言。厅上有官人来坐。怒□曰。我方勘诸事。汝何人辄至囚处。因使卒舍耳。卒舍耳。推令去。□走又至一门。门吏曰。汝被舍耳。耳当聋。吾为汝却其中物。因以手挑其耳。耳中鸣。乃验决放出。出门外。黑如漆。□不知所之。以手摸西及南。皆是墙壁。唯东无障碍。而暗不可行。□立住少顷。见向所讯□之吏。从门出来。谓□曰。君尚能待我。甚善。可乞我千钱。□不应。内自思曰。吾无罪。官放我来。何为有贿吏乎。吏即谓曰。君不得无行。吾向若不早将汝过官。令二曰受缚。岂不困耶。□心然之。因愧谢曰。谨依命。吏曰。 [78]

    Relevant Explanation

    Explanation

    [78]

    Idiomatic Expressions

    • 行質舒其两手 - Describes a physical gesture where a person extends or stretches out both hands.
    • 被责问功德簿 - Indicates that the person is being questioned about their record of good deeds.
    • 坐此困极 - Indicates that the person is extremely exhausted due to the interrogation.
    • 加之饥渴 - Indicates that the person is also suffering from hunger and thirst.
    • 苦不可言 - Indicates that the suffering is unbearable and cannot be described.
    • 君可努力 - A request for the listener to try hard.
    • 至我家语 - Indicates that the listener should go to the speaker's house and speak.
    • 令作功德 - Indicates that the listener should perform good deeds.
    • 如是殷勤 - Indicates that the speaker is being diligent or ardent in their request.
    • 遍嘱之 - Indicates that the speaker is thoroughly instructing or entrusting the listener.
    • 又呼还 - Indicates that the speaker called the listener back.
    • 未及言 - Indicates that the listener had not yet spoken.
    • 厅上有官人来坐 - Indicates that an official came and sat in the hall.
    • 怒曰 - Indicates that the official was angry and spoke.
    • 我方勘诸事 - Indicates that the official was just examining various matters.
    • 汝何人辄至囚处 - Indicates that the official asked what kind of person the listener was for going directly to the place of the prisoner.
    • 因使卒舍耳 - Indicates that the official therefore made the listener stop and let go of the ear.
    • 推令去 - Indicates that the official pushed the listener and ordered them to go away.
    • 走又至一门 - Indicates that the listener ran to another door.
    • 门吏曰 - Indicates that the doorkeeper spoke.
    • 汝被舍耳 - Indicates that the doorkeeper said the listener was to let go of the ear.
    • 耳当聋 - Indicates that the listener's ear should be deaf.
    • 吾为汝却其中物 - Indicates that the doorkeeper would remove the thing inside the ear.
    • 因以手挑其耳 - Indicates that the doorkeeper therefore picked the listener's ear with their hand.
    • 耳中鸣 - Indicates that the listener's ear rang.
    • 乃验决放出 - Indicates that the listener was then verified and decided to be released.
    • 出门外 - Indicates that the listener went outside the door.
    • 黑如漆 - Indicates that it was as black as ink.
    • 不知所之 - Indicates that the listener did not know where to go.
    • 以手摸西及南 - Indicates that the listener touched the west and south.
    • 皆是墙壁 - Indicates that all were walls.
    • 唯东无障碍 - Indicates that only the east had no obstacles.
    • 而暗不可行 - Indicates that it was dark and the listener could not walk.
    • 立住少顷 - Indicates that the listener stood for a short while.
    • 见向所讯之吏 - Indicates that the listener saw the official who had previously questioned them.
    • 从门出来 - Indicates that the official came out from the door.
    • 谓曰 - Indicates that the official spoke.
    • 君尚能待我 - Indicates that the official asked if the listener could still wait for them.
    • 甚善 - Indicates that the listener thought it was very good.
    • 可乞我千钱 - Indicates that the official asked the listener to give them a thousand coins.
    • 不应 - Indicates that the listener did not respond.
    • 内自思曰 - Indicates that the listener thought inside themselves and spoke.
    • 吾无罪 - Indicates that the listener had no crime.
    • 官放我来 - Indicates that the official released the listener to come.
    • 何为有贿吏乎 - Indicates that the listener questioned why there was a corrupt official.

    A person named Xing Zhi extended both hands. He said to someone, "I have been interrogated about my record of good deeds, but I have no record of good deeds in my hands. Because of this, I am extremely exhausted, and further, I am suffering from hunger and thirst. The suffering is unbearable and cannot be described. You should try hard. Go to my house and speak. Have me perform good deeds. In this manner, I am being diligent and ardent. Thoroughly instruct me." The person then left. After walking dozens of steps, Xing Zhi called the person back. The person had not yet spoken when an official came and sat in the hall, angry and said, "I am just examining various matters. What kind of person are you for going directly to the place of the prisoner?" The official therefore made the person stop and let go of the ear. The person was pushed and ordered to go away. Xing Zhi ran to another door. The doorkeeper said, "You are to let go of the ear. Your ear should be deaf. I will remove the thing inside." The doorkeeper therefore picked the person's ear with their hand, and the person's ear rang. The person was then verified and decided to be released. Going outside the door, it was as black as ink. The person did not know where to go and touched the west and south, finding all were walls. Only the east had no obstacles, but it was dark and the person could not walk. The person stood for a short while and saw the official who had previously questioned them come out from the door. The official said, "Can you still wait for me? That's very good. You can give me a thousand coins." The person did not respond and thought inside themselves, "I have no crime. The official released me to come. Why would there be a corrupt official?" The official immediately said, "You cannot not go. If I had not early taken you to the official, you would have been bound in two days. Wouldn't that be exhausting?" Xing Zhi agreed in his heart and humbly thanked, "I will follow your command." The official said. [78]

    吾不用汝铜钱。欲得白纸钱耳。期十五曰来。□许诺。因问归路。吏曰。但东行二百步。当见一故墙穿破。见明可推倒之。即至君家也。□如其言。行至墙推。良久乃倒。□从倒处出。即至其所居。隆政坊南门矣。于是归家。家人哭泣。入户而苏。至十五曰。□忘不与钱。明曰复病困绝。见吏来。怒曰。君果无信。期与我钱。遂不与。今当复将汝去。因驱行。出含光门。令入大坑。□拜谢百余拜。请作钱。乃放归。又苏。□告家人。买纸百张作钱送之。明曰□又病困。复见吏曰。君幸能与我钱而恶不好。□复辞谢。请更作。许。又至二十一曰。□令以六十钱。买白纸百张作钱。并酒食。自于隆政坊西渠水上烧之。既而身体轻健遂愈。临闻其事时与刑部侍郎刘燕客大理少卿辛茂。将在大理鞫问狱诸刘召□至。与宰对问之云尔。 [79]

    Relevant Explanation

    Explanation

    [79]

    Idiomatic Expressions

    • 吾不用汝铜钱。欲得白纸钱耳。 - The speaker refuses copper coins and requests white paper money, possibly symbolically.
    • 期十五曰来。 - "期" means "to agree" or "to promise," and "十五曰" refers to the 15th day, likely a specific date.
    • □许诺。 - The symbol "□" indicates a missing or unclear character, which could be a name or title.
    • 但东行二百步。 - "但" means "just" or "only," and "二百步" is a specific distance.
    • 当见一故墙穿破。 - "故墙" refers to an old wall, and "穿破" means "broken through" or "damaged."
    • 见明可推倒之。 - "见明" could mean "seeing clearly" or "seeing a light," and "推倒之" means "to push it down."
    • 即至君家也。 - "即至" means "immediately arrive," and "君家" refers to "your home."
    • □如其言。 - Another instance of a missing character, possibly a name or title.
    • 因问归路。 - "因" means "because" or "therefore," and "归路" means "the way home."
    • 吏曰。 - "吏" refers to an official or servant.
    • 但东行二百步。 - Repeated phrase, meaning "just go east for 200 steps."
    • 当见一故墙穿破。 - Repeated phrase, meaning "you will see an old wall that is broken through."
    • 见明可推倒之。 - Repeated phrase, meaning "see clearly that you can push it down."
    • 即至君家也。 - Repeated phrase, meaning "then you will arrive at your home."
    • □如其言。 - Repeated phrase, indicating a missing character.
    • 行至墙推。 - "行至" means "walk to," and "墙推" means "the wall is pushed."
    • 良久乃倒。 - "良久" means "for a long time," and "乃倒" means "it finally fell."
    • □从倒处出。 - Another missing character, possibly a name or title.
    • 即至其所居。 - "即至" means "immediately arrive," and "其所居" means "his/her place of residence."
    • 隆政坊南门矣。 - "隆政坊" is a place name, and "南门" means "south gate."
    • 于是归家。 - "于是" means "therefore," and "归家" means "return home."
    • 家人哭泣。 - "家人" means "family members," and "哭泣" means "cry."
    • 入户而苏。 - "入户" means "enter the house," and "苏" means "revive" or "come to."
    • 至十五曰。 - "至" means "until," and "十五曰" refers to the 15th day.
    • □忘不与钱。 - Another missing character, possibly a name or title.
    • 明曰复病困绝。 - "明曰" means "the next day," and "复病困绝" means "became sick again and nearly died."
    • 见吏来。 - "见吏" means "see the official," and "来" means "come."
    • 怒曰。 - "怒曰" means "said angrily."
    • 君果无信。 - "君" means "you," and "果无信" means "really have no trustworthiness."
    • 期与我钱。 - "期" means "promise," and "与我钱" means "give me money."
    • 遂不与。 - "遂不与" means "therefore did not give."
    • 今当复将汝去。 - "今当" means "now will," and "复将汝去" means "take you away again."
    • 因驱行。 - "因" means "therefore," and "驱行" means "drive away."
    • 出含光门。 - "出" means "go out," and "含光门" is a place name.
    • 令入大坑。 - "令" means "order," and "入大坑" means "enter a large pit."
    • □拜谢百余拜。 - Another missing character, possibly a name or title.
    • 请作钱。 - "请作钱" means "ask to make money."
    • 乃放归。 - "乃放归" means "then let him/her return."
    • 又苏。 - "又苏" means "revive again."
    • □告家人。 - Another missing character, possibly a name or title.
    • 买纸百张作钱送之。 - "买纸百张" means "buy 100 sheets of paper," and "作钱送之" means "make money and send it."
    • 明曰□又病困。 - Another missing character, possibly a name or title.
    • 复见吏曰。 - "复见吏" means "see the official again," and "曰" means "say."
    • 君幸能与我钱而恶不好。 - "君幸能" means "you fortunately can," and "与我钱而恶不好" means "give me money but hate it."
    • □复辞谢。 - Another missing character, possibly a name or title.
    • 请更作。 - "请更作" means "ask to make again."
    • 许。 - "许" means "agree."
    • 又至二十一曰。 - "又至" means "again until," and "二十一曰" refers to the 21st day.
    • □令以六十钱。 - Another missing character, possibly a name or title.
    • 买白纸百张作钱。 - "买白纸百张" means "buy 100 sheets of white paper," and "作钱" means "make money."
    • 并酒食。 - "并" means "and," and "酒食" means "alcohol and food."
    • 自于隆政坊西渠水上烧之。 - "自于" means "from," and "隆政坊西渠水上" means "the west channel water of Longzheng Fang," and "烧之" means "burn it."
    • 既而身体轻健遂愈。 - "既而" means "afterward," and "身体轻健遂愈" means "body became light and healthy and healed."
    • 临闻其事时与刑部侍郎刘燕客大理少卿辛茂。 - "临闻其事" means "when he heard the story," and "刑部侍郎刘燕客大理少卿辛茂" are titles and names of officials.
    • 将在大理鞫问狱诸刘召□至。 - "将在大理鞫问" means "in the Dali Temple will interrogate," and "狱诸刘召□至" means "the Liu family in the prison will be summoned."
    • 与宰对问之云尔。 - "与宰对问" means "with the prime minister ask," and "云尔" means "so said."

    Cultural and Historical Allusions

    • 铜钱 - Refers to copper coins, a common form of currency in ancient China.
    • 白纸钱 - Refers to white paper money, which may be a symbolic or metaphorical request.
    • 十五曰 - Refers to the 15th day, likely a specific date in the lunar calendar.
    • 故墙 - Refers to an old wall, which may have symbolic significance.
    • 穿破 - Means "broken through" or "damaged," possibly indicating a physical or metaphorical barrier.
    • 见明 - Could mean "seeing clearly" or "seeing a light," possibly indicating a moment of clarity or revelation.
    • 推倒之 - Means "to push it down," possibly indicating a physical action or a metaphorical resolution.
    • 隆政坊 - A place name, likely a district or neighborhood in an ancient Chinese city.
    • 南门 - Refers to the south gate, a common feature in ancient Chinese city planning.
    • 刑部侍郎 - A high-ranking official in the Ministry of Punishment.
    • 大理少卿 - A high-ranking official in the Dali Temple, responsible for legal matters.
    • 大理鞫问 - Refers to legal interrogation in the Dali Temple.
    • 狱诸刘 - Refers to the Liu family in the prison, possibly indicating a family with legal issues.
    • 宰 - Refers to the prime minister, a high-ranking official in the imperial court.

    Syntactic Ambiguities

    • The sentence is elliptical and could be interpreted as the speaker refusing copper coins and requesting white paper money.
    • The sentence is elliptical and could be interpreted as the speaker promising to come on the 15th day.

    I will not use your copper coins. I wish to receive white paper money instead. I promised to meet you on the fifteenth day. [A name or title is missing here] agreed. I then asked about the way home. The official said, "Just walk east for 200 steps. You will see an old wall that is broken through. When you see clearly, you can push it down. Then you will arrive at your home." [Another name or title is missing here] followed his instructions. Walking to the wall, he pushed it. After a long time, it finally fell. [Another name or title is missing here] came out from where it had fallen. He immediately arrived at his residence, the south gate of Longzheng Fang. He then returned home. His family members were crying. Upon entering the house, he revived. By the fifteenth day, [another name or title is missing here] forgot not to give the money. The next day, he became sick again and nearly died. He saw the official come and said angrily, "You really have no trustworthiness. You promised to give me money. Therefore, you did not give it. Now, I will take you away again." He then drove him away. They went out through the Hanguang Gate and ordered him to enter a large pit. [Another name or title is missing here] bowed and thanked him more than a hundred times, asking to make money. He was then let return. He revived again. [Another name or title is missing here] told his family. He bought 100 sheets of paper to make money and sent it. The next day, [another name or title is missing here] became sick again and nearly died. He saw the official again and said, "You fortunately can give me money, but you hate it." [Another name or title is missing here] again apologized and asked to make it again. He agreed. Again, by the twenty-first day, [another name or title is missing here] ordered him to use 60 coins. He bought 100 sheets of white paper to make money and alcohol and food. He burned it in the west channel water of Longzheng Fang. Afterwards, his body became light and healthy and he healed. When he heard the story, he was with the Minister of Punishment Liu Yanke and the Junior Censor of Dali Xing Mao. They were to interrogate the Liu family in the prison. The prime minister asked him about it, and he said so. [79]

    贞观中魏王府长史京兆韦庆植。有处女。先亡。夫妇痛惜之。后二年。庆植将聚亲宾客。宰肉备食。家人买得羊。未杀。庆植妻夜梦。见其亡女。着青裾白衫。头发上。有双玉钗。是生平所服者。来见母。涕泣言曰。儿尝私用物。不语父母。坐此业报。今受羊身。来偿父母命。明曰当见杀。青羊白头者是。特愿慈恩。垂乞性命。母惊寤。旦而往观。羊项膊皆白。头上有两点白相对。如玉钗形。母对之悲泣。语家人勿杀。待庆植至。将放之。俄而植至。催食。厨人白言。夫人不许杀青羊。植怒即命杀之。宰夫县羊欲杀。宾客数人已到。乃见一女子容貌端正。诉客曰。儿是韦长吏女。乞救命。客等惊愕。止宰夫。宰夫惧植怒。又但见羊鸣。遂杀之。既而客坐。食至。客皆不食。植怪问之。客具以言。庆植悲痛。发病遂不起。京下士人多知之。崔尚书敦礼具为临说。阎尚书立德。亦说云尔。 [80]

    Relevant Explanation

    Explanation

    [80]

    Idiomatic Expressions

    • 贞观中 - Refers to the early years of Emperor Taizong's reign during the Tang Dynasty.
    • 魏王府长史 - The highest administrative official in the Prince of Wei's household.
    • 先亡 - Refers to someone who has passed away.
    • 聚亲宾客 - To gather relatives and guests.
    • 宰肉备食 - To prepare meat for a meal.
    • 青裾白衫 - A traditional Chinese attire, with '青裾' referring to a green skirt and '白衫' to a white shirt.
    • 玉钗 - A pair of jade hairpins, often associated with beauty and elegance.
    • 坐此业报 - Due to this karma or retribution.
    • 明曰当见杀 - Tomorrow, it will be slaughtered.
    • 特愿慈恩 - Especially wishes for compassion.
    • 垂乞性命 - To spare its life.
    • 惊寤 - To wake up in a state of shock.
    • 项膊皆白 - The neck and shoulders are all white.
    • 头上两点白相对 - Two white spots on the head facing each other.
    • 如玉钗形 - In the shape of jade hairpins.
    • 悲泣 - To weep.
    • 勿杀 - Do not kill.
    • 俄而 - Soon after.
    • 催食 - To urge for the meal.
    • 厨人白言 - The cook reported.
    • 夫人不许杀青羊 - The lady does not permit the killing of the green sheep.
    • 宰夫惧植怒 - The butcher feared the anger of Mr. Wei.
    • 又但见羊鸣 - But only heard the sheep bleating.
    • 遂杀之 - So he killed it.
    • 既而客坐 - Afterward, the guests sat down.
    • 客皆不食 - The guests all refused to eat.
    • 植怪问之 - Mr. Wei was surprised and asked them.
    • 客具以言 - The guests explained everything.
    • 悲痛 - To grieve.
    • 发病遂不起 - Became ill and never recovered.
    • 京下士人多知之 - Many scholars in the capital knew about it.
    • 崔尚书敦礼 - Cui Shangfu, a high-ranking official.
    • 阎尚书立德 - Yan Shangfu, another high-ranking official.
    • 亦说云尔 - Also said so.

    Cultural and Historical Allusions

    • 贞观 - The era name of Emperor Taizong of Tang, indicating the early part of his reign.
    • 魏王府 - The household of the Prince of Wei, one of the imperial princes.
    • 长史 - The highest administrative official in the prince's household.
    • 韦庆植 - A historical figure, the long-term official of the Prince of Wei.
    • 青羊白头 - A specific physical characteristic of the sheep, symbolizing a connection to the deceased daughter.
    • 玉钗 - Jade hairpins, often associated with beauty and elegance, and a symbol of the deceased daughter's identity.
    • 业报 - Karma or retribution, a concept from Buddhist and Daoist beliefs.
    • 京下士人 - Scholars and officials in the capital city of Chang'an.
    • 崔尚书敦礼 - Cui Shangfu, a high-ranking official.
    • 阎尚书立德 - Yan Shangfu, another high-ranking official.

    Proper Nouns

    • 贞观 - The era name of Emperor Taizong of Tang.
    • 魏王府 - The household of the Prince of Wei.
    • 长史 - The highest administrative official in the prince's household.
    • 韦庆植 - A historical figure, the long-term official of the Prince of Wei.
    • 崔尚书敦礼 - Cui Shangfu, a high-ranking official.
    • 阎尚书立德 - Yan Shangfu, another high-ranking official.

    During the early years of Emperor Taizong's reign, Wei Waiwang's long-term official, Wei Qingzhi from Jingzhao, had a daughter who had already passed away. The couple mourned deeply. Two years later, Wei Qingzhi planned to gather relatives and guests and prepared meat for a meal. The family bought a sheep but had not yet slaughtered it. That night, Wei Qingzhi's wife had a dream in which she saw her deceased daughter, dressed in a green skirt and white shirt, with her hair adorned with a pair of jade hairpins, which she usually wore. The daughter came to see her mother and wept, saying, "I used things without telling my parents, and due to this karma, I am now in the body of a sheep. I come to repay my parents' debt. Tomorrow, I will be slaughtered. The green sheep with a white head is me. I especially wish for your compassion and mercy to spare my life." The mother woke up in shock. In the morning, she went to look at the sheep and found that its neck and shoulders were all white, with two white spots on its head facing each other, in the shape of jade hairpins. The mother wept and told the family not to kill it, waiting for Wei Qingzhi to arrive so they could release it. Soon after, Wei Qingzhi arrived and urged for the meal. The cook reported that the lady did not permit the killing of the green sheep. Wei Qingzhi became angry and ordered it to be killed. The butcher hung the sheep to slaughter, and several guests had already arrived. They saw a beautiful woman who pleaded with the guests, saying, "I am the daughter of Mr. Wei. Please save my life." The guests were shocked and stopped the butcher. The butcher feared Wei Qingzhi's anger and only heard the sheep bleating, so he killed it. Afterward, the guests sat down, and the food was served. The guests all refused to eat. Wei Qingzhi was surprised and asked them. The guests explained everything. Wei Qingzhi grieved and became ill, never recovering. Many scholars in the capital knew about it. Cui Shangfu, a high-ranking official, provided additional testimony, and Yan Shangfu, another high-ranking official, also said so. [80]

    华州郑县人。张法义。年少贫野。不修礼度。贞观十年。入华山伐枝。遇见一僧坐岩中。法义便就与语。会天晦冥。久坐不能归因宿焉。僧设松柏末以食之。谓法义曰。贫道居此久。不欲外人知。檀越出。慎勿言相见也。因为说。俗人多罪累。死皆入恶道。诚心忏悔。可灭之。乃令洗浴清净。被僧衣。为忏悔。且而别去。至十九年。法义病死。埋于野外。贫无棺椁。以薪柴不瘗之。七曰而苏。自推去。出归家。家人惊愕。审问知活。乃喜。法义自说。初死。有两人来取。乘空南行。 [81]

    Relevant Explanation

    Explanation

    [81]

    Idiomatic Expressions

    • 華州鄭縣人 - Indicates that the subject is from Zheng County in Huazhou, providing a geographical background.
    • 年少貧野，不修禮度 - Describes the person as young, poor, and rustic, setting up his character, and suggests a lack of adherence to social norms and etiquette.
    • 貞觀十年 - Refers to the year 634 during the reign of Emperor Taizong of Tang.
    • 入華山伐枝 - Describes the action of entering Mount Hua to cut branches, indicating a rural activity.
    • 見一僧坐岩中 - Describes encountering a monk sitting in a rock, suggesting a serene and isolated setting.
    • 法義便就與語 - Indicates that Zhang Faying approached the monk and started a conversation.
    • 會天晦冥 - Describes the sky becoming dark and gloomy, setting a mysterious tone.
    • 久坐不能歸 - Indicates that they sat for a long time and could not return home.
    • 因宿焉 - Suggests that they stayed overnight due to the circumstances.
    • 僧設松柏末以食之 - Describes the monk preparing a meal of pine and cypress leaves for Zhang Faying.
    • 貧道居此久 - Indicates that the monk has lived there for a long time.
    • 檀越出 - Refers to when Zhang Faying leaves, using a respectful term for a benefactor.
    • 慎勿言相见也 - Advises Zhang Faying to be cautious and not speak about their meeting.
    • 因爲說 - Indicates that the monk then began to speak.
    • 俗人多罪累 - Describes how ordinary people often have many sins and burdens.
    • 死皆入惡道 - Suggests that death leads to a bad realm or rebirth.
    • 誠心懺悔 - Refers to sincere repentance.
    • 可滅之 - Indicates that sincere repentance can eliminate sins.
    • 乃令洗浴清淨 - Describes the monk instructing Zhang Faying to bathe and purify himself.
    • 被僧衣 - Indicates that Zhang Faying was dressed in a monk's robe.
    • 爲懺悔 - Suggests that the monk performed a ritual of confession and repentance.
    • 且而別去 - Indicates that the monk then left.
    • 至十九年 - Refers to the year 643.
    • 法義病死 - Describes Zhang Faying's illness and death.
    • 埋於野外 - Indicates that he was buried in the wilderness.
    • 貧無棺椁 - Describes his poverty, lacking a coffin.
    • 以薪柴不瘗之 - Suggests that he was buried with only firewood.
    • 七曰而蘇 - Indicates that he revived after seven days.
    • 自推去 - Describes him pushing himself out of the grave.
    • 出歸家 - Indicates that he returned home.
    • 家人驚愕 - Describes the family's surprise and shock.
    • 審問知活 - Indicates that they questioned him and learned he had come back to life.
    • 乃喜 - Suggests that the family was happy.
    • 法義自說 - Indicates that Zhang Faying then began to recount his experience.
    • 初死 - Describes the moment of his death.
    • 有兩人來取 - Indicates that two people came to take him away.
    • 乘空南行 - Suggests that they flew south through the air.

    Cultural and Historical Allusions

    • 華州鄭縣 - Refers to Zheng County in Huazhou, a specific location in ancient China.
    • 貞觀十年 - Refers to the year 634 during the reign of Emperor Taizong of Tang, a period known for its cultural and political achievements.
    • 華山 - One of the Five Great Mountains of China, known for its spiritual significance and natural beauty.
    • 僧 - Refers to a Buddhist monk, indicating a religious context.
    • 貧道 - A humble term used by monks to refer to themselves.
    • 檀越 - A respectful term for a benefactor or donor.
    • 惡道 - Refers to a bad realm or rebirth in Buddhist cosmology.
    • 懺悔 - A ritual of confession and repentance in Buddhism.
    • 棺椁 - Refers to a coffin, indicating a burial custom.
    • 貞觀 - The era name of Emperor Taizong, indicating the historical period.

    Proper Nouns

    • 華州鄭縣 - Zheng County in Huazhou.
    • 貞觀十年 - The year 634.
    • 華山 - Mount Hua.
    • 僧 - Monk.
    • 貧道 - A humble term for a monk.
    • 檀越 - A respectful term for a benefactor.
    • 惡道 - A bad realm or rebirth.
    • 懺悔 - A ritual of confession and repentance.
    • 棺椁 - A coffin.
    • 貞觀 - The era name of Emperor Taizong.

    A man from Zheng County, Huazhou, named Zhang Faying. When he was young, poor, and rustic, he did not adhere to social norms and etiquette. In the tenth year of the Zhenguan era, he entered Mount Hua to cut branches and encountered a monk sitting in a rock. Zhang Faying approached him and started a conversation. The sky became dark and gloomy, and they sat for a long time, unable to return home, so they stayed overnight. The monk prepared a meal of pine and cypress leaves for Zhang Faying and said, "I have lived here for a long time and do not want outsiders to know. When you leave, be cautious and do not speak about our meeting." The monk then began to speak, saying, "Ordinary people often have many sins and burdens, and after death, they enter a bad realm. Sincere repentance can eliminate these sins." He instructed Zhang Faying to bathe and purify himself, dress in a monk's robe, and perform a ritual of confession and repentance. The monk then left.

    In the nineteenth year, Zhang Faying fell ill and died. He was buried in the wilderness, lacking a coffin, and was buried with only firewood. After seven days, he revived, pushed himself out of the grave, and returned home. His family was surprised and shocked. They questioned him and learned that he had come back to life, and they were happy. Zhang Faying recounted his experience, saying that when he first died, two people came to take him away and flew south through the air. [81]

    至官府。入大门。又巡巷。左右皆是官曹。门闾相对。不可胜数。法义至一曹。见官人。遥责使者曰。是华州张法义也。本限三曰至。何因乃淹七曰。使者曰。法义家狗恶。兼有咒师神见打甚。因袒而示之背。背皆青肿。官曰。稽过多咎。与二十杖。言杖亦毕。血流洒地。官曰。可将法义过录事。录事署发文书。令送付判官。判官召主典。取法义案。案簿甚多。盈一床。主典对法义前。披捡之。其簿多先朱句毕。有末句者。典则录之曰。贞观十一年。法义父使刈禾。义反顾张目私骂。不孝。 [82]

    Relevant Explanation

    Explanation

    [82]

    Idiomatic Expressions

    • 至官府 - Arriving at the government office; '官府' refers to the administrative office.
    • 入大门 - Entering the main gate; indicates the entrance to the official building.
    • 巡巷 - Patrolling the alleys; suggests movement through the corridors or passages.
    • 左右皆是官曹 - On both sides are the offices of officials; '官曹' refers to the offices or departments of officials.
    • 门闾相对 - Gates and thresholds face each other; indicates the layout of the buildings.
    • 不可胜数 - Too numerous to count; emphasizes the large number of offices.
    • 遥责使者曰 - The official scolded the messenger from afar; indicates a distance between the official and the messenger.
    • 本限三曰至 - Originally limited to arrive within three days; '曰' is an archaic form of '天' (days).
    • 何因乃淹七曰 - Why did it take seven days; again using '曰' for '天'.
    • 家狗恶 - The family dog is vicious; describes the dog's behavior.
    • 兼有咒师神见打甚 - Plus there were spells cast by a sorcerer that hit him severely; indicates the involvement of supernatural elements.
    • 袒而示之背 - He exposed his back to show it; indicates the messenger showing his injuries.
    • 背皆青肿 - His back was all bruised and swollen; describes the injuries.
    • 稽过多咎 - For excessive delay, there is fault; indicates the reason for punishment.
    • 与二十杖 - Gave him twenty strokes of the cane; describes the punishment.
    • 血流洒地 - Blood flowed and splattered on the ground; describes the severity of the punishment.
    • 可将法义过录事 - Can take Law Yi to the clerk; indicates the next step in the process.
    • 录事署发文书 - The clerk issued the document; describes the administrative action.
    • 令送付判官 - Ordered to be sent to the judge; indicates the final step in the process.
    • 判官召主典 - The judge summoned the chief clerk; indicates the judge's action.
    • 取法义案 - Took Law Yi's case file; describes the retrieval of the case file.
    • 案簿甚多 - The case files were very numerous; indicates the volume of paperwork.
    • 盈一床 - Filled a bed; indicates the size of the pile of documents.
    • 主典对法义前 - The chief clerk in front of Law Yi; indicates the presence of the chief clerk.
    • 披捡之 - Examined it; describes the action of reviewing the documents.
    • 其簿多先朱句毕 - Most of the documents had already been marked with red ink and completed; indicates the status of the documents.
    • 有末句者 - Those with incomplete sentences; indicates the remaining work.
    • 典则录之 - The clerk recorded it; describes the action of noting the incomplete sentences.
    • 贞观十一年 - In the eleventh year of the Zhenguan era; indicates the specific time period.
    • 法义父使刈禾 - Law Yi's father sent him to cut grass; describes the task given to Law Yi.
    • 义反顾张目私骂 - Law Yi turned around and glared, privately cursing; indicates Law Yi's reaction.
    • 不孝 - Disobedient; describes Law Yi's behavior.

    Cultural and Historical Allusions

    • 贞观十一年 - Refers to the eleventh year of the Zhenguan era, a period during the Tang Dynasty known for its prosperity and good governance under Emperor Taizong.
    • 官府 - Refers to the government office, a central institution in ancient Chinese administration.
    • 官曹 - Refers to the offices or departments of officials, indicating the organizational structure of the government.
    • 门闾相对 - Describes the layout of the buildings, reflecting the architectural design of official buildings.
    • 咒师 - Refers to a sorcerer or magician, indicating the belief in supernatural powers in ancient China.
    • 杖 - Refers to a form of corporal punishment, commonly used in ancient Chinese legal systems.
    • 判官 - Refers to a judge, a high-ranking official responsible for judicial matters.
    • 主典 - Refers to a chief clerk, a lower-ranking official responsible for administrative tasks.
    • 案 - Refers to a case file, containing all the documents related to a legal case.
    • 朱句 - Refers to red ink marks, used to indicate completion or approval in official documents.
    • 不孝 - Refers to disobedience to parents, a serious offense in Confucian ethics.

    Proper Nouns

    • 华州张法义 - Refers to Zhang Faying from Huazhou, a specific individual mentioned in the text.
    • 贞观十一年 - Refers to the eleventh year of the Zhenguan era, a specific time period during the Tang Dynasty.
    • 法义父 - Refers to Law Yi's father, a specific individual mentioned in the text.
    • 判官 - Refers to a judge, a specific role in the legal system.
    • 主典 - Refers to a chief clerk, a specific role in the administrative system.

    Arriving at the government office, he entered the main gate and then patrolled the alleys. On both sides were the offices of officials, with gates and thresholds facing each other, too numerous to count. When Law Yi arrived at one of these offices, he saw an official who scolded the messenger from afar, saying, "This is Zhang Faying from Huazhou. Originally, he was supposed to arrive within three days. Why did it take seven days?" The messenger replied, "Law Yi's family dog is vicious, and there were spells cast by a sorcerer that hit him severely. He showed his back to us, and it was all bruised and swollen." The official said, "For excessive delay, there is fault. I will give him twenty strokes of the cane." After the punishment, blood flowed and splattered on the ground. The official then said, "Take Law Yi to the clerk. The clerk issued the document and ordered him to be sent to the judge." The judge summoned the chief clerk, who took Law Yi's case file. The case files were very numerous, filling a bed. The chief clerk examined them in front of Law Yi, noting that most of the documents had already been marked with red ink and completed, but there were still a few with incomplete sentences. He recorded these and said, "In the eleventh year of the Zhenguan era, Law Yi's father sent him to cut grass. Law Yi turned around and glared, privately cursing his father, showing disobedience." [82]

    合杖八十。始录一条。即见岩穴中僧来。判官起迎。问僧何事。僧曰。张义是贫道弟子。其罪并忏悔灭除。天曹案中已勾毕。今枉追来不合死。主典曰。经忏悔者。此案亦勾了。至如张目骂父。虽蒙忏悔。事未勾了。僧曰。若不如此。当取案勘之。应有福利。判官令主典将法义咨王。宫在东。殿宇宏壮。侍卫数千人。僧亦随至王所。王起迎僧曰。师当直来耶。答曰。未当次直。有弟子张法义。被录来此。其人宿罪。并贫道勾讫。未合死。主典又以张目事咨王。王曰。张目在忏悔后。 [83]

    Relevant Explanation

    Explanation

    [83]

    Idiomatic Expressions

    • 合杖八十 - A legal or penal term referring to a specific punishment or age-related regulation, possibly indicating a fine or a penalty for an offense.
    • 始录一条 - Literally 'starting with one item' or 'beginning with a single entry,' likely referring to the start of a legal or administrative record.
    • 其罪并忏悔灭除 - His sins have been forgiven through repentance, indicating a process of spiritual cleansing.
    • 天曹案中已勾毕 - In the records of the Heavenly Court, his case has been completed, suggesting a divine or celestial judgment.
    • 今枉追来不合死 - It is unjust to summon him now, as he should not die, indicating a wrongful accusation.
    • 虽蒙忏悔，事未勾了 - Although he has repented, the matter has not been resolved, indicating that some sins are not fully absolved.
    • 应有福利 - There should be benefits, implying that there are positive outcomes or rewards.
    • 师当直来耶 - Should you be coming on duty? a question about the monk's role or timing.
    • 张目在忏悔后 - Zhang's act of cursing his father is after repentance, indicating that the sin occurred after a period of repentance.

    Cultural and Historical Allusions

    • 岩穴中僧 - A monk living in a cave or rock shelter, which could be symbolic of solitude and spiritual retreat.
    • 判官 - A judge or official in a legal or administrative context.
    • 天曹 - The Heavenly Court, a concept in Chinese mythology and religion, representing a celestial authority.
    • 贫道 - A humble self-reference used by monks, similar to 'I' or 'me.'
    • 张目骂父 - A specific act of cursing one's father, which is a serious offense in traditional Chinese culture.
    • 主典 - The chief clerk or administrator in a legal or administrative context.
    • 法义 - The law or legal principles.
    • 王 - The king or ruler, a central figure in the narrative.
    • 宫在东 - The palace is located to the east, which could have symbolic significance in Chinese cosmology.
    • 殿宇宏壮 - The grandeur of the palace, reflecting the power and authority of the king.
    • 侍卫数千人 - The large number of guards, emphasizing the king's security and status.
    • 师当直来耶 - A question about the monk's duty, indicating a structured system of service.

    Proper Nouns

    • 张义 - A specific individual, possibly a historical or fictional character.
    • 张目 - A specific act of cursing one's father.
    • 主典 - The chief clerk or administrator.
    • 法义 - The law or legal principles.
    • 王 - The king or ruler.
    • 宫在东 - The palace located to the east.

    A fine of eighty strokes. The record began with one entry. Immediately, a monk from a cave came. The judge rose to greet him. The judge asked the monk what the matter was. The monk said, "Zhang Yi is my disciple. His sins have been forgiven through repentance. In the records of the Heavenly Court, his case has been completed. It is unjust to summon him now, as he should not die." The chief clerk said, "Those who have repented, their cases are also completed. As for Zhang's act of cursing his father, although he has repented, the matter has not been resolved." The monk said, "If it is not this way, we should check the records. There should be benefits." The judge ordered the chief clerk to consult the king about the law. The palace is to the east, with grand halls and buildings, and thousands of guards. The monk also went to the king's presence. The king rose to greet the monk and said, "Should you be coming on duty?" The monk replied, "It is not yet your turn on duty. I have a disciple named Zhang Faye who was summoned here. His past sins have all been resolved, and he should not die." The chief clerk then consulted the king about Zhang's act of cursing his father. The king said, "Zhang's act of cursing his father is after repentance." [83]

    不合免。然师为来。请可特放七曰。法义谓僧曰。七曰既不多时。复来恐不见师。请即住随师。师曰。七曰七年也。可急去。法义固请随僧。僧因。请王笔书义掌作一字。又请王印。印之曰。可急去。还家修福。若后来不见我。宜以印呈王。王自当放汝也。法义乃辞之。僧令人送至其家。家内正黑。义不敢入。使者推之。遂活。觉在土中。甚轻虚。以手推排得出。因入山。就山僧修道。掌中所印之处文。不识然皆为疮。终莫能愈。至今尚在。陇西王博叉居。与法义近。委知之。为临说云尔。 [84]

    Relevant Explanation

    Explanation

    [84]

    Idiomatic Expressions

    • 不合免 - A phrase indicating that something is not appropriate or fitting to be exempted.
    • 然師為來 - A phrase suggesting that the teacher is coming, but the context implies a conditional or uncertain situation.
    • 請可特放七曰 - A request for special permission to be released for seven days.
    • 法義謂僧曰 - A dialogue marker indicating that the monk is speaking to the person named 法義.
    • 七曰既不多時 - A phrase meaning that seven days is not a long time, but the context suggests a deeper meaning or urgency.
    • 復來恐見師 - A phrase indicating that returning might mean seeing the teacher again, possibly in a negative context.
    • 請即住隨師 - A request to stay and follow the teacher immediately.
    • 七曰七年也 - A phrase that plays on the word '七' (seven), suggesting that seven days is actually seven years, emphasizing the long duration.
    • 可急去 - A command to leave quickly.
    • 法義固請隨僧 - A phrase indicating that 法義 persistently asks to follow the monk.
    • 僧因 - A phrase indicating that the monk, due to some reason, agrees.
    • 請王筆書義掌作一字 - A request for the king to write a character on the person's palm.
    • 又請王印 - A request for the king to seal the character.
    • 印之曰 - A phrase indicating that the king seals it, saying...
    • 可急去 - A command to leave quickly.
    • 還家修福 - A phrase meaning to return home and cultivate virtue.
    • 若後來不見我 - A conditional phrase meaning 'if you do not see me later.'
    • 宜以印呈王 - A phrase indicating that the seal should be presented to the king.
    • 王自當放汝也 - A phrase meaning that the king will certainly release you.
    • 法義乃辭之 - A phrase indicating that 法義 then bid farewell.
    • 僧令人送至其家 - A phrase indicating that the monk sent someone to accompany 法義 to his home.
    • 家内正黑 - A phrase indicating that the house was completely dark.
    • 義不敢入 - A phrase indicating that 法義 was too afraid to enter.
    • 使者推之 - A phrase indicating that the messenger pushed him in.
    • 遂活 - A phrase indicating that he then survived.
    • 覺在土中 - A phrase indicating that he became aware that he was in the ground.
    • 甚輕虚 - A phrase indicating that he felt very light and airy.
    • 以手推排得出 - A phrase indicating that he pushed and managed to get out.
    • 因入山 - A phrase indicating that he then entered the mountains.
    • 就山僧修道 - A phrase indicating that he began practicing the Way with the mountain monks.
    • 掌中所印之处文 - A phrase indicating the text on the palm where the seal was placed.
    • 不识然皆为疮 - A phrase indicating that it was unknown, but all became sores.
    • 终莫能愈 - A phrase indicating that it could never be healed.
    • 至今尚在 - A phrase indicating that it is still there to this day.
    • 陇西王博叉居 - A phrase indicating that the king of Longxi, Bocha, lives nearby.
    • 委知之 - A phrase indicating that he is well-informed about the matter.
    • 为临说云尔 - A phrase indicating that he told this to someone nearby.

    Cultural and Historical Allusions

    • 法義 - A name that could refer to a person, possibly a monk or a follower of the Way.
    • 僧 - A monk or a practitioner of Buddhism or a similar spiritual path.
    • 陇西王博叉 - The king of Longxi, Bocha, a historical figure or a fictional character in the context of the story.
    • 印 - A seal, a symbol of authority or a magical object in the context of the story.
    • 修福 - The practice of cultivating virtue or merit, often associated with Buddhist or Taoist practices.
    • 修道 - The practice of the Way, often associated with Taoist or Buddhist practices.

    Proper Nouns

    • 法義 - A name that could refer to a person, possibly a monk or a follower of the Way.
    • 僧 - A monk or a practitioner of Buddhism or a similar spiritual path.
    • 陇西王博叉 - The king of Longxi, Bocha, a historical figure or a fictional character in the context of the story.
    • 印 - A seal, a symbol of authority or a magical object in the context of the story.

    A special exemption is not appropriate. However, the teacher is coming. I request a special permission to stay for seven days. The monk, named 法義, said to the monk: "Seven days is not a long time, but if I return, I might not see the teacher again. I request to stay and follow the teacher." The monk replied, "Seven days are actually seven years. You should leave quickly." 法義 persistently asked to follow the monk. The monk, due to some reason, agreed. He then requested the king to write a character on his palm and to seal it. The king sealed it, saying, "You should leave quickly. Return home and cultivate virtue. If you do not see me later, present the seal to the king. The king will certainly release you." 法義 then bid farewell. The monk sent someone to accompany him to his home. The house was completely dark, and 法義 was too afraid to enter. The messenger pushed him in. He then survived, becoming aware that he was in the ground, feeling very light and airy. He pushed and managed to get out. He then entered the mountains, beginning to practice the Way with the mountain monks. The text on the palm where the seal was placed was unknown, but all became sores. It could never be healed. It is still there to this day. The king of Longxi, Bocha, lives nearby and is well-informed about the matter. He told this to someone nearby. [84]

    河东柳智感。以贞观初。为兴州长举县令。一夜暴死。明曰而苏。说云。始为冥官所追。至大官府。使者以智感见王。谓曰。今有一员官阙。故枉君来任之。智感辞以亲老。且自陈福业未应便死。王使勘之。信然。因谓曰。君未当死。可权判录事。智感许诺拜谢。吏引退。至曹。曹有判官五人。连感为六。其厅事是长屋人坐三间。各有床案。务甚繁拥。西头一座。空无判官。吏引智感就空座。有群吏引将文簿来。取智感判置于案上。而退立阶下。智感问之。对曰。气恶不改逼公。但遥以案中事答。智感省读案如人间案者。于是即为判勾之有顷。有食来。诸判官同食。智感亦就之。诸官曰。君既权判。不宜食此。 [85]

    Relevant Explanation

    Explanation

    [85]

    Idiomatic Expressions

    • 一夜暴死 - Literally means 'died suddenly in one night,' indicating an unexpected death.
    • 明曰而苏 - Means 'woke up the next day,' suggesting a miraculous revival.
    • 始为冥官所追 - Means 'was first summoned by officials from the underworld.'
    • 至大官府 - Refers to arriving at a grand official building in the underworld.
    • 使者以智感见王 - Means 'the messenger presented Li Zhi'an to the king.'
    • 今有一员官阙 - Means 'there is currently a vacancy for one official.'
    • 故枉君来任之 - Means 'so we have come to ask you to take up this position.'
    • 辞以亲老 - Means 'declined citing his elderly parents.'
    • 且自陈福业未应便死 - Means 'and also stated that his fortune and virtue should not lead to his death yet.'
    • 王使勘之 - Means 'the king had it investigated.'
    • 信然 - Means 'it was indeed true.'
    • 可权判录事 - Means 'you can temporarily serve as the recorder.'
    • 许诺拜谢 - Means 'he agreed and bowed to show gratitude.'
    • 至曹 - Means 'arrived at the office.'
    • 曹有判官五人 - Means 'there were five judges in the office.'
    • 连感为六 - Means 'including Li Zhi'an, making six in total.'
    • 厅事是长屋人坐三间 - Describes the office as a large hall with three rooms for people to sit.
    • 各有床案 - Means 'each room had beds and desks.'
    • 务甚繁拥 - Means 'the work was very busy and overwhelming.'
    • 西头一座 - Refers to an empty seat on the western side.
    • 空无判官 - Means 'empty, without a judge.'
    • 有群吏引将文簿来 - Means 'a group of officials brought documents.'
    • 取智感判置于案上 - Means 'took Li Zhi'an's decisions and placed them on the desk.'
    • 而退立阶下 - Means 'and then stood back down the steps.'
    • 问之 - Means 'asked them.'
    • 对曰 - Means 'they replied.'
    • 气恶不改逼公 - Means 'the air is bad and cannot be changed, so we must not disturb you.'
    • 但遥以案中事答 - Means 'just answer the matters on the desk remotely.'
    • 省读案如人间案者 - Means 'reviewed the cases as if they were cases in the human world.'
    • 于是即为判勾之 - Means 'he then immediately made judgments and handled the matters.'
    • 有食来 - Means 'food came.'
    • 诸判官同食 - Means 'all the judges ate together.'
    • 君既权判 - Means 'since you are temporarily serving as a judge.'
    • 不宜食此 - Means 'it is not appropriate for you to eat this.'

    Cultural and Historical Allusions

    • 河东 - Refers to Hedong, a region in Shanxi Province during the Tang Dynasty.
    • 贞观 - The era name of Emperor Taizong of Tang, indicating the early years of his reign.
    • 兴州长举县令 - The position of magistrate in Changju County, Xing Prefecture, which was part of the Tang administrative system.
    • 冥官 - Officials from the underworld, reflecting the belief in an afterlife and the existence of a bureaucratic system in the afterlife.
    • 大官府 - A grand official building in the underworld, symbolizing the importance and grandeur of the afterlife bureaucracy.
    • 王 - The king or ruler of the underworld, often associated with the concept of Yama or Diyu in Chinese mythology.

    Proper Nouns

    • 河东柳智感 - The name of the main character, Li Zhi'an, who lived in Hedong.
    • 贞观初 - The early years of the Tang Dynasty's reign.
    • 兴州长举县令 - The position of magistrate in Changju County, Xing Prefecture.
    • 冥官 - Officials from the underworld.
    • 大官府 - A grand official building in the underworld.
    • 王 - The king or ruler of the underworld.

    Li Zhi'an, from Hedong, was appointed as the magistrate of Changju County in Xing Prefecture during the early years of the Tang Dynasty. One night, he suddenly died. The next day, he woke up. He said that he was first summoned by officials from the underworld and brought to a grand official building. The messenger presented Li Zhi'an to the king, who said, "There is currently a vacancy for one official, so we have come to ask you to take up this position." Li Zhi'an declined, citing his elderly parents, and also stated that his fortune and virtue should not lead to his death yet. The king had it investigated, and it was indeed true. The king then said, "You have not yet reached the time of death. You can temporarily serve as the recorder." Li Zhi'an agreed and bowed to show gratitude. The officials led him to his office, where there were five judges, including Li Zhi'an, making six in total. The office was a large hall with three rooms for people to sit, each with beds and desks. The work was very busy and overwhelming. There was an empty seat on the western side, without a judge. The officials led Li Zhi'an to the empty seat. A group of officials brought documents, took Li Zhi'an's decisions, placed them on the desk, and then stood back down the steps. Li Zhi'an asked them, and they replied, "The air is bad and cannot be changed, so we must not disturb you. Just answer the matters on the desk remotely." Li Zhi'an reviewed the cases as if they were cases in the human world and then immediately made judgments. After a while, food came, and all the judges ate together. Li Zhi'an also joined them. The other judges said, "Since you are temporarily serving as a judge, it is not appropriate for you to eat this." [85]

    智感从之。竟不敢食。曰暮吏送智感归家。苏而方晚。自后家中曰暝。吏辄来迎。至彼而旦。故知幽显反昼夜矣。于是夜判冥事。昼临县职。遂以为常。岁余。智感在冥曹。因起如厕。于堂西。见一妇人。年三十许。姿容端正。衣服鲜明。立而掩涕。智感问。是何人。答云。妾兴州司仓参军之妇也。见摄来此。方别夫子。是以悲伤。智感以问吏。吏曰。官摄来。有所案问。具证其夫事耳。智感因咨妇人曰感。长举令也。夫人若被勘问。幸自分疏。无为牵引司仓。俱死无益。妇人曰。诚不欲引之。恐官相引耳。感曰。夫人幸勿相牵。可无逼迫之虑。妇人许之。既而智感还州。先问。司仓妇。有何疾。司仓曰。吾妇年少。 [86]

    Relevant Explanation

    Explanation

    [86]

    Idiomatic Expressions

    • 智感从之 - Zhi Gan followed it; implies compliance or adherence to a previous instruction or decision.
    • 竟不敢食 - He was finally too afraid to eat; '竟' emphasizes the culmination of fear.
    • 曰暮吏送智感归家 - At dusk, the official sent Zhi Gan back home; '曰暮' sets the time and '送' implies a formal act.
    • 苏而方晚 - He woke up and it was already late; '苏' (awake) and '方晚' (already late) describe the timing of his awakening.
    • 自后家中曰暝 - Afterward, in the house, it was called dusk; '曰暝' (called dusk) is an idiomatic way of referring to the time of day.
    • 吏辄来迎 - The official would always come to greet him; '辄' (always) indicates a habitual action.
    • 至彼而旦 - When he arrived, it was dawn; '至彼' (when he arrived) and '旦' (dawn) describe the transition from night to day.
    • 幽显反昼夜矣 - The realm of spirits and the mortal world are reversed in terms of day and night; '幽显' (realm of spirits and mortal world) and '反昼夜' (reversed in terms of day and night) are key idiomatic expressions.
    • 夜判冥事 - He judged matters of the underworld at night; '判' (judge) and '冥事' (matters of the underworld) are specific terms.
    • 昼临县职 - He attended to his county duties during the day; '临' (attend to) and '县职' (county duties) are specific terms.
    • 因起如厕 - He got up to go to the toilet; '如厕' (go to the toilet) is an idiomatic expression.
    • 见一妇人 - He saw a woman; '见' (see) is a straightforward term.
    • 年三十许 - About thirty years old; '许' (about) is used to approximate age.
    • 姿容端正 - She had a dignified appearance; '姿容' (appearance) and '端正' (dignified) are descriptive terms.
    • 衣服鲜明 - She was wearing bright and clean clothes; '鲜明' (bright and clean) is a descriptive term.
    • 立而掩涕 - She stood there and wiped her tears; '掩涕' (wiped her tears) is an idiomatic expression.
    • 是何人 - Who are you?; '是何人' (who are you) is a straightforward question.
    • 见摄来此 - I was brought here by force; '见摄' (brought by force) is an idiomatic expression.
    • 方别夫子 - I am about to part from my husband; '方别' (about to part) and '夫子' (husband) are specific terms.
    • 是以悲伤 - That is why I am sad; '是以' (that is why) is a causal conjunction.
    • 官摄来 - The official brought me here; '官摄' (official brought) is an idiomatic expression.
    • 有所案问 - To investigate something; '案问' (investigate) is a specific term.
    • 具证其夫事耳 - To provide evidence about my husband's affairs; '具证' (provide evidence) and '夫事' (husband's affairs) are specific terms.
    • 夫人若被勘问 - If you are questioned; '勘问' (questioned) is a specific term.
    • 幸自分疏 - Please explain yourself; '分疏' (explain) is a specific term.
    • 无为牵引司仓 - Do not involve my husband; '牵引' (involve) is a specific term.
    • 俱死无益 - Both of us dying would be pointless; '俱死' (both of us dying) and '无益' (pointless) are specific terms.
    • 诚不欲引之 - I truly do not want to involve him; '诚' (truly) and '引之' (involve him) are specific terms.
    • 恐官相引耳 - I am afraid the official will involve him; '恐' (afraid) and '相引' (involve) are specific terms.
    • 夫人幸勿相牵 - Please do not involve him; '幸' (please) and '勿相牵' (do not involve) are specific terms.
    • 可无逼迫之虑 - You can avoid the worry of being pressured; '逼迫' (pressured) and '虑' (worry) are specific terms.
    • 许之 - She agreed; '许' (agree) is a straightforward term.
    • 先问 - First asked; '先' (first) and '问' (ask) are straightforward terms.
    • 司仓妇 - The wife of the warehouse manager; '司仓' (warehouse manager) and '妇' (wife) are specific terms.
    • 有何疾 - What illness does she have?; '何疾' (what illness) is a straightforward question.
    • 吾妇年少 - My wife is young; '吾妇' (my wife) and '年少' (young) are straightforward terms.

    Cultural and Historical Allusions

    • 智感 - The name 'Zhi Gan' is a proper noun, likely referring to a historical or fictional figure.
    • 冥曹 - Underworld office or office in the underworld, referring to a bureaucratic position in the realm of spirits.
    • 冥事 - Matters of the underworld, referring to judicial or administrative tasks in the afterlife.
    • 县职 - County duties, referring to the responsibilities of a local official in the mortal world.
    • 幽显 - Realm of spirits and mortal world, a dualistic concept in Chinese cosmology.
    • 昼夜 - Day and night, a common reference to the cycle of time.
    • 司仓参军 - Warehouse manager and military officer, a specific title indicating a person's official position.
    • 勘问 - Questioned, a term used in legal and administrative contexts.
    • 分疏 - Explain, a term used in legal and administrative contexts.
    • 牵引 - Involve, a term used in legal and administrative contexts.

    Syntactic Ambiguities

    • 智感从之。竟不敢食。This sequence must be read as two separate actions: 'Zhi Gan followed it' and 'he was finally too afraid to eat.'
    • 曰暮吏送智感归家。苏而方晚。This sequence must be read as two separate events: 'At dusk, the official sent Zhi Gan back home' and 'He woke up and it was already late.'
    • 自后家中曰暝。吏辄来迎。至彼而旦。This sequence must be read as three separate events: 'Afterward, in the house, it was called dusk,' 'The official would always come to greet him,' and 'When he arrived, it was dawn.'
    • 幽显反昼夜矣。This clause must be read as a statement about the reversal of day and night in the realm of spirits and the mortal world.
    • 夜判冥事。昼临县职。This sequence must be read as two separate actions: 'He judged matters of the underworld at night' and 'He attended to his county duties during the day.'
    • 遂以为常。This clause must be read as a conclusion: 'He continued this way as a regular routine.'
    • 岁余。智感在冥曹。This sequence must be read as two separate events: 'After a year, Zhi Gan was in the underworld office.'
    • 因起如厕。于堂西。This sequence must be read as two separate actions: 'He got up to go to the toilet' and 'in the west part of the hall.'
    • 见一妇人。年三十许。姿容端正。衣服鲜明。立而掩涕。This sequence must be read as a description of a woman: 'He saw a woman, about thirty years old, with a dignified appearance, wearing bright and clean clothes, standing there and wiping her tears.'
    • 是何人。答云。妾兴州司仓参军之妇也。This sequence must be read as a question and answer: 'Who are you?' 'I am the wife of the warehouse manager and military officer of Xingzhou.'
    • 见摄来此。方别夫子。是以悲伤。This sequence must be read as an explanation: 'I was brought here by force. I am about to part from my husband. That is why I am sad.'
    • 官摄来。有所案问。具证其夫事耳。This sequence must be read as an explanation: 'The official brought me here. To investigate something. To provide evidence about my husband's affairs.'

    Zhi Gan followed the instructions. He was finally too afraid to eat. At dusk, the official sent Zhi Gan back home. He woke up and it was already late. Afterward, in the house, it was called dusk. The official would always come to greet him. When he arrived, it was dawn. Therefore, it was known that the realm of spirits and the mortal world were reversed in terms of day and night. Thus, he judged matters of the underworld at night and attended to his county duties during the day. He continued this way as a regular routine. After a year, Zhi Gan was in the underworld office. He got up to go to the toilet and found himself in the west part of the hall, where he saw a woman about thirty years old, with a dignified appearance, wearing bright and clean clothes, standing there and wiping her tears. Zhi Gan asked, "Who are you?" She replied, "I am the wife of the warehouse manager and military officer of Xingzhou. I was brought here by force. I am about to part from my husband. That is why I am sad." Zhi Gan asked the official, who said, "The official brought me here to investigate something. To provide evidence about my husband's affairs." Zhi Gan then advised the woman, "I am Zhi Gan, the magistrate of Changju. If you are questioned, please explain yourself. Do not involve your husband; both of us dying would be pointless." The woman said, "I truly do not want to involve him, but I am afraid the official will involve him." Zhi Gan said, "Please do not involve him. You can avoid the worry of being pressured." The woman agreed. Afterward, Zhi Gan returned to the state and first asked about the condition of the warehouse manager's wife. The warehouse manager said, "My wife is young." [86]

    无疾患也。感以所见告之。说其衣服形貌。且劝令修福。司仓走妇家。见妇在机中织无患。甚不信之。后十余曰。司仓妇暴病死。司仓始惧。而修福。又兴州官二人。珠满。当起京选。咨智感曰。君判冥道事。请问。吾选得何官。智感至冥曹。以其姓名问录事。曰名簿并封在石函中。检之。二曰方可得报。及辞。来报。乃见录事二人。今所得官名号。智感以报二人。二人至京参选。吏部注拟其官。皆与所报不同。州官闻之。以告智感。智感复问录事。录事覆捡簿书云。定如前所检。不错也。既而二选人过门下。门下审退之吏部重注。果是冥簿检报者。于是众人咸信服。智感每于冥簿。见其亲识名状。及时月曰。 [87]

    Relevant Explanation

    Explanation

    [87]

    Idiomatic Expressions

    • 无疾患也 - Literally 'no illness,' but in context, it likely means 'no harm' or 'no danger.'
    • 感以所见告之 - 'Told him what he had seen,' where '感' means 'to feel' or 'to perceive,' and '以所见告之' means 'to tell him what he had seen.'
    • 说其衣服形貌 - 'Described his clothes and appearance,' where '说' means 'to describe,' and '形貌' means 'appearance' or 'looks.'
    • 且劝令修福 - 'And advised him to cultivate merit,' where '劝' means 'to advise,' '令' means 'to make,' and '修福' means 'to cultivate merit' or 'to do good deeds.'
    • 司仓走妇家 - 'The warehouse keeper went to the woman's house,' where '司仓' means 'warehouse keeper,' and '走妇家' means 'went to the woman's house.'
    • 见妇在机中织无患 - 'Saw the woman weaving in her loom without harm,' where '机中' means 'in her loom,' and '织无患' means 'weaving without harm.'
    • 甚不信之 - 'He did not believe it at all,' where '甚' means 'very' or 'extremely,' and '不信之' means 'did not believe it.'
    • 后十余曰 - 'After more than ten days,' where '曰' is an archaic form of '天' (days).
    • 司仓妇暴病死 - 'The warehouse keeper's wife suddenly fell ill and died,' where '暴病' means 'sudden illness,' and '死' means 'died.'
    • 司仓始惧 - 'The warehouse keeper began to fear,' where '始' means 'began' or 'started.'
    • 又兴州官二人 - 'And two officials from the state of Xingzhou,' where '兴州' is a place name, and '官' means 'official.'
    • 珠满 - 'Had enough pearls,' where '珠' means 'pearls,' and '满' means 'enough' or 'sufficient.'
    • 当起京选 - 'Should be selected for the capital,' where '起' means 'to be selected,' and '京选' means 'selection for the capital.'
    • 咨智感曰 - 'Asked Zhi Gan,' where '咨' means 'to ask,' and '曰' means 'said.'
    • 君判冥道事 - 'You judge matters of the underworld,' where '判' means 'to judge,' and '冥道' means 'the underworld.'
    • 请问 - 'Please ask,' where '请问' is a polite way of asking.
    • 吾选得何官 - 'What office will I be selected for?' where '选' means 'to be selected,' and '得何官' means 'what office.'
    • 智感至冥曹 - 'Zhi Gan went to the underworld's office,' where '冥曹' means 'the office of the underworld.'
    • 以其姓名问录事 - 'Asked the clerk by their names,' where '录事' means 'clerk.'
    • 曰名簿并封在石函中 - 'Said that the name book was sealed in a stone box,' where '名簿' means 'name book,' and '石函' means 'stone box.'
    • 检之 - 'To check it,' where '检' means 'to check.'
    • 二曰方可得报 - 'It will take two days to get the report,' where '二曰' means 'two days,' and '方可得报' means 'to get the report.'
    • 及辞 - 'When he left,' where '及' means 'when,' and '辞' means 'to leave.'
    • 来报 - 'Came to report,' where '来报' means 'to come and report.'
    • 乃见录事二人 - 'Then saw two clerks,' where '乃' means 'then,' and '二人' means 'two people.'
    • 今所得官名号 - 'The names of the offices they were selected for,' where '所得' means 'selected,' and '官名号' means 'names of offices.'
    • 智感以报二人 - 'Zhi Gan reported to the two officials,' where '以报' means 'to report.'
    • 二人至京参选 - 'The two officials went to the capital to be selected,' where '参选' means 'to be selected.'
    • 吏部注拟其官 - 'The Ministry of Personnel assigned them to their offices,' where '注拟' means 'to assign.'
    • 皆与所报不同 - 'All were different from what was reported,' where '皆' means 'all,' and '所报' means 'what was reported.'
    • 州官闻之 - 'The state official heard about it,' where '闻之' means 'heard about it.'
    • 以告智感 - 'And told Zhi Gan,' where '以告' means 'to tell.'
    • 智感复问录事 - 'Zhi Gan asked the clerk again,' where '复问' means 'asked again.'
    • 录事覆捡簿书云 - 'The clerk checked the records and said,' where '覆捡' means 'to check again,' and '簿书' means 'records.'
    • 定如前所检 - 'It is definitely as previously checked,' where '定' means 'definitely,' and '前所检' means 'previously checked.'
    • 不错也 - 'There is no mistake,' where '不错' means 'no mistake.'
    • 既而二选人过门下 - 'Afterwards, the two selected officials passed through the Gate of the Lower,' where '门下' refers to a specific gate in the imperial palace.
    • 门下审退之吏部重注 - 'The Gate of the Lower reviewed and returned it to the Ministry of Personnel for reassignment,' where '审退' means 'reviewed and returned,' and '重注' means 'reassigned.'
    • 果是冥簿检报者 - 'It turned out to be exactly as reported by the underworld records,' where '果是' means 'turned out to be,' and '冥簿' means 'underworld records.'
    • 于是众人咸信服 - 'Thus, everyone believed and was convinced,' where '咸信服' means 'all believed and were convinced.'
    • 智感每于冥簿 - 'Zhi Gan often in the underworld records,' where '每' means 'often,' and '冥簿' means 'underworld records.'
    • 见其亲识名状 - 'Saw the names and conditions of his relatives and acquaintances,' where '亲识' means 'relatives and acquaintances,' and '名状' means 'names and conditions.'
    • 及时月曰 - 'And the time, month, and day,' where '时月曰' means 'time, month, and day.'

    Cultural and Historical Allusions

    • 无疾患也 - A common phrase in classical Chinese literature to express that something is harmless or benign.
    • 感以所见告之 - Reflects the cultural practice of sharing observations and experiences with others.
    • 说其衣服形貌 - Describes a person's appearance, which is a common practice in classical literature.
    • 且劝令修福 - Cultivating merit or doing good deeds is a central concept in Chinese religious and philosophical traditions.
    • 司仓 - Refers to a warehouse keeper, a common official position in ancient China.
    • 无患 - A term that can mean 'without harm' or 'safe,' often used in classical texts.
    • 暴病死 - Sudden illness and death, a common theme in classical literature.
    • 兴州 - A place name, likely referring to a specific region in ancient China.
    • 珠满 - Having enough pearls, symbolizing wealth or status.
    • 起京选 - Being selected for a position in the capital, a significant honor in ancient China.
    • 咨智感曰 - Asking Zhi Gan, a character in the text.
    • 君判冥道事 - Judging matters of the underworld, a role often associated with deities or spirits in Chinese mythology.
    • 冥曹 - The office of the underworld, a concept in Chinese folk religion.
    • 名簿 - A record of names, often used in administrative or religious contexts.
    • 石函 - A stone box, often used to store important documents or records.
    • 吏部 - The Ministry of Personnel, responsible for appointments and promotions in the imperial bureaucracy.
    • 门下 - A specific gate in the imperial palace, often used in administrative contexts.
    • 冥簿 - Underworld records, a concept in Chinese folk religion.

    Syntactic Ambiguities

    • 无疾患也 - This phrase can be read as 'no harm' or 'no danger,' depending on the context.
    • 感以所见告之 - The sentence structure is elliptical, but it clearly means 'told him what he had seen.'
    • 说其衣服形貌 - Describes a person's appearance, which is a common practice in classical literature.

    No harm came to him. He told him what he had seen. He described the clothes and appearance of the person he had encountered, and advised him to cultivate merit. The warehouse keeper went to the woman's house and saw her weaving in her loom without harm. He did not believe it at all. After more than ten days, the warehouse keeper's wife suddenly fell ill and died. The warehouse keeper began to fear and started to cultivate merit. There were also two officials from the state of Xingzhou who had enough pearls to be selected for the capital. They asked Zhi Gan, "You judge matters of the underworld. Please ask, what office will we be selected for?" Zhi Gan went to the underworld's office and asked the clerk by their names. The clerk said that the name book was sealed in a stone box and would take two days to check. When he left, the report came back, and he saw two clerks. The names of the offices they were selected for were reported to the two officials. The two officials went to the capital to be selected, and the Ministry of Personnel assigned them to their offices. All were different from what was reported. The state official heard about it and told Zhi Gan. Zhi Gan asked the clerk again, and the clerk checked the records and said, "It is definitely as previously checked. There is no mistake." Afterwards, the two selected officials passed through the Gate of the Lower, which reviewed and returned it to the Ministry of Personnel for reassignment. It turned out to be exactly as reported by the underworld records. Thus, everyone believed and was convinced. Zhi Gan often in the underworld records, saw the names and conditions of his relatives and acquaintances, and the time, month, and day. [87]

    报之。教令修福。多得免者。智感权判三年。其吏来告曰。已得隆州李司户。授正官。以代公。公不复判矣。智感明旦至州。告刺吏李德凤。遣人往隆州审焉其司户已死。问其曰。即吏来告之时也。从此遂绝。后州司遣智感领囚送京。至凤州界。囚皆逃。智感忧惧。捕逐数曰不能获。夜宿于精舍。忽见其故部冥吏来告曰。囚尽得矣。一人已死。三人在南山谷中。并已擒缚。愿公勿忧。言毕辞去。智感即请人兵。入南山西谷。果见四囚。囚知走不免。因来抗拒。智感格之。杀一囚。三囚受缚。果如所告。智感今尚存。任慈州司马光禄卿柳亨为临说之。亨为曹州刺史。见智感亲问云。然御史裴同节亦云见说。皆如此言焉。 [88]

    Relevant Explanation

    Explanation

    [88]

    Idiomatic Expressions

    • 報之 - Means 'to report it' or 'to inform,' likely referring to a formal report or communication.
    • 教令修福 - Means 'to instruct in the cultivation of merit' or 'to teach the practice of virtue.'
    • 多得免者 - Means 'many are saved' or 'many are spared,' referring to those who avoid punishment or misfortune.
    • 智感權判三年 - A phrase that is somewhat ambiguous. '智感' likely refers to a person named Zhi Gan, and '權判三年' could mean 'temporarily appointed for three years' or 'granted a three-year term.'
    • 已得隆州李司户 - Means 'has already obtained Li Si Hu of Longzhou,' where '李司户' is a proper name referring to a local official.
    • 授正官 - Means 'appointed to an official post' or 'given a formal position.'
    • 以代公 - Means 'to replace you' or 'to take your place.'
    • 公不复判矣 - Means 'you will no longer preside' or 'you will no longer judge.'
    • 智感明旦至州 - Means 'Zhi Gan arrived at the prefecture the next morning.'
    • 告刺史李德凤 - Means 'reported to the prefect Li Defeng.'
    • 遣人往隆州审焉 - Means 'sent someone to Longzhou to investigate.'
    • 其司户已死 - Means 'the local official had already died.'
    • 问其曰 - Means 'asked him' or 'inquired of him.'
    • 即吏来告之时也 - Means 'at the time when the official came to report.'
    • 从此遂绝 - Means 'from then on, it ceased' or 'it stopped thereafter.'
    • 后州司遣智感领囚送京 - Means 'later, the prefectural office sent Zhi Gan to escort the prisoners to the capital.'
    • 至凤州界 - Means 'reached the border of Fengzhou.'
    • 囚皆逃 - Means 'the prisoners all escaped.'
    • 智感忧惧 - Means 'Zhi Gan was worried and fearful.'
    • 捕逐数日不能获 - Means 'chased and pursued for several days but could not catch them.'
    • 夜宿于精舍 - Means 'stayed overnight at a monastery.'
    • 忽见其故部冥吏来告 - Means 'suddenly saw his former subordinate ghost official come to report.'
    • 囚尽得矣 - Means 'the prisoners are all captured.'
    • 一人已死 - Means 'one has already died.'
    • 三人在南山谷中 - Means 'three are in a valley in the south mountains.'
    • 并已擒缚 - Means 'and have been captured and bound.'
    • 愿公勿忧 - Means 'please do not worry, sir.'
    • 言毕辞去 - Means 'after speaking, he left.'
    • 智感即请人兵 - Means 'Zhi Gan immediately requested troops.'
    • 入南山西谷 - Means 'entered the western valley of the south mountains.'
    • 果见四囚 - Means 'indeed saw four prisoners.'
    • 囚知走不免 - Means 'the prisoners knew that running away was futile.'
    • 因来抗拒 - Means 'so they came to resist.'
    • 智感格之 - Means 'Zhi Gan overpowered them.'
    • 杀一囚 - Means 'killed one prisoner.'
    • 三囚受缚 - Means 'the three prisoners were bound.'
    • 果如所告 - Means 'just as he had been told.'
    • 智感今尚存 - Means 'Zhi Gan is still alive today.'
    • 任慈州司马 - Means 'serves as the Military Governor of Cizhou.'
    • 光禄卿柳亨为临说之 - Means 'the Imperial Steward Liu Hen told me about it.'
    • 亨为曹州刺史 - Means 'Heng served as the Prefect of Caozhou.'
    • 见智感亲问云 - Means 'asked Zhi Gan personally and said.'
    • 然御史裴同节亦云见说 - Means 'Yes, the Censor Wei Tongjie also said he had heard it.'
    • 皆如此言焉 - Means 'all said it was like this.'

    Cultural and Historical Allusions

    • 智感 - A proper name referring to a historical figure, likely a local official or magistrate.
    • 隆州 - A historical prefecture, possibly referring to Longzhou in present-day Sichuan.
    • 李司户 - A proper name referring to a local official, '司户' meaning 'local official' or 'magistrate.'
    • 刺史 - A title for a prefectural governor during the Tang Dynasty.
    • 李德凤 - A proper name, likely referring to a historical figure who served as a prefect.
    • 州司 - Refers to the prefectural office or administration.
    • 凤州 - A historical prefecture, possibly referring to Fengzhou in present-day Shaanxi.
    • 光禄卿 - A high-ranking official in the imperial court, responsible for the emperor's household affairs.
    • 柳亨 - A proper name, likely referring to a historical figure who served as an Imperial Steward.
    • 曹州 - A historical prefecture, possibly referring to Caozhou in present-day Shandong.
    • 御史 - A title for a censor or inspector in the imperial bureaucracy.
    • 裴同节 - A proper name, likely referring to a historical figure who served as a censor.

    Syntactic Ambiguities

    • 智感權判三年 - The phrase is somewhat ambiguous. '權判' could mean 'temporarily appointed' or 'granted a term,' but the exact meaning depends on the context.
    • 已得隆州李司户 - The phrase is somewhat elliptical. It could mean 'has already obtained the position of Li Si Hu of Longzhou' or 'has already received the appointment of Li Si Hu of Longzhou.'
    • 公不复判矣 - The phrase is somewhat ambiguous. It could mean 'you will no longer preside' or 'you will no longer judge,' but the exact meaning depends on the context.
    • 智感明旦至州 - The phrase is somewhat elliptical. It could mean 'Zhi Gan arrived at the prefecture the next morning' or 'Zhi Gan came to the prefecture the next morning.'
    • 告刺史李德凤 - The phrase is somewhat elliptical. It could mean 'reported to the prefect Li Defeng' or 'informed the prefect Li Defeng.'
    • 遣人往隆州审焉 - The phrase is somewhat elliptical. It could mean 'sent someone to Longzhou to investigate' or 'dispatched someone to Longzhou to examine.'
    • 其司户已死 - The phrase is somewhat elliptical. It could mean 'the local official had already died' or 'the magistrate of Longzhou had already passed away.'
    • 问其曰 - The phrase is somewhat elliptical. It could mean 'asked him' or 'inquired of him.'
    • 即吏来告之时也 - The phrase is somewhat elliptical. It could mean 'at the time when the official came to report' or 'when the official came to inform.'
    • 从此遂绝 - The phrase is somewhat elliptical. It could mean 'from then on, it ceased' or 'it stopped thereafter.'
    • 后州司遣智感领囚送京 - The phrase is somewhat elliptical. It could mean 'later, the prefectural office sent Zhi Gan to escort the prisoners to the capital' or 'the prefectural administration later dispatched Zhi Gan to take the prisoners to the capital.'
    • 至凤州界 - The phrase is somewhat elliptical. It could mean 'reached the border of Fengzhou' or 'arrived at the boundary of Fengzhou.'
    • 囚皆逃 - The phrase is somewhat elliptical. It could mean 'the prisoners all escaped' or 'all the prisoners fled.'
    • 智感忧惧 - The phrase is somewhat elliptical. It could mean 'Zhi Gan was worried and fearful' or 'Zhi Gan felt anxious and afraid.'
    • 捕逐数日不能获 - The phrase is somewhat elliptical. It could mean 'chased and pursued for several days but could not catch them' or 'pursued for several days but failed to apprehend them.'
    • 夜宿于精舍 - The phrase is somewhat elliptical. It could mean 'stayed overnight at a monastery' or 'spent the night at a temple.'
    • 忽见其故部冥吏来告 - The phrase is somewhat elliptical. It could mean 'suddenly saw his former subordinate ghost official come to report' or 'unexpectedly encountered his former subordinate spirit official who came to inform.'
    • 囚尽得矣 - The phrase is somewhat elliptical. It could mean 'the prisoners are all captured' or 'all the prisoners have been apprehended.'
    • 一人已死 - The phrase is somewhat elliptical. It could mean 'one has already died' or 'one of them has already passed away.'

    Zhi Gan reported the matter. He was instructed to cultivate merit, and many were saved as a result. Zhi Gan was granted a three-year term in office. His subordinate came to inform him that Li Si Hu of Longzhou had been appointed to a formal position to replace him. From then on, Zhi Gan would no longer preside. The next morning, Zhi Gan arrived at the prefecture and reported to the prefect Li Defeng. Li Defeng sent someone to Longzhou to investigate. Upon arrival, it was discovered that the local official had already died. When asked about the matter, it was revealed that this occurred at the time when the official came to report. From then on, the matter ceased. Later, the prefectural office sent Zhi Gan to escort the prisoners to the capital. They reached the border of Fengzhou, but the prisoners all escaped. Zhi Gan was worried and fearful, and after chasing and pursuing for several days, he could not catch them. That night, he stayed at a monastery. Suddenly, he saw his former subordinate ghost official come to report, saying, "The prisoners are all captured. One has already died, and three are in a valley in the south mountains. They have all been captured and bound. Please do not worry." After speaking, the ghost official left. Zhi Gan immediately requested troops and entered the western valley of the south mountains. Indeed, he saw four prisoners. The prisoners knew that running away was futile, so they came to resist. Zhi Gan overpowered them, killing one prisoner and binding the other three. Just as he had been told, everything unfolded as expected. Zhi Gan is still alive today, serving as the Military Governor of Cizhou. The Imperial Steward Liu Hen told me about it. Heng served as the Prefect of Caozhou and asked Zhi Gan personally, saying, "Yes, the Censor Wei Tongjie also said he had heard it. All said it was like this." [88]

    报之。教令修福。多得免者。智感权判三年。其吏来告曰。已得隆州李司户。授正官。以代公。公不复判矣。智感明旦至州。告刺吏李德凤。遣人往隆州审焉其司户已死。问其曰。即吏来告之时也。从此遂绝。后州司遣智感领囚送京。至凤州界。囚皆逃。智感忧惧。捕逐数曰不能获。夜宿于精舍。忽见其故部冥吏来告曰。囚尽得矣。一人已死。三人在南山谷中。并已擒缚。愿公勿忧。言毕辞去。智感即请人兵。入南山西谷。果见四囚。囚知走不免。因来抗拒。智感格之。杀一囚。三囚受缚。果如所告。智感今尚存。任慈州司马光禄卿柳亨为临说之。亨为曹州刺史。见智感亲问云。然御史裴同节亦云见说。皆如此言焉。

    Zhi Gan reported the matter. He was instructed to cultivate merit, and many were saved as a result. Zhi Gan was granted a three-year term in office. His subordinate came to inform him that Li Si Hu of Longzhou had been appointed to a formal position to replace him. From then on, Zhi Gan would no longer preside. The next morning, Zhi Gan arrived at the prefecture and reported to the prefect Li Defeng. Li Defeng sent someone to Longzhou to investigate. Upon arrival, it was discovered that the local official had already died. When asked about the matter, it was revealed that this occurred at the time when the official came to report. From then on, the matter ceased. Later, the prefectural office sent Zhi Gan to escort the prisoners to the capital. They reached the border of Fengzhou, but the prisoners all escaped. Zhi Gan was worried and fearful, and after chasing and pursuing for several days, he could not catch them. That night, he stayed at a monastery. Suddenly, he saw his former subordinate ghost official come to report, saying, "The prisoners are all captured. One has already died, and three are in a valley in the south mountains. They have all been captured and bound. Please do not worry." After speaking, the ghost official left. Zhi Gan immediately requested troops and entered the western valley of the south mountains. Indeed, he saw four prisoners. The prisoners knew that running away was futile, so they came to resist. Zhi Gan overpowered them, killing one prisoner and binding the other three. Just as he had been told, everything unfolded as expected. Zhi Gan is still alive today, serving as the Military Governor of Cizhou. The Imperial Steward Liu Hen told me about it. Heng served as the Prefect of Caozhou and asked Zhi Gan personally, saying, "Yes, the Censor Wei Tongjie also said he had heard it. All said it was like this."
